congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING PIÑA-M. CHAVEZ PROVINCIAL ROAD, PROVINCE OF GUIMARAS INTO A NATIONAL ROAD

name: HBN-01106
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING MAGAMAY-PANDARAONAN-DOLORES (GUISI) PROVINCIAL ROAD INTO A NATIONAL ROAD

name: HBN-01107
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING BRGY. GABAN-BRGY. EAST VALENCIA PROVINCIAL ROAD, PROVINCE OF GUIMARAS INTO A NATIONAL ROAD

name: HBN-01108
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE 'DON FERNANDO LOPEZ AVENUE' CONCORDIA-SAN ANTONIO PROVINCIAL ROAD, PROVINCE OF GUIMARAS INTO A NATIONAL ROAD

name: HBN-01109
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE SAN MIGUEL-MILLAN-LININGUAN-BUBOG-SEBASTE PROVINCIAL ROAD, PROVINCE OF GUIMARAS INTO A NATIONAL ROAD

name: HBN-00001
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE PROVISIONS OF THE 1992 INTERNATIONAL CONVENTION ON CIVIL LIABILITY FOR OIL POLLUTION DAMAGE AND THE 1992 INTERNATIONAL CONVENTION ON THE ESTABLISHMENT OF AN INTERNATIONAL FUND FOR COMPENSATION FOR OIL POLLUTION DAMAGE, PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATION THEREOF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Oil Pollution Compensation Act\". To protect the country's marine wealth, this bill proposes to adopt and implement the provisions of the 1992 International Convention on Civil Liability for Oil Pollution Damage and the 1992 International Convention on the Establishment of an International Fund for Compensation for Oil Pollution Damage. The two (2) conventions provide a system of compensation for pollution damage caused by a ship as a result of an incident, and for the cost of recovery measures to prevent or minimize such damage.

name: HBN-00002
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF DRUG REHABILITATION CENTER IN EVERY REGION OF THE COUNTRY AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The illegal drug menace has penetrated almost all sectors of society. This measure seeks to establish a drug rehabilitation center in every region in the country to provide medical and psychiatric treatment to drug dependents. By making medical facilities easily accessible, drug dependents are provided an opportunity to become useful and productive members of the community again.

name: HBN-00003
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE NATIONAL LAND ADMINISTRATION AUTHORITY, RATIONALIZING THE ORGANIZATION AND FUNCTIONS OF GOVERNMENT AGENCIES RELATED TO LAND ADMINISTRATION, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Land Administration Reform Act of 2004\". The land administration powers and functions in the country are currently dispersed among different government agencies. This bill seeks to reform the existing land administration and management system by consolidating and streamlining the land administration functions and powers within a single government agency to be known as the National Land Administration Reform Authority.

name: HBN-00004
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING JANUARY 22 OF EACH YEAR AS A SPECIAL NONWORKING HOLIDAY FOR THE CITY OF VIGAN, ILOCOS SUR

name: HBN-00005
congress_website_title: AN ACT DEFINING THE CRIME OF BETRAYAL OF DUTY IN THE CUSTODY OF PRISONERS PROVIDING FOR ADDITIONAL RULES ON THE IMPRISONMENT, DETENTION, CONFINEMENT AND CUSTODY OF PRISONERS AND DETAINEES, SPECIFYING ADDITIONAL WORK AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF GOVERNMENT PERSONNEL CHARGED WITH THE CUSTODY OF SUCH PRISONERS AND STATING THE PENALTIES THEREFORE, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: In many instances, a government official or employee commits the crime of infidelity in the custody of prisoner because the penalty for such act is light and the inducement of a bribe is too tempting. A guard who frees an inmate not only participates post litem to the commission of the crime but also turns traitor to the government. This bill seeks to define the crime of betrayal of duty in the custody of prisoners and impose stiffer penalties on officials who betray their duty as caretakers of prisoners.

name: HBN-00006
congress_website_title: AN ACT ABOLISHING THE DEATH PENALTY, REPEALING FOR THIS PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED 7659, AND AMENDING CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF LAW THAT IMPOSE DEATH AS A PENALTY FOR CRIMES COMMITTED
congress_website_abstract: The bill decrees the imposition of the maximum penalty of reclusion perpetua or life imprisonment in lieu of the death penalty.

name: HBN-00007
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY SISIM, MUNICIPALITY OF CABUGAO, PROVINCE OF ILOCOS SUR TO BE KNOWN AS THE SISIM NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00008
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY SAN SEBASTIAN, MUNICIPALITY OF SAN VICENTE, PROVINCE OF ILOCOS SUR TO BE KNOWN AS THE SAN SEBASTIAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00009
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE AND FREEPORT IN THE PROVINCE OF ILOCOS SUR, CREATING FOR THIS PURPOSE THE ILOCOS SUR SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE AND FREEPORT AUTHORITY, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00010
congress_website_title: AN ACT ABOLISHING THE DEATH PENALTY IN THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: This bills seeks to abolish the death penalty and to substitute the same with the penalty of reclusion perpetua or life imprisonment. The death penalty is the most cruel, inhuman and degrading punishment and it has no place in civilized society.

name: HBN-00011
congress_website_title: AN ACT DEFINING AND PENALIZING ENFORCED OR INVOLUNTARY DISAPPEARANCE AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Enforced or Involuntary Disappearance Act of 2004\". The State resorts to involuntary disappearance to stifle dissent and to silence political activists. While it may no longer be rampant, it remains unbated with not a single case having been resolved. This bill seeks, among others, to adopt the United Nations definition of enforced or involuntary disappearance, to consider involuntary disappearance as a continuing offense as long as the perpetrators continue to conceal the whereabouts of the persons who have disappeared and to disallow time limitation for the prosecution of persons responsible for the commission of involuntary disappearance.

name: HBN-00032
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE PANGANGAN HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX, LOCATED IN THE POBLACION OF THE MUNICIPALITY OF CALAPE, PROVINCE OF BOHOL FROM THE PANGANGAN HIGH SCHOOL, LOCATED IN PANGANGAN ISLAND, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE CALAPE NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00033
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE BACLAYON HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY LANDICAN, MUNICIPALITY OF BACLAYON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE BACLAYON NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00034
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY MANTACIDA, MUNICIPALITY OF CATIGBIAN, PROVINCE OF BOHOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE MANTACIDA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00035
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE CAWAYANAN HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY CAWAYANAN, MUNICIPALITY OF TUBIGON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE CAWAYANAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00036
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE BANTOLINAO HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY BANTOLINAO, MUNICIPALITY OF ANTEQUERA, PROVINCE OF BOHOL FROM THE SIKATUNA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE BANTOLINAO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00037
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY CUASI, MUNICIPALITY OF LOON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE LOON SOUTH NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00038
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE SANDINGAN HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY CALAYUGAN NORTE, MUNICIPALITY OF LOON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE SANDINGAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00039
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING AN EXTENSION CAMPUS OF THE CENTRAL VISAYAS STATE COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURE, FORESTRY AND TECHNOLOGY (CVSCAFT) IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF BALILIHAN, PROVINCE OF BOHOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00040
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED EIGHT THOUSAND FIVE HUNDRED SIXTY-NINE, ENTITLED 'AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF CATAGBACAN, LOON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE GOV. JACINTO C. BORJA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00041
title: HEALTH CARE DELIVERY MODERNIZATION ACT OF 2006
long_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE MODERNIZATION OF THE HEALTH CARE DELIVERY SYSTEM, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE MODERNIZATION OF THE HEALTH CARE DELIVERY SYSTEM, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Health Care Delivery Modernization Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to modernize the health care delivery system in the country to a level where it can fully and effectively perform its constitutional mandate to protect and promote the right to health of the citizenry and instill health consciousness among them. The proposed modernization program consists of several components that will ultimately phase out the antiquated system of health care delivery to complement state-of-the-art equipment and technology.

name: HBN-00042
congress_website_title: AN ACT REGULATING THE ESTABLISHMENT AND OPERATIONS OF HEALTH MAINTENANCE ORGANIZATIONS (HMOs), PROVIDING THEM INCENTIVES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Health Maintenance Organizations Act of 2004\". The measure proposes to grant health maintenance organizations (HMOs) reasonable incentives to encourage the participation of the private sector in the government's endeavor to make health care available to all. It also seeks to establish the operating guidelines for HMOs to rectify the gross inequality of bargaining power that currently characterizes the relationship of HMOs with their members and health care providers.

name: HBN-00043
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO SET ASIDE THE FORTY PERCENT (40%) SHARE OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS FROM THE VALUE ADDED TAX (VAT) COLLECTED BY THE BUREAU OF CUSTOMS FOR THE BUREAU OF INTERNAL REVENUE TO AUGMENT THE FUNDING EXCLUSIVELY FOR BASIC HEALTH CARE SERVICES AND FACILITIES DEVOLVED TO PROVINCES AND HIGHLY URBANIZED CITIES
congress_website_abstract: This bill mandates the national government agencies concerned to set aside the forty percent (40%) share of local government units in the total value added tax collected by the Bureau of Customs for the Bureau of Internal Revenue to augment the funding requirement for basic health care services and facilities devolved to provinces and highly urbanized cities. The share of each province or highly urbanized city, with devolved health care services and facilities, shall be distributed equitably on the basis of population within its territorial jurisdiction.

name: HBN-00044
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING CERTAIN LOCAL OFFICIALS TO FILL THE VACANCY IN THE OFFICES OF THE GOVERNOR OR MAYOR AND VICE-GOVERNOR OR VICE-MAYOR, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 44 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Section 44 of the Local Government Code delineates the line of succession in case of vacancy in the offices of the governor or mayor, vice-governor, vice-mayor and the sanggunian member. It is, however, silent as to whether the official, who is next in line of succession, can fill the very same vacant position he is prohibited by law to serve beyond three consecutive terms. The bill seeks to amend the aforementioned provision to provide a crystal clear mandate on succession by prohibiting certain local officials, who are disqualified by law to service during the prohibitive term, to fill permanent vacancies in elected positions covered by the prohibition.

name: HBN-00045
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A MANDATORY PERIOD TO RELOCATE SQUATTERS, GRANTING DISTURBANCE FEE AND TAX EXEMPTION TO OWNER OF ILLEGALLY OCCUPIED RESIDENTIAL LAND, AND PENALIZING VIOLATION THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Landowners Protection Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to provide a mandatory period in the relocation of squatters and to grant tax exemption to illegally occupied residential lands. It also seeks to compensate the landowners in case of failure of the local government unit (LGU) concerned to act on the request for assistance to evict illegal occupants within the prescribed period. The measure aims to provide equal protection to the squatters and to the landowners. It is intended to minimize, if not totally eliminate, squatting because LGUs will then be compelled to monitor vacant lands to prevent trespassers from constructing illegal structures therein.

name: HBN-00046
congress_website_title: AN ACT ABOLISHING THE DEATH PENALTY REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7659 ENTITLED 'AN ACT TO IMPOSE THE DEATH PENALTY ON CERTAIN HEINOUS CRIMES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE REVISED PENAL CODE, AS AMENDED, OTHER SPECIAL PENAL LAWS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES'
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to abolish the imposition of death penalty in the country. Scientific evidence show that death penalty has not been instrumental in deterring crime. In fact, in the Philippines, the number of death row inmates continue to grow despite the execution of several death convicts already. The bill further advocates the reduction of the death penalty already imposed to reclusion perpetua.

name: HBN-00047
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE WAIVER OF THE RETIREMENT AGE REQUIREMENT OF GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEE, WHO HAS RENDERED AT LEAST TWENTY (20)YEARS OF SERVICE, FURTHER AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1146, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED GOVERNMENT SERVICE INSURANCE SYSTEM ACT OF 1977
congress_website_abstract: To enable to government to get rid of employees who no longer have the drive to perform efficiently as they are merely waiting for the right age to retire and to maintain fresh legs and minds in the service, this bill proposes to allow government employees to retire after serving the government for a period of twenty (20) years. This will open windows of opportunities for retirees to venture into other fields using the amounts received from their retirement benefits while still in their productive years.

name: HBN-00048
title: MEDICAL PRACTITIONER PROTECTION ACT OF 2006
long_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A FAIR AND EQUITABLE TERMS FOR THE RELEASE OF MEDICAL PRACTITIONERS' PROFESSIONAL FEE WITHHELD BY HOSPITALS OR MEDICAL CLINICS FOR FAILURE OF THE PATIENT TO PAY HOSPITAL BILLS AND PENALIZING VIOLATIONS THEREFOR
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A FAIR AND EQUITABLE TERMS FOR THE RELEASE OF MEDICAL PRACTITIONERS' PROFESSIONAL FEE WITHHELD BY HOSPITALS OR MEDICAL CLINICS FOR FAILURE OF THE PATIENT TO PAY HOSPITAL BILLS AND PENALIZING VIOLATIONS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Medical Practitioners Protection Act of 2004\". It is the practice in many hospitals to withhold the payment of professional fees of medical practitioners pending full settlement of patient's hospital bills. This bill seeks to extend protection to medical practitioners by instituting a mechanism that will ensure fair and equitable terms for the release of professional fees of medical practitioners withheld by hospitals or medical clinics because of the failure of a patient to pay his hospital bills.

name: HBN-00049
congress_website_title: AN ACT BANNING PUBLIC OFFICERS AND EMPLOYEES FROM SMOKING WITHIN THE WORK PREMISES DURING OFFICE HOURS AND PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Public Officers and Employees Smoking Ban Act of 2004\". Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular (MC) NO. 30, Series of 1991, bans public servants from smoking. Under the MC, the first violation would lead to a reprimand; a second violation, suspension; and a third violation, dismissal. However, this MC has been ignored. To put more teeth to this policy, this bill seeks to ban public officers and employees from smoking within the work premises during office hours. It proposes to impose a penalty of prision correccional or temporary disqualification from holding public office or both to any person found guilty of violating the provisions of the act.

name: HBN-00050
congress_website_title: AN ACT CHANGING THE NAME OF ODIONGAN ELEMENTARY SCHOOL LOCATED AT SITIO COGON II, BARANGAY SAN JOSE, CITY OF TANJAY, PROVINCE OF NEGROS ORIENTAL TO EGMIDIO V. MANZO MEMORIAL ELEMENTARY SCHOOL

name: HBN-00051
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING THE CONCURRENCE OF THE LOCAL SCHOOL BOARD ON THE ASSIGNMENT OF DIVISION SUPERINTENDENTS, DISTRICT SUPERVISORS, SCHOOL PRINCIPALS AND OTHER SCHOOL OFFICIALS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 99, PARAGRAPH (d) OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The ambiguous wordings of Section 99 of the Local Government Code has led to various interpretations on the subject provision and which in turn, has resulted in some cases of discord between local officials and the appointee of the Department of Education. To make the provisions crystal clear, this bill seeks to requires the concurrence of the local school board on the assignment of division superintendents, district supervisors, school principals and other school officials to a particular local government unit.

name: HBN-00052
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING CERTAIN PERSON WHO MAY REGISTER AS A VOTER TO PRESENT ANY PROOF OF LEGAL RESIDENCE, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8189, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE VOTERS REGISTRATION ACT OF 1996, AND PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: There are numerous cases where the number of election registrants in a particular residential unit far exceeds the ideal or reasonable number of residents for each of them to live comfortably in such dwelling. This bill seeks to require a person, residing in the property of another, to present some proof of legal residence upon registration as a voter. It also requires the Punong Barangay to attest to the certification issued by the landlord and to maintain a record of the actual number of residentes in a particular property or dwelling unit.

name: HBN-00053
congress_website_title: AN ACT EMPOWERING THE LOCAL SCHOOL BOARDS TO SCREEN AND RECOMMEND THE APPLICANTS TO TEACHING POSITIONS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to strengthen the ranking system in the appointment of school teachers by empowering the Local School Boards to screen and recommend applicants to any teaching position in their area of jurisdiction. The bill further requires the applicants to possess the necessary qualifications and standards set by the Department of Education.

name: HBN-00054
title: HYDROTHERMAL, GEOTHERMAL PROCEEDS (LGUS SHARE)
long_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS TO APPLY THE SHARE OF THE PROCEEDS DERIVED FROM THE DEVELOPMENT AND UTILIZATION OF HYDROTHERMAL, GEOTHERMAL AND OTHER SOURCES OF ENERGY FOR ELECTRIFICATION OF OTHER LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS WITHIN THE PROVINCE WHERE THE SOURCE OF ENERGY IS LOCATED, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 294 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS TO APPLY THE SHARE OF THE PROCEEDS DERIVED FROM THE DEVELOPMENT AND UTILIZATION OF HYDROTHERMAL, GEOTHERMAL AND OTHER SOURCES OF ENERGY FOR ELECTRIFICATION OF OTHER AREAS WITHIN THE PROVINCE WHERE THE SOURCE OF ENERGY IS LOCATED, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 294 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: More than two (2) decades after the electrification program was launced to implement the total electrification of the entire country, several areas in the countryside still do not have any supply of electricity. The failure of the program to provide electricity to the whole countryside can be traced to lack of funds. This bill seeks to authorize local government unit to apply the share of the proceeds derived from the development and utilization of hydrothermal, geothermal and other sources of energy for the electrification of other areas within the province where the source of energy is located.

name: HBN-00055
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A MAGNA CARTA FOR THE POOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Magna Carta for the Poor\". It is the policy of the State to uplift the standard of living and quality of life of the poor and provide them with sustained opportunities for growth and development. This bill clearly defines the basic irreducible rights of the poor, to wit: a. the right to employment; b. the right to food; c. the right to free quality education; d. the right to shelter; and e. the right to free health services and medicine.

name: HBN-00056
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A LIGHT RAIL TRANSPORT (LRT) OR MONORAIL OR ANY OTHER APPROPRIATE MODERN MASS TRANSPORT SYSTEM IN CEBU CITY AND METRO CEBU AND A RAILWAY SYSTEM FROM CEBU CITY TO THE NORTHERN AND SOUTHERN TIPS OF THE PROVINCE OF CEBU, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00057
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING A CEBU RAIL TRANSPORT AUTHORITY, VESTING THE SAME WITH AUTHORITY TO CONSTRUCT AND OPERATE THE LIGHT RAIL TRANSPORT PROJECT IN CEBU CITY AND METRO CEBU, AND RAILWAY SYSTEM IN THE PROVINCE OF CEBU AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00058
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE CEBU FREE PORT, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00059
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7621, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT CREATING THE CEBU PORT AUTHORITY DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, PROVIDING APPROPRIATION THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES'

name: HBN-00060
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 6 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6958, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT CREATING THE MACTAN-CEBU INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT AUTHORITY, TRANSFERRING EXISTING ASSETS OF THE MACTAN INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT AND THE LAHUG AIRPORT TO THE AUTHORITY, VESTING THE AUTHORITY WITH POWER TO ADMINISTER AND OPERATE THE MACTAN INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT AND THE LAHUG AIRPORT, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00061
title: RO-RO FERRY LANDING FACILITIES (CEBU)
long_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF RO-RO FERRY LANDING FACILITIES AT THE CEBU CITY PIER AND AT THE MUNICIPALITY OF CORDOVA, AND A COASTAL ROAD FROM THE MUNICIPALITY OF CORDOVA TO LAPU-LAPU CITY AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF RORO FERRY LANDING FACILITIES AT THE CEBU CITY PIER AND AT THE MUNICIPALITY OF CORDOVA, AND A COASTAL ROAD FROM THE MUNICIPALITY OF CORDOVA TO LAPU LAPU CITY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00062
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF A FLY-OVER AT THE INTERSECTION OF JUAN LUNA AVENUE (FORMERLY SAN JOSE DE LA MONTAÑA AVENUE) AND CARDINAL ROSALES AVENUE, AND F. CABAHUG STREET IN THE CITY OF CEBU, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00063
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF A FLY-OVER AT THE INTERSECTION OF JUAN LUNA AVENUE (FORMERLY SAN JOSE DE LA MONTANA AVENUE) AND M.J. CUENCO AVENUE IN THE CITY OF CEBU, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00064
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF A FLY-OVER AT THE INTERSECTION OF GORORDO AVENUE AND ARCHBISHOP REYES AVENUE AND D. JAKOSALEM EXTENSION IN THE CITY OF CEBU, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00065
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF A FLY-OVER AT THE INTERSECTION OF GORORDO AVENUE AND N. ESCARIO AVENUE IN THE CITY OF CEBU, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00066
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF AN UNDERPASS AT THE INTERSECTION OF D. JAKOSALEM STREET - D. JAKOSALEM EXTENSION AND GEN. MAXILOM AVENUE IN THE CITY OF CEBU, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00067
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF A CIRCUMFERENTIAL ROAD FROM CEBU CITY'S FIRST DISTRICT (NORTH) TO THE SECOND DISTRICT (SOUTH)

name: HBN-00068
title: PATIENTS DETENTION FOR NONPAYMENT OF HOSPITAL BILLS BAN
long_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING THE DETENTION OF PATIENTS IN HOSPITALS AND MEDICAL CLINICS ON GROUNDS OF NONPAYMENT OF HOSPITAL BILLS OR MEDICAL EXPENSES
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING THE DETENTION OF LIVE OR DEAD PATIENTS IN HOSPITALS AND MEDICAL CLINICS ON GROUNDS OF NONPAYMENT OF HOSPITAL BILLS OR MEDICAL EXPENSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to stop the undesirable practice of hospitals and medical clinics of holding the release of fully recovered and/or dead patients because of non-payment, either partially or fully, of hospital bills. This practice has resulted in prolonged and, hence, more expensive stay of financially distressed patients in the hospitals. The bill allows underprivileged patients to leave the hospital upon the execution of a duly secured promissory note and imposes a penal sanction for any violation of the proposed act.

name: HBN-00069
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND RESETTLEMENT, ABOLISHING THE EXISTING HOUSING AGENCIES, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Omnibus Housing and Urban Development Act of 2004\". The bill focuses on the housing needs of the poor and the homeless. It proposes the creation of the Department of Housing and Resettlement, which shall serve as the primary agency that will prepare, integrate, coordinate and supervise plans, programs, projects and activities of the government relative to urban planning, development and renewal.

name: HBN-00070
congress_website_title: AN ACT REGULATING THE PRACTICE OF REAL ESTATE SERVICE IN THE PHILIPPINES, CREATING FOR THE PURPOSE A PROFESSIONAL REGULATORY BOARD OF REAL ESTATE SERVICE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Real Estate Service Act of the Philippines\". The real estate industry plays a vital role in the economic development of the country. A real estate transaction has a multiplier effect on the country's economy. The bill elevates the real estate service practice. i.e., real estate brokerage, appraisal and consultancy, to the category of a profession, thereby placing realty service practitioners under the jurisdiction of the Professional Regulatory Commission. It also provides for the integration of all realty service practitioners under one national organization.

name: HBN-00071
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO REGULATE THE PRACTICE OF DENTISTRY, DENTAL HYGIENE AND DENTAL TECHNOLOGY IN THE PHILIPPINES, REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 4419, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT TO REGULATE THE PRACTICE OF DENTISTRY IN THE PHILIPPINES, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES', AND REPUBLIC ACT NO. 768, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT TO REGULATE THE PRACTICE OF DENTAL HYGIENISTS IN THE PHILIPPINES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES', APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"The Philippine Dental Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to upgrade the total practice of the dentistry profession to keep pace with developments in the field and in allied disciplines, particularly the current focus on public health and safety which are also areas of concerns of the dentistry profession. The current practice of the dentistry profession is regulated by a 1965 legislation which the dentistry community deems not to have fully addressed the varied aspects of the practice of the profession.

name: HBN-00012
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING THE OPEN HIGH SCHOOL SYSTEM IN THE PHILIPPINES, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Open High School System Act of 2000\". This measure seeks to make secondary education available to out-of-school youth and adults who have graduated from the elementary but are unable to go to high school because of geographic and socio-economic constraints. It proposes to use print, radio, television and computer-based communications, satellite broadcasting, teleconferencing and other multi-media learning and teaching technologies that allow students to study on their own without having to regularly attend classes in conventional classrooms.

name: HBN-00013
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING TAX BENEFITS TO PRIVATE INDIVIDUALS, CORPORATIONS, PARTNERSHIPS OR INSTRUMENTALITIES, DONATING TO A SCHOLARSHIP FOUNDATION, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE TAX REFORM ACT OF 1997
congress_website_abstract: As an incentive to donors to scholarship foundations, this bill seeks to exempt any individual, corporation, partnership or instrumentality donating to a scholarship foundation from paying donor's tax. It provides that the total amount of the donations shall be deducted from the personal or corporate tax imposed on the donor.

name: HBN-00014
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE MODERNIZATION OF THE SCHOOL HEALTH AND NUTRITION PROGRAM (SHNP) OF THE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION, CULTURE AND SPORTS, AUTHORIZING THE APPROPRIATION OF FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"School Health and Nutrition Modernization Act\". This bill seeks to modernize and upgrade the School Health and Nutrition Program by prioritizing the following concerns: a. the recruitment of school health and nutrition personnel; b. the upgrading of the salary of health and nutrition personnel to be at par with their counterparts in other Departments; c. the provision of equipment, medical and dental supplies and medicines; and d. health examination of all GRade I entrants and provision of supplementary feeding to Grade I pupils.

name: HBN-00015
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING POST-HARVEST FACILITIES TO RICE FARMERS
congress_website_abstract: \"Post-Harvest Facilities Support Act of 2004\". The bill provides for the government, in consultation with farmer cooperatives, to construct warehouses and rice mills in every rice-producing municipality and city, and to provide the equipment and machinery necessary to operate these warehouse and rice mills. It aims to raise farm incomes to a level high enough to allow farmers to satisfy their material needs and enhance their self-esteem.

name: HBN-00016
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING A REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH AND POPULATION MANAGEMENT COUNCIL FOR THE IMPLEMENTATION OF AN INTEGRATED POLICY ON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH RELATIVE TO SUSTAINABLE HUMAN DEVELOPMENT AND POPULATION MANAGEMENT, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: As a preventive measure against abortion, this bill provides for timely and accurate information and education on reproductive health as well as ready access to safe, adequate and affordable reproductive health care services. It guarantees freedom of choice of individuals and couples on the number and spacing of their children. It sets the ideal family size to two (2) children.

name: HBN-00017
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A MAGNA CARTA OF STUDENTS
congress_website_abstract: \"Magna Carta for Students\". The proposed Magna Carta for Students seeks to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of students in the secondary, post-secondary, graduate and post-graduate courses. Among others, it provides for student representation in the governing board and other policy-making bodies of the school, the creation of multi-sectoral School Fee Board, the right to organize and run an autonomous student council, and the right to publish and circulate an independent school paper. The Magna Carta also seeks to penalize any violation of the rights of students.

name: HBN-00018
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE CHARTER OF THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT ACADEMY (LGA), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6975, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT ACT OF 1990, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Local Government Academy (LGA) Act of 2004\". The Local Government Academy (LGA) is tasked to train and improve the managerial skills and technical capability of local government officials and personnel to properly equip them with the demands of their position. This measure aims to strengthen the LGA and transform it to a more autonomous corporate body with the end in view of making its program more responsive to the needs of its clients.

name: HBN-00019
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO UPGRADE THE POSITION OF SECRETARIES TO THE SANGGUNIAN, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 469(A), OF CHAPTER 3, BOOK III, TITLE 5, ARTICLE 1 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The functions, duties and responsibilities of the secretaries of the local sanggunians are vital to the program of local government units. This bill seeks to elevate the position of all secretaries to the sanggunians to an appropriate rank with a commensurate salary.

name: HBN-00020
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING THE LOCAL SANGGUNIANS, EXCEPT THE SANGGUNIANG BARANGAYS THE POWER TO CONDUCT INQUIRIES, IN AID OF LEGISLATION, TO ISSUE SUBPOENA AND TO PUNISH FOR CONTEMPT ANY PERSON WHO REFUSES TO OBEY SUCH PROCESS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 447, 458 AND 468 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to expressly grant the local sangguniang panlalawigans the power to issue subpoena and subpoena duces tecum. Although the power to issue subpoena and subpoena duces tecum is impliedly granted to the local sangguniang panlalawigans by virtue of the statutory grant of delegated legislative power, there is a need to epxressly grant the same because the subpoena power is judicial in character. Allowing local legislative bodies to exercise this power without express statutory basis will impinge on the principle of separation of powers.

name: HBN-00021
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONVERSION OF THE PANGLAO ISLAND CIRCUMFERENTIAL ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF PANGLAO AND DAUIS, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL ROAD, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00022
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE SANDINGAN ISLAND CIRCUMFERENTIAL PROVINCIAL ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF LOON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00023
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONVERSION OF THE BALILIHAN-HANOPOL-BATUAN PROVINCIAL ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF BALILIHAN, HANOPOL AND BATUAN, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL ROAD, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00024
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONVERSION OF THE SIKATUNA-ALBURQUERQUE PROVINCIAL ROAD IN THE MUNICPALITIES OF SIKATUNA AND ALBURQUERQUE, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL ROAD, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00025
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONVERSION OF THE CALAPE-PANGANGAN PROVINCIAL ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF CALAPE, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL ROAD, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00026
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONVERSION OF THE ANTEQUERA-SAN ISIDRO-LIBERTAD (TUBIGON) PROVINCIAL ROAD TRAVERSING THE MUNICIPALITIES OF ANTEQUERA, SAN ISIDRO AND TUBIGON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL ROAD, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00027
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONVERSION OF THE STO. NIÑO PROVINCIAL ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF BACLAYON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL ROAD, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00028
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONVERSION OF THE COGON NORTE-BADBAD ORIENTAL AND OCCIDENTAL-TUBODACU-NAGTUANG-TUBUAN-BONGCO AND CANSUBAYON PRIP ROADS IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF LOON, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO NATIONAL ROAD AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00029
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE CONGRESSMAN PABLO MALASARTE MEMORIAL HIGH SCHOOL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF BALILIHAN, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE CONGRESSMAN PABLO MALASARTE NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00030
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE FERMIN TAYABAS HIGH SCHOOL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF CALAPE, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00031
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE FATIMA HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY FATIMA, MUNICIPALITY OF CORTES, PROVINCE OF BOHOL INTO A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE FATIMA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00072
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A PRE-SCHOOL LEARNING CENTER IN EACH OF THE FORTY-SIX (46) BARANGAYS IN THE FIRST DISTRICT OF CEBU CITY AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00073
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING AND PROVIDING FOR FREE COLLEGE EDUCATION IN INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY AND OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Free Information and Communications Technology College Education Act of 2001\". The measure seeks to provide free college education in information and communications technology (ICT), a two-year college degree course, to all high school graduates of public schools. These beneficiaries would have the right to choose the public or state college or university within the city, province or region where he may enroll to enjoy the free college education benefit.

name: HBN-00074
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1869, EXPRESSLY EXCLUDING JAI-ALAI GAMES FROM THE FRANCHISED ACTIVITIES OF THE PHILIPPINE AMUSEMENT AND GAMING CORPORATION
congress_website_abstract: The bill expressly excludes jai-alai games from the franchised activities of the PAGCOR.

name: HBN-00075
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN SECTIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6948, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT STANDARDIZING AND UPGRADING THE BENEFITS FOR MILITARY VETERANS AND THEIR DEPENDENTS'
congress_website_abstract: It is disheartening to note that our war veterans receive a meager pension. With the continuing increase in the cost of living, most of them could hardly afford a decent and humane standard of living. This bill seeks to increase the present benefits of our Filipino World War veterans by increasing their monthly pension and at the same time provide them with medical assistance. It is hoped that the proposed benefits would help improve the quality of their lives.

name: HBN-00076
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING INCENTIVES TO ALL BARANGAY OFFICIALS, INCLUDING BARANGAY TANODS AND MEMBERS OF THE LUPONG TAGAPAMAYAPA, AND BARANGAY EMPLOYEES, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Barangay Incentives Act of 2004\". To give due recognition to the important job being performed by grassroots workers, this bill proposes to provide incentives to barangay officials, including barangay tanods and members of the lupong tagapamayapa and barangay empployees. Among the benefits being proposed in the bill are: a. exemption from income tax on salaries, wages, compensation, remuneration and other emoluments; b. exemption from capital gains and transfer taxes; c. GSIS benefit coverage; d. health benefits; and e. free legal representation.

name: HBN-00077
title: LIBEL CASES AGAINST COMMUNITY JOURNALISTS (VENUE OF CRIMINAL/CIVIL ACTION)
long_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE VENUE OF THE CRIMINAL AND CIVIL ACTION IN LIBEL CASES AGAINST COMMUNITY JOURNALISTS, PUBLICATIONS OR BROADCAST STATIONS
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE VENUE OF THE CRIMINAL AND CIVIL ACTION IN LIBEL CASES AGAINST COMMUNITY JOURNALISTS, PUBLICATIONS OR BROADCAST STATIONS
congress_website_abstract: Under the present rule on venue, the complainant, if he is a public officer, can file the complaint in Manila if his office is in Manila or in the office outside Manila if his office is located there. This rule on venue has placed community journalists and local broadcasters in a situation where they have to answer complaints filed, rightly or wrongly, in distant places, diliberately filed far away from their place of work. The bill seeks to provide a specific venue of action in libel cases against a community journalist, community publication or community broadcast station.

name: HBN-00078
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE SALARY GRADE OF PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS FROM SALARY GRADE 10 TO 20 AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Public school teachers are among the most underpaid workers in our society. Considering that teachers are the prime movers in the education system, the government should give priority to their interest and welfare. This measure seeks to upgrade the salary level of public school teachers in the elementary and secondary schools from Grade 10 to Grade 20.

name: HBN-00079
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXPANDING THE PROHIBITED ACTS OF DISCRIMINATION AGAINST WOMEN ON ACCOUNT OF SEX, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE ARTICLES 135 AND 137 OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 442, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LABOR CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: It is the policy of the State to \"ensure the fundamental equality before the law of women and men\". Consistent with this mandate, the bill seeks to prohibit absolutely any act of employer or person, acting on behalf of the employer, from discriminating, directly or indirectly, against female employees or workers - or giving preference to male workers - in all matters pertaining to employment or employment opportunities and employment benefits. The prohibited acts shall include announcements, publications or advertisements of any kind or any form that would deny women equal employment opportunities.

name: HBN-00080
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE METRO CEBU TRAFFIC AUTHORITY, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00081
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A CIVIL SERVICE CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Philippine Civil Service Code of 2004\". This bill seeks to update the existing laws, decrees, letters on instructions, executive orders and make them consistent with the needs of the present system of civil governance. It aims to address the deeply-rooted problems confronting the public service such as graft and corruption, bureaucratic red tape, violations of the rights of employees, among others.

name: HBN-00082
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A NATIONAL HEALTH CODE OF 2004
congress_website_abstract: \"National Health Code of the Philippines of 2004\". The measure seeks to unify the health care delivery system of the country by emphasizing the meaningful partnership of each health component - the government units, the private sector, the nongovernment units and the community, which should perform to make health care available, accessible, acceptable and affordable. The Code will serve as an important directional reference and policy basis for planners, public and private administrators, program implementers, researchers and the general public.

name: HBN-00083
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO ESTABLISH THE DIMANPUDSO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY DIMANPUDSO, MUNICIPALITY OF MARIA AURORA, PROVINCE OF AURORA, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00084
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING INCENTIVES TO PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS WHO ARE ASSIGNED OUTSIDE OF THEIR TOWNS AND PROVINCES APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Public School Teachers' Incentives' Act of 2004\". The Constitution provides for the State to \"assign the highest budgetary priority to education and ensure that teaching will attract and retain its rightful share of the best available talents through adequate remuneration and other means of job satisfaction and fulfillment\". Despite the above, the welfare of the teachers remain neglected. This measure proposes additional incentive to public school teachers who are assigned to towns other than their own town but within the same province. Those who are assigned to towns of other provinces shall be entitled to more incentives under the proposed bill.

name: HBN-00085
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A MAGNA CARTA FOR THE INFORMAL SECTOR, INSTITUTIONALIZING MECHANISM FOR IMPLEMENTATION THEREOF AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Magna Carta for the Informal Sector\". \"Informal sector\" refers to a specific sector in the economy where most of the participants are categorized as \"unregistered, undocumented, untaxed, uninsured, disorganized and doing irregular economic activities\". The informal sector comprises almost half of the country's total workforce. The proposed Code seeks to introduce an integrated, holistic and comprehensive policy instrument which shall serve as the springboard for the informal sector to join the mainstream of the Philippine economy.

name: HBN-00086
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PROMOTE THE PROTECTION AND CONSERVATION OF THE NATIONAL HERITAGE, THE CREATION OF A NATIONAL HERITAGE COMMISSION AND PROVIDING PENALTIES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Heritage Act of 2004\". The Philippines is blessed with rich resources and heritage. However, the government's efforts to forestall the deterioration of our heritage has proven inadequate. This bill seeks to protect, preserve, conserve and promote the nation's cultural heritage, its properties and its histories. It also proposes the creation of the National Heritage Commission, which shall be tasked to promulgate rules and regulations pertaining to the conservation of our heritage, among others.

name: HBN-00087
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING THE SHEIKH KARIMUL MAKHDUM MOSQUE, CONSIDERED AS THE FIRST AND OLDEST MUSLIM HOUSE OF PRAYER OR MOSQUE IN THE PHILIPPINES, CONSTRUCTED IN 1380 A.D. AT TUBIG, INDANGAN, SIMUNUL, PROVINCE OF TAWI-TAWI, AS A NATIONAL SHRINE, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00088
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING THE USE OF THE WORD 'MUSLIM' OR 'ISLAMIC' IN PRINT, RADIO, TELEVISION AND OTHER FORMS OF BROADCAST MEDIA TO REFER TO OR DESCRIBE ANY PERSON CONVICTED OF ANY CRIME, OR SUSPECTED OF COMMITTING ANY UNLAWFUL ACT, AND PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The use of the words \"Muslim\" and \"Islamic\" in association with suspected or criminal and unalawful acts are offensively biased against Muslims. This practice is highly prejudicial and extremely injurious to the Islamic religious culture and to Muslim individuals. This measure prohibits the use of the word \"Muslim\" or \"Islamic\" in print, television and other forms of broadcast media to refer to or to describe any person convicted of a crime or suspected of committing any unlawful act. The outlawing of this biased practice would definitely help lessen enmity between Muslims and non-Muslims.

name: HBN-00089
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING A SCHOOL MODERNIZATION AND INNOVATION PROGRAM FOR PUBLIC ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY SCHOOLS, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"School Modernization and Innovation Act of 2004\". This bill aims to institutionalize a comprehensive modernization program for public schools and to provide a mechanism for the private sector and for local government units to participate in the program. The bill also seeks to establish the School Modernization and Innovation Trust Fund which will augment regular appropriations under the General Appropriations Act.

name: HBN-00090
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE LEGITIMATION OF CHILDREN BORN TO PARENTS BELOW MARRYING AGE, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE FAMILY CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES, AS AMENDED
congress_website_abstract: Today, the incidents of children born out of wedlock to parents who are disqualified under the law to marry each other because of the issue of minority are still very common. To uphold the rights and dignity of these children, this bill provides for the legitimation of children born to parents below marrying age.

name: HBN-00091
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A PROGRAM FOR THE ACQUISITION OF SCHOOL SITES AND THE CONSTRUCTION, MAINTENANCE, REHABILITATION, AND REPAIR OF SCHOOL BUILDINGS IN THE PUBLIC ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY SCHOOLS NATIONWIDE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"School Building Program Act of 2004\". This bill seeks to create a comprehensive program which will address the problem of classroom shortage. It carries a provision for a build-operate, build-lease-transfer, build-transfer, rehabilitate-operate-transfer and other contractual arragements that will allow the use of both government and private funds to finance the construction of school buildings. It is hoped that with the passage of the bill, class sizes will be reduced to a manageable level and thus result in the improvement in the quality of education.

name: HBN-00092
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING REFORMS IN LAND ADMINISTRATION, CREATING FOR THE PURPOSE THE LAND ADMINISTRATION AUTHORITY AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Land Administration Reform Act of 2004\". In line with the government's program to institute reforms in the present land administration system, this measure seeks to consolidate and streamline land administration powers and functions within a single government agency to be known as the Land Administration Authority. Under the present system, land administration is dispersed across the Department of Justice; Land Registration Authority and Register of Deeds; the Department of Environment and Natural Resources; Lands Management Bureau; Land Management Services in the regional, provincial and community offices; National Mapping and Resource Information and the CARP Secretariat and its field offices.

name: HBN-00093
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE AURORA SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE AND FREE PORT IN THE PROVINCE OF AURORA, CREATING FOR THE PURPOSE THE AURORA SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE AND FREE PORT AUTHORITY, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00094
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO ESTABLISH A PROVIDENT PERSONAL SAVINGS PLAN KNOWN AS THE 'PERSONAL EQUITY AND RETIREMENT ACCOUNT (PERA)'
congress_website_abstract: \"Personal Equity and Retirement Account (PERA)\". This bill seeks to institutionalize the Personal Equity and Retirement Account (PERA) for all private and private employees in order to promote among them positive attitudes toward saving for their retirement years. It is hoped that the institutionalization of the PERA would give our workforce a comfortable and financially secure retirement.

name: HBN-00095
congress_website_title: AN ACT ENHANCING THE ADMINISTRATION OF JUVENILE JUSTICE, CREATING THE OFFICE OF JUVENILE JUSTICE AND DELINQUENCY PREVENTION UNDER THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Youth offenders should be treated differently from the way adult offenders are handled. This measure intends to provide a just and more humane system of treating juvenile offenders in society through a comprehensive and integrated approach toward rehabilitating the delinquency. In consonance with the provisions of the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child and the UN Standard Minimum Rules for the Administration of Juvenile Systems, the bill emphasizes on the need of rehabilitating and reintegrating the juvenile offender into the mainstream of society.

name: HBN-00096
title: BORLONGAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL (AURORA)
long_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX I IN BARANGAY BORLONGAN, MUNICIPALITY OF DIPACULAO, PROVINCE OF AURORA FROM THE AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE BORLONGAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX I IN BARANGAY BORLONGAN, MUNICIPALITY OF DIPACULAO, PROVINCE OF AURORA FROM AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE BORLONGAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00097
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX II IN BARANGAY BAYANIHAN, MUNICIPALITY OF MARIA AURORA, PROVINCE OF AURORA FROM AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE BAYANIHAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00098
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE PUANGI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, ALSO KNOWN AS AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL - ANNEX III OF BARANGAY PUANGI, MUNICIPALITY OF DIPACULAO, PROVINCE OF AURORA FROM AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY RESERVA, MUNICIPALITY OF BALER, AND CONVERTING IT INTO A SEPARATE AND INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE PUANGI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00099
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE EXEMPTION OF FIXED INCOME EARNERS FROM THE FILING OF INCOME TAX RETURNS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 51(A)(2)(b) OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8424, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE TAX REFORM ACT OF 1997
congress_website_abstract: Since employers already withhold the income taxes of fixed income earners, the bill seeks to exempt fixed income earners from filing income tax returns. The exemption of fixed income earners would help the Bureau of Internal Revenue meet its objective of simplifying and streamlining the country's entire tax collection system.

name: HBN-00100
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE YEARLY ALLOCATION FOR SCIENTIFIC AND TECHNOLOGICAL ACTIVITIES (STA)
congress_website_abstract: \"Science and Technology Funding Act of 2004\". This bill seeks to allocate an amount equivalent to the prescribed UNESCO norm of at least one percent (1%) of the gross national product as the annual budget for scientific and technological activities (STA). It also proposes the allocation for the STA to gradually increase until it reaches the prescribed norm on the 5th year which shall include both government and private sector activities.

name: HBN-00101
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING AN ADVANCED STUDIES DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM FOR EXCEPTIONAL EMPLOYEES FROM THE GOVERNMENT AND THE PRIVATE SECTOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Pensionado Act of 2004\". It is imperative for the government to devise a program for overseas education and training for the brightest and most promising Filipinos who are already employed or self-employed in a branch of science or technology along identified priority areas. The proposed bill endeavors to provide the country with a continuing supply of highly trained leaders for industry, research and the academe through the coordination of these talents under a single governing body where all scholarships, under foreign and local sponsorships, are pooled together.

name: HBN-00102
congress_website_title: AN ACT RATIONALIZING THE FORFEITURE AND DISPOSITION OF PROPERTY SEIZED BY THE BUREAU OF CUSTOMS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE CERTAIN SECTIONS OF THE TARIFF AND CUSTOMS CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES, AS AMENDED
congress_website_abstract: The government's efforts to curb smuggling and other frauds have been stymied by the indiscriminate issuance of temporary retraining orders and injuctive reliefs by judicial authorities. This measure seeks to rationalize the forfeiture and disposition procedure of goods seized by the Bureau of Customs. Under the proposed bill, the Secretary of Finance, upon the recommendation of the Commissioner of Customs, shall be empowered to summarily seize smuggled goods and to sell the same.

name: HBN-00103
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING GOVERNMENT HOSPITALS TO UTILIZE ALL ITS INCOME FOR THEIR MAINTENANCE AND OTHER OPERATING EXPENSES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The practice of disallowing government hospitals from using their income for maintenance and other operating expenses has limited their capacity to expand and to deliver quality and efficient basic health services to the people. This bill seeks to authorize government hospitals to utilize their income from pharmacy, fees from in-house services and facilities, including but not limited to laboratory, radiology, operating room and nursery facilities; income derived form nonpatient related services such as parking fees, interview fees and tour fees, for maintenance and other operating expenditures including capital outlay.

name: HBN-00104
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING ADDITIONAL INSURANCE BENEFITS TO BARANGAY CAPTAINS, PROVIDING FUNDS FOR THE PAYMENT OF PREMIUMS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Expanded Barangay Captains' Insurance Act of 2004\". The barangay, as the basic political unit, plays a significant role in the implementation of government policies, plans and programs. This measure attempts to provide supplemental term insurance coverage to all barangay captains for death, injuries and sickness that may be sustained in the conduct of their official duties and functions. These supplemental benefits are in addition to the insurance coverage being extended to barangay captains by the Government Service Insurance System.

name: HBN-00105
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING PRE-SCHOOL EDUCATION, INCREASING THE MONTHLY COMPENSATION OF DAY CARE WORKERS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Pre-School Development Act of 2004\". It is the declared policy of the State to provide for a universal early childhood or pre-school education that promotes effective social, intellectual and skills stimulation and value-formation for children from three to five years old with the end in view of preparing them for formal elementary schooling. To implement this policy, this measure aims to institutionalize pre-school education as part of compulsory basic education. The barangay centers shall constitute the public sector pre-school institutions.

name: HBN-00106
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE AUTOMATIC RETENTION BY THE BARANGAY OF ITS SHARE OF FIFTY PERCENT (50%) IN THE COMMUNITY TAX COLLECTED BY SAID BARANGAY, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 164 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Section 164, par (c), subpar (2) of the Local Government Code of 1991 entitles barangay units to share up to fifty percent (50%) of their community tax collection. The section also provides that the share of the national government from the community tax collection shall be remitted by the city or municipality concerned within ten (10) days after the end of each quarter. It is silent as when the barangay shall receive its share of the community taxes. To correct the situation, the bill seeks to amend the provision to allow barangays to automatically retain their fifty percent (50%) share in the community tax collections and to remit the share of the city or municipality within ten (10) days after the end of each month.

name: HBN-00107
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE TAX ON IDLE LANDS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE, SECTION 236 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The country has a total land area of 30 million hectares, 47% of which are classified as alienable and disposable. The bill seeks to increase the ceiling tax on idle lands from five percent (5%) to ten percent (10%) to give local government units more flexibility in the imposition of the tax. The current maximum of five percent (5%) has not proven to be deterrent against the non-utilization of agricultural lands for productive purposes. The idle land tax is in addition to the basic real property tax.

name: HBN-00108
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTIONS 285 AND 286 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991, PROVIDING FOR AN EQUITABLE DISTRIBUTION OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE ALLOTMENT (IRA) TO LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS
congress_website_abstract: The primary objective for distributing internal revene allotment (IRA) to local government units (LGUs) is to lessen the fiscal disparities among urban and rural communities. Unfortunately, the IRA distribution formula under the Local Government Code does not actually address the equity objective. In effect, the well-developed LGUs get bigger IRA shares than those which need them the most. To correct this inequity, the bill proposes to introduce poverty-sensitive factors in the IRA formula in lieu of population, land area and equal sharing. The poverty-sensitive factors are population density, poverty incidence and income class of the LGU. It also allows LGUs to automatically retain their IRA shares.

name: HBN-00109
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE RE-ALLOCATION OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE ALLOTMENT OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 285 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Under the bill, the allocation of the IRA share shall be made after deducting the cost of devolved responsibilities, including the city-funded hospitals and other legislated benefits such as the Magna Carta for Health Workers and also the salaries of devolved personnel. The proposed reallocation will not only correct the disparity that LGUs suffer from the added cost of maintaining devolved responsibilities but it will erase the apparent mismatch between the allocation of the IRA shares and the distribution of the financial burden as a direct consequence of devolution.

name: HBN-00110
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTING A PROGRAM FOR THE CONTINUING EDUCATION OF COLLEGE TEACHERS, AMENDING FOR SUCH PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7784, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT TO STRENGTHENING TEACHER EDUCATION IN THE PHILIPPINES BY ESTABLISHING CENTERS OF EXCELLENCE, CREATING A TEACHER EDUCATION COUNCIL FOR THE PURPOSE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The proposed bill seeks to institute program for college teachers in both private and public universities and colleges for the purpose of improving the quality of tertiary education in the country by means of upgrading the capability and competence of teachers. The proposed training program shall focus on proper teaching methods and on other areas of study where continuing education is necessary.

name: HBN-00111
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING ADDITIONAL INSURANCE BENEFITS TO ALL PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS, PROVIDING FUNDS FOR THE PAYMENT OF ITS PREMIUMS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Public School Teachers' Insurance Act of 2004\". Teachers are the single most important factor in education. However, because of their not-so-attractive remuneration, teaching is perceived as a poorly esteemed profession that can not attract the best minds. This bill seeks to improve the plight of teachers by improving the benefits afforded to them to encourage the best minds to join the teaching profession. Specifically, this provides additional insurance benefits to teachers, over and above the benefits given by the Government Service Insurance System.

name: HBN-00112
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING ALL BRANCHES, SUBDIVISIONS, INSTRUMENTALITIES, AND AGENCIES OF THE GOVERNMENT, INCLUDING GOVERNMENT-OWNED OR -CONTROLLED CORPORATIONS OR THEIR SUBSIDIARIES TO DIVULGE THE IDENTITY OF HIRED FOREIGN CONSULTANTS, THE NATURE OF THEIR WORK AND THEIR COMPENSATION, PERKS AND OTHER PRIVILEGES, AND PROVIDING PENALTIES IN CASE OF VIOLATION THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to require full disclosure of the identity, nature or work and compensation of foreign consultants hired or retained by the government, its agencies and instrumentalities including government-owned or controlled corporations. The three main objectives of the bills are: a. to inform the public of the amount being used to pay consultants hired by the government; b. to appraise the public of whether or not the services of these consultants are indispensable and cannot be rendered by our local experts; and c. to enlighten the public of the personalities providing expert advice and support to the government.

name: HBN-00113
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING BONDS ISSUED BY THE LAND BANK OF THE PHILIPPINES FOR LAND ACQUISITIONS FROM PAYMENT OF THE TWENTY PERCENT (20%) FINAL TAX ON INTEREST INCOME, AMENDING FOR THE SAID PURPOSE SECTION 24 (B)(1) OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8424, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE TAX REFORM ACT OF 1997
congress_website_abstract: In the implementation of the law, the contemplated protection of the just share for the landowners did not actually materialize. In most cases, only the productive portions of the lands were bought, leaving the landowners with unproductive and undesirable portions of the land. To redress a perceived social wrong, this bill proposes to exempt interest earnings on land bank notes paid to landowners from the payment of withholding tax.

name: HBN-00114
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN SECTIONS OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBERED SIX HUNDRED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE MARINE POLLUTION DECREE OF 1974
congress_website_abstract: Presidential Decree No. 600 declares as unlawful the throwing, discharge or deposit of any refuse matter of any kind or description. However, the penalty of a fine of five hundred pesos (P500.00) or imprisonment of not more than one (1) year is not at all parallel to the concomitant damage to our waters. This measure seeks to increase the penalty for unlawful dumping and holds the president, manager or director of a corporation or partnership liable for such acts. It sets the guidelines under which dumping may be allowed.

name: HBN-00115
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE AUTOMATIC RELEASE OF THE SHARE OF THE BARANGAY IN THE PROCEEDS OF NATIONAL TAXES AND TAXES ON THE UTILIZATION AND DEVELOPMENT OF THE NATIONAL WEALTH, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE SECTION 286, CHAPTER 1 AND SECTION 293, CHAPTER 2, TITLE 3, BOOK II, RESPECTIVELY, OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991 AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Under the present system, barangays have to literally beg for their rightful share of the national taxes. To discourage patronage politics and to help attain our goals of decentralization, this measure seeks to provide for the automatic release of the share of the barangays in the national taxes and in taxes imposed on the utilization and development of national wealth.

name: HBN-00116
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE PHILIPPINE COMPETITION COMMISSION, REGULATING AND PENALIZING TRADE PRACTICES THAT LESSEN COMPETITION AND OTHER ANTI-COMPETITIVE PRACTICES AND CONDUCT, UNLAWFUL MERGERS, ACQUISITIONS AND COMBINATIONS IN RESTRAINT OF TRADE, UNFAIR COMPETITION, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Philippine Competition Act\". Competition Law is the primary mechanism in establishing the rules to promote and achieve competitive markets. Its goal is to correct market failure, enhance community welfare, achieve higher economic growth and be competitive in both domestic and international markets. This bill provides for the rules of a competition law and fair trade practices which shall apply to all businesses in the country. It seeks the creation of the Philippine Competition Commission which shall administer the rules on competition.

name: HBN-00117
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE REGULATION OF INVESTMENT COMPANIES
congress_website_abstract: \"Investment Company Act of 2004\". The measure seeks to amend RA 2629 to provide for a more conducive regulatory environment for the development of investment companies including mutual funds. It also proposes the establishment of the Investment Company Investors Protection Fund which shall compensate investors for financial injury they may suffer due to reasons other than market decline in the investment portfolio of the company.

name: HBN-00118
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A PROVIDENT PERSONAL SAVINGS PLAN KNOWN AS THE PERSONAL EQUITY AND RETIREMENT ACCOUNT (PERA)
congress_website_abstract: \"Personal Equity and Retirement Account (PERA) Act of 2004\". Generally, Filipinos view retirement with trepidation as it means lower or even loss of income because majority of Filipinos do not have retirement benefits to look forward to. This bill seeks to remedy the situation by institutionalizing the Personal Equity and Retirement Account (PERA) for all public and private employees. Under the proposed measure, people will be encouraged to save for their retirement years.

name: HBN-00119
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE PRE-NEED PLAN CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: Concerns have been raised over the pre-need industry's financial status and the protection it affords to investors and pre-need planholders. This bill seeks to establish the legislative framework that will govern the pre-need industry. It sets the industry's regulatory standards with the Securities and Exchange Commission as its regulatory agency. The salient features of the bill include the stipulation of a minimum paid-up capital of PhP 50 million and a margin of solvency of at least PhP 10 million in incorporating a pre-need firm. It also seeks the creation of Liquidity Reserve Fund to protect planholders.

name: HBN-00120
congress_website_title: AN ACT GOVERNING THE ESTABLISHMENT, OPERATION AND REGULATION OF LENDING COMPANIES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Lending Company Regulation Act of 2004\". In view of the significant impact of lending companies (LCs) in the financial sector, there is an urgent need for a law that would regulate the establishment and activities of LCs to prevent and mitigate practices of LCs that are prejudicial to public interest.

name: HBN-00121
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR PROTECTION TO CONSUMERS IN THEIR CREDIT CARD TRANSACTIONS
congress_website_abstract: \"Credit Card Consumer Protection Act of 2004\". In view of continuing complaints raised against credit card companies for double charging, high interest, penalties, finance and other charges, there is a need to review the issuance of credit cards to protect the interests of consumers. This bill seeks to protect consumders from deceptive price increases for goods and services by prohibiting unfair credit card surcharges. It also aims to encourage the availability of discounts by retailers who wish to offer lower prices for goods and services purchased through the use of credit cards.

name: HBN-00122
congress_website_title: AN ACT HARMONIZING AND RATIONALIZING THE ADMINISTRATION AND GRANT OF FISCAL AND NON-FISCAL INCENTIVES, REPEALING FOR THIS PURPOSE EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 226, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE OMNIBUS INVESTMENTS CODE OF 1987
congress_website_abstract: \"The Investments and Incentives Code of the Philippines\". This bill proposes to extend the maximum period for income tax holiday offered to investors from eight (8) to twelve (12) years subject to certain conditions. The proposed incentive merely tries to align our country's income tax holiday incentive with those granted by our neighboring countries. By matching this incentive with those offered in other countries, we stand an even chance of attracting investments and partners crucial to our development.

name: HBN-00123
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7653, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE NEW CENTRAL BANK ACT
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to: a. remove the five-year limit on the Bangko Sentral's tax exemption; b. authorize the Monetary Board (MB) to provide a compensation structure based on job evaluation studies and wage surveys, but not lower than those of other government financial institutions; and c. clarify that financial derivatives are subject to the regulation of the MB.

name: HBN-00124
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8799, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE SECURITIES REGULATION CODE
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to amend certain provisions of RA 8799, otherwise known as the Securities Regulation Code, in order to promote a more vibrant and healthy capital market. The pertinent provisions, among others, are a) Section 19.1 - ON MANDATORY TENDER OFFERS; b) Section 28.4; (b) - ON MINIMUM CAPITAL REQUIREMENTS; c) Section 30.1 - ON BROKER-DIRECTOR PROHIBITION; and d) Section 33.2 (c) - ON LIMITATION ON OWNERSHIP OF AN EXCHANGE.

name: HBN-00125
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO FURTHER STRENGTHEN CONSUMER PROTECTION BY AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7394, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE CONSUMER ACT OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to make the National Consumer Affairs Council (NCAC) a truly independent body. By placing the NCAC under the Office of the President, it shall have the power to prepare its annual budget, create other positions necessary to pursue its mandate and assume the authority to represent consumers in their claims and complaints involving violations of the Consumer Act.

name: HBN-00126
congress_website_title: AN ACT MODIFYING THE DEFINITION OF A CONDOMINIUM UNIT, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 4726, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE CONDOMINIUM LAW TO APPLY TO INDUSTRIAL ESTATES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The Condominium Law does not include the industrial estate as a form of a condominium where a single factory, building or plant may comprise a single unit. Said law implicity allows only multi-unit or vertical buildings. This bill seeks to amend the law to include industrial estates in the definition of \"condominium\". This will give investors more flexibility, especially in planning, to enable them to maximize the use of space according to the needs of their operation.

name: HBN-00127
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE RECOVERY OF FINANCIALLY DISTRESSED ENTERPRISES AND THE RESOLUTION OF THEIR INDEBTEDNESS
congress_website_abstract: \"Corporate Recovery Act\". The bill offers a comprehensive approach to the problems faced by enterprises when they, for whatever reason, can no longer pay their debts as they come due. It emphasizes on the survival of the enterprise while at the same time keeping paramount the rights of creditors with duly registered liens against the enterprise's assets. It also clarifies the rights of debtors and creditors to initiate formal debt relief proceedings.

name: HBN-00128
congress_website_title: AN ACT ENHANCING ECONOMIC COORDINATION, ADOPTING THE APPROPRIATE MECHANISM THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Economic Coordination Act of 2004\". This bill is anchored on the dictum that economic goal settling should properly involve Congress, the Governor and Monetary Board of the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) as well as the National Economic Development Authority headed by the President. It also gives flesh to the mandate of accountability and transparency of the BSP. It specifically sets a feedback loop mechanism on the state of the economy.

name: HBN-00129
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE SUBSIDY OVERSIGHT COUNCIL AND PROVIDING THE RULES FOR A RATIONAL MANAGEMENT OF THE GOVERNMENT SUBSIDY SYSTEM
congress_website_abstract: \"The Subsidy Oversight Council Act of 2004\". An important feature of the bill is the creation of the Subsidy Oversight Council that shall serve as a supervisory and recommendatory commission. The Council is tasked to monitor the grant of incentives, government loan guarantees, debt condonation and concessional loans. All government agencies which are empowered to grant subsidies, shall regularly report on such activities to the Council.

name: HBN-00130
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE RULES FOR A COMPETITIVELY NEUTRAL GOVERNMENT AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Despite its policy of privatization and deregulation, the State continues to compete with private enterprises for available economic resources. This bill seeks to establish a competitively neutral government. Competitive neutrality arrangements are intended to minimize resource allocation distortions arising out of public ownership of significant business activities.

name: HBN-00131
congress_website_title: AN ACT REGULATING THE DRINKING WATER BUSINESSES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Drinking Water Business Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to regulate the business of producing and selling mineral, pure or distilled water. It also protects and safeguards the health of the citizens by insuring that the water they procure and consume are safe to drink as promoted and advertised by drinking water suppliers, distributors or sellers.

name: HBN-00152
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE MINDANAO POLYTECHNIC STATE COLLEGE INTO A STATE UNIVERSITY TO BE KNOWN AS THE MINDANAO UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00153
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING EACH CITY AND MUNICIPALITY TO CREATE A RIVER DEVELOPMENT AUTHORITY FOR THE PRESERVATION, PROTECTION, AND DEVELOPMENT OF ALL RIVERS, RIVERSYSTEMS, AND NATURAL WATERWAYS WITHIN ITS RESPECTIVE JURISDICTION, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The Philippines had 421 principal rivers and 384 river systems. A great number of these rivers are gradually vanishing, many are dying while others are suffering from physical, biological or chemical damage and some have simply died and vanished. The bill seeks to mandate each city and municipality to create a River Development Authority with the funcitons of preserving, protecting and developing all the rivers, river systems and all natural waterways within their respective jurisdiction.
