name: HBN-00163
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1069, KNOWN AS THE 'PHILIPPINE EXTRADITION LAW', RATIONALIZING THE EXTRADITION PROCEEDINGS
congress_website_abstract: The bill stipulates that in the case of a resident Filipino citizen, no warrant of arrest shall be issued before a final determination that such citizen is extraditable. Articles, alleged to have intimate relation to or are the products of the crime for which the accused is sought to be extradited and there is reasonable ground to believe that this is true, may be seized upon order of the court when extradition is granted.

name: HBN-00164
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING AS NATIONAL POLICY THE FREEDOM OF THE FILIPINO FROM ONEROUS, UNREASONABLE, AND UNCONSCIONABLE DEBT OR OBLIGATION, REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 31 (b) OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1177 AND SIMILAR LAWS, DECREES, EXECUTIVE AND ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS, AND CIRCULARS
congress_website_abstract: The Filipinos have suffered for long from the burdens of onerous, unreasonable and unconscionable debts and obligation which have eaten up as much as thirty to forty percent of the annual budget as a result of the automatic appropriation provision under Section 31 (b) of PD 1117 and other similar laws, decrees, executive and administrative orders or circulars. This bill seeks to free the Filipino from the burdens of onerous and unreasonable debts by repealing the automatic appropriation provision under PD 1117.

name: HBN-00165
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE ALLOCATION FOR THE SPECIAL EDUCATION FUND, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Quality education has taken second place to quantity in our educational system. Elementary and secondary schools, particularly public schools, are operating below standards due inadequate facilities and lack of well-trained educators and classrooms. This bill seeks to double the Special Education Fund in order to give schools the necessary facilities needed by children and to enable teachers to undergo skills upgrading through seminars and training.

name: HBN-00145
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTING A CHILDREN'S WELFARE FUND FOR THE PROTECTION AND REHABILITATION OF ABANDONED, ABUSED AND SEXUALLY EXPLOITED CHILDREN, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED 7610, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE SPECIAL PROTECTION OF CHILDREN AGAINST CHILD ABUSE, EXPLOITATION AND DISCRIMINATION ACT
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend RA 7610 by incorporating a Children's Welfate Fund which shall be used exclusively to: a. provide protection and assistance to abandoned or abused or sexually exploited children; b. establish, operate and maintain facilities necessary to rehabilitate such children; c. provide basic education; and d.assist the State in curtailing all forms of child abuse.

name: HBN-00146
title: SPECIAL PROGRAM FOR EMPLOYMENT OF STUDENTS (SPES)
long_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING FURTHER AND BROADENING THE COVERAGE OF THE SPECIAL PROGRAM FOR EMPLOYMENT OF STUDENTS (SPES), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7323
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING FURTHER AND BROADENING THE COVERAGE OF THE SPECIAL PROGRAM FOR EMPLOYMENT OF STUDENTS (SPES), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7323
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to upgrade the provisions Ra 7323 (Poor But Deserving Students Employment Act) by: a. pegging the salary rate of trainees on the minimum wage for private employees and the applicable hiring rate for national and local government agencies; b. indexing the income qualifications for the Special Program for Employment of Students to the annual national poverty threshold line for a family of six to insulate it from legislative amendments every time there is a shift in the poverty line.
subjects: ['Special Prog. for Employment of Students', 'R.A.No. 7323 (Expansion Coverage)']

name: HBN-00147
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING CERTAIN BUILDINGS, INSTITUTIONS, ESTABLISHMENTS AND PUBLIC UTILITIES TO RESERVE SUFFICIENT AND SUITABLE PARKING OR SEATING SPACES FOR THE USE OF EXPECTANT MOTHERS
congress_website_abstract: Expectant mothers should be given the appropriate care and attention by the State by implementing laws which will help them through the 9 months pregnancy. This bill seeks to require owners of buildings, institutions, establishments and public utilities to reserve sufficient and suitable parking or seating spaces for the use of expectant mothers.

name: HBN-00148
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING HUMAN CLONING IN THE PHILIPPINES AND PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATION THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: \"Cloning Prohibition Act of 2001\". Scientific information indicate that cloning, at this point, is not safe to use on humans and that the technique would violate important ethical obligations. The measure seeks to prohibit human cloning in the Philippines, even on an experimental basis. It also provides penalties for violations thereof.

name: HBN-00149
congress_website_title: AN ACT DEFINING THE BEGINNING OF HUMAN LIFE, RECOGNIZING RIGHTS THEREUNDER, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Human Life Act of 2004\". As articulated and intended by the 1986 Constitutional Commission, conception begins with the combination, fusion or integration of the ovum and the sperm cell forming the human nucleus. It also emphasized that the definition of the beginning of human life as from conception shall be provided by an appropriate legislation. This bill seeks to define the beginning of human life and to recognize the rights of the unborn.

name: HBN-00150
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING FROM ANY AND ALL TAXES THE AMOUNTS AND SIMILAR BENEFITS THAT WILL BE GIVEN TO AND RECEIVED BY HUMAN RIGHTS VICTIMS AS COMPENSATION UNDER THE JUDGE MANUEL REAL DECISION, AND ALL THOSE SIMILARLY SITUATED
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to exempt from any and all kinds of taxes, local or national, any amount of money or similar benefit given to and received by human rights victims as compensation under the decision of Judge Manila Real and those similarly situated. Subjecting such compensation to any form of tax would constitute a travesty of justice for such is not income. At most, such compensation will only be rehabilitative or restorative but it will never approximate the depth and magnitude of the injury or suffering of human rights victims.

name: HBN-00151
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE INCLUSION OF THE MINORITY FLOOR LEADER OF THE SANGGUNIAN CONCERNED IN THE MEMBERSHIP OF THE LOCAL SCHOOL BOARDS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE, THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The measure seeks to amend Section 98 of the Local Government Code by providing for the inclusion of the Minority Floor Leader of the Sanggunian concerned in the Local School Board (LSB). The Minority in the Sanggunian are the fiscalizers in the disbursement of funds and promulgation of laws in the Sanggunian and their presence in the LSB will give added assurance that the administration of the Special Education Fund will be subjected to scrutiny.

name: HBN-00180
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO FURTHER PROMOTE ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND SUPPORT THE DEVELOPMENT OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6977, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE MAGNA CARTA FOR SMALL ENTERPRISES
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to give recognition to the role of micro enterprises which has been tradionally lumped as part of the small enterprise sector. It further seeks to broaden the membership of the Small and Medium Enterprise Development (SMED) Council to include members from the executive and legislative branches. Among other major amendments to RA 6977, it proposes to rename the SMED Council to the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprise Development (MSMED) Council.

name: HBN-00181
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING COMPUTER EDUCATION IN PUBLIC HIGH SCHOOLS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Computer education and information technology is the wave sweeping every corner of the globe. In order to raise our competitiveness with our ASEAN neighbors, we should equip our students with the necessary skills in the operation of computers. This bill seeks to require all public high schools to establish and maintain a computer facility that will provide and ensure students access to computers.

name: HBN-00182
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE COFFEE RESEARCH, DEVELOPMENT AND EXTENSION CENTER IN THE CAVITE STATE UNIVERSITY AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00183
congress_website_title: AN ACT PUNISHING THE PILFERAGE OR THEFT OF ANY MATERIAL, PRODUCT, MERCHANDISE, ARTICLE, EQUIPMENT OR BY-PRODUCT MANUFACTURED, PRODUCED, OWNED, OR BELONGING TO INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY ENTERPRISES LOCATED IN SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Pilferage of Information Technology Materials in Special Economic Zones Act\". Information technology (IT) and related industries have given direct employment to thousands of our countrymen. Unfortunately, theft and pilferage of this industry's products have been on the rise because of the relative ease in which these products can be taken out because of their miniature size and a high value. The measure seeks to declare as unlawful for any person to tkae, pilfer or steal, with intent to gain, any material, article, equipment or by-products manufactured, produced, owned or belonging to IT enterprises in special economic zones.

name: HBN-00184
congress_website_title: AN ACT IMPOSING A HEAVIER PENALTY AGAINST PUBLIC OFFICERS CHARGED WITH THE CUSTODY OF PRISONERS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE ARTICLES 223 AND 224 OF THE REVISED PENAL CODE
congress_website_abstract: The Revised Penal Code punishes offenses committed by public officers who are tasked with the custody of prisoners. However, the penalties provided therein are relatively light. The \"inadequacy\" of the penalty imposed plus the windfall that awaits the jail guard who \"facilitates\" an escape, has led to the \"escape\" of some high-profile criminals. The bill seeks to impose harsher penalties against public officers charged with the custody of prisoners.

name: HBN-00185
congress_website_title: AN ACT ABOLISHING THE SANGGUNIANG KABATAAN AND AMENDING RELEVANT PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991, AND IN LIEU THEREOF, PROVIDING FOR THE ELECTION OF A YOUTH SECTOR REPRESENTATIVE IN THE VARIOUS LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS
congress_website_abstract: In the ten (10) years since its formal establishment, the Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) has failed to become the mainstream youth organization as envisioned by the government. It remains unsuccessful in its role as the principal vehicle of the Filipino youth in fulfilling its sectoral goals. The bill provides for a new mechanism that will ensure the realization of true youth involvement.

name: HBN-00190
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE POLITICAL PARTY SYSTEM AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Political Party Act of 2004\". Reforming campaign finance is one key strategy for fighting corruption in the Philippines. The bill proposes public funding for political parties that demonstrate broad national support as a way of reducing their dependence on interest groups for election campaign contributions. It proposes two sources for public campaign funding: a) a check off from personal and corporate income taxes; and b) a direct allocation from the national budget. Public financing can also be viewed as a way of simplifying and strengthening our party system.

name: HBN-00191
congress_website_title: AN ACT ACCELERATING THE DEVELOPMENT OF A NATIONAL RAILWAY SYSTEM, CREATING FOR THE PURPOSE THE NATIONAL RAILWAY AUTHORITY, PRESCRIBING ITS POWERS, FUNCTIONS AND DUTIES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Railways Act\". In order to provide and maintain an adequate, efficient, inexpensive, convenient and reliable public transport system responsive to the needs of the citizenry, this bill provides for the creation the National Railway Authority which shall formulate and adopt a comprehensive development plan for a national railway system that meets ecological and environmental standards. It shall promote and encourage the active participation of the private sector, both local and international, in the development and implementation of a comprehensive development plan for a railway system.

name: HBN-00192
congress_website_title: AN ACT IMPROVING THE EFFICIENCY IN THE DELIVERY OF GOVERNMENT SERVICES TO THE PUBLIC BY REDUCING BUREAUCRATIC RED TAPE AND PREVENTING GRAFT AND CORRUPTION
congress_website_abstract: \"Red Tape Reduction Act of 2004\". Bureaucratic red tape does not only lengthen the procedure in securing vital government services but it also opens avenues for bribery and corruption. The bill provides a maximum of fifteen and thirty working days for concerned government agencies to act on simple and complex transactions, respectively. It limits the number of signatories to the officers and employees directly supervising the specific unit where a particular transaction is being acted upon. It also institutionalizes a Report Card Survey that shall assess the performance of the different agencies.

name: HBN-00193
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO REENGINEER THE GOVERNMENT BUREAUCRACY BY AUTHORIZING THE PRESIDENT TO IMPLEMENT THE RE-ENGINEERING PLAN, CREATING THE COMMISSION ON GOVERNMENT RE-ENGINEERING AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"The Re-Engineering Act of 2004\". The government bureucracy has become unwieldy and corruption-ridden. The bill proposes for a multi-year process of bureaucratic re-engineering to allow for thorough studies and consultation . Civil servants in agencies deemed non-vital shall be offered a attractive gratuity package. Employees of such agencies who wish to remain in government may be re-assigned to other surviving entities. On the other hand, agencies deemed important will have the option to undertake the gratuity package for not more than 20% of their employees.

name: HBN-00194
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTIONS 141, 142, 143, 144 AND 145 OF THE NATIONAL INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OF 1997
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes to increase the existing excise tax rate on tobacco and alcohol products or \"sin products\" and to index the same to inflation. It further proposes the removal of the codal classification of excisable products by brand. The passage of the bill is expected to generate at least PhP 4 billion in the first year of implementation.

name: HBN-00195
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING ADDITIONAL ALLOWABLE TAX DEDUCTIONS FOR INDIVIDUAL TAXPAYERS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 34 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8424, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE TAX REFORM ACT OF 1997
congress_website_abstract: The increasing cost of rentals on dwellings has forced countless families to scrimp on other basic necessities. Many have resorted to becoming informal settlers, turning blind eyes to the dangers posed to their children in such communities. This bill seeks to provide some measure of relief to our taxpayers who are married or heads of their families, by including their rentals as allowable deductions from their gross income. It is envisioned that the said amount shall be used to provide for other equally important needs.

name: HBN-00196
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE MARITIME AND OCEAN AFFAIRS CENTER AS AN ATTACHED AGENCY OF THE DEPARTMENT OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS, PROVIDING FOR ITS FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00197
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCY COUNCIL, PROVIDING POWERS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Emergency Health Powers Act\". It is imperative that a plan of action be established to counteract the emergence of contagions like SARS (Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome). The need is made even more urgent with the threat of a deliberate outbreak via bioterrosm. The bill seeks to establish a Public Health Emergency Council which shall draw up a battle plan against public health emergencies and to implement the same. It aims to give us a fighting chance against biological, chemical or nuclear health treats through preparation, management and disposal.

name: HBN-00198
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING LOCAL HOUSING BOARDS IN EVERY CITY AND MUNICIPALITY, PROVIDING FOR ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Local Housing Boards Act\". The problem of shelter is one that is shared by almost all municipalities and cities in the country. Because it is basic need, it is imperative that government exerts a concentrated effort to find solutions to the shelter problem. The bill seeks to institutionalize Local Housing Boards which will be created at the level of local government units (LGUs). The LGUs are in the best position to know the peculiarities of their locality and establish their priorities. Local housing boards shall be responsible for the formulation of a housing program in their respective areas of jurisdiction.

name: HBN-00199
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO ESTABLISH A SOCIALIZED AND LOW-COST HOUSING LOAN RESTRUCTURING PROGRAM, PROVIDING THE MECHANISMS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Socialized and Low-Cost Housing Loan Restructuring Act of 2003\". The Asian crisis of 1997 affected many Filipinos including those who availed of low-cost and socialized housing offered on a supply-dictated basis at the behest of the Ramos administration. Despite the enactment of RA 8501 to remedy the problem of unpaid mortgages and defaulting housing loan borrowers, only 19,000 out 180,000 defaulting accounts availed of the condonation program. The bill seeks to provide fresh hope to those who failed to avail of the provisions of RA 8501 but wish to keep their homes and pay their loans at rates affordable and in keeping with their current economic status.

name: HBN-00200
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING TRAVEL ALLOWANCES TO BARANGAY HEALTH WORKERS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7883, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'THE BARANGAY HEALTH WORKERS' BENEFITS AND INCENTIVES ACT OF 1995,' AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend Section 6 of RA 7883 by granting travel allowances to barangay health workers to aid in the fulfillment of their duties. The token amount can help defray the cost of transportation incurred in the performance of their duties. More importantaly, it will also exhibit the government's appreciation of their heroic efforts.

name: HBN-00201
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING FRANKING PRIVILEGES TO SANGGUNIANG BARANGAYS
congress_website_abstract: Sangguniang Barangays (SBs are often required to communicate with other agencies. However, the limited budget of SBs also limit their capacity to send communications essential in the performance of their duties. To address this problem, the bill proposes to grans SBs limited franking privileges for communications sent to other government agencies.

name: HBN-00202
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FILING OF CERTIFICATES OF CANDIDACY, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SEC. 74 OF BATAS PAMBANSA BLG. 881, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE OMNIBUS ELECTION CODE
congress_website_abstract: An ordinary applying vying for a certain job is required by his prospective employer to present a National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) clearance and a police clearance to determine his integrity, character and trustworthiness. The same should be required of a candidate for public office to show that he has always observed good conduct and has obeyed the laws. This measure seeks to require the presentation of an NBI clearance and police clearance as additional requirements in the filing of certificates of candidacy of persons aspiring to run for public office.

name: HBN-00203
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE PHILIPPINE COAST GUARD AS A SEPARATE AND DISTINCT SERVICE ATTACHED TO THE DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION AND COMMUNICATIONS, FURTHER AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 5173, AS AMENDED AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Philippine Coast Guard Law of 2001\". The bill seeks to amend RA 5173 by providing for the establishment of the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) as a separate and distinct armed service attached to the Department of Transportation and Communications. The PCG, under the bill, shall have its own organizational set-up with uniformed and non-uniformed personnel and shall have the authority to establish Civilian Volunteer Organizations or the PCG Auxiliary which shall assist the PCG in the protection of the marine environment, promotion of safety of life at sea and other activities aimed to enhance maritime community relations.

name: HBN-00204
congress_website_title: AN ACT CHANGING THE NOMENCLATURE OF THE MEMBERS OF THE CUSTODIAL FORCE OF THE BUREAU OF CORRECTIONS AND ADJUSTING THEIR SALARY GRADES ACCORDINGLY AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Bureau of Corrections Custodial Force Act of 2004\". The measure seeks to change the title from \"Prison Guard\" to \"Corrections Officer\" attached to the ranks of the Custodial Force of the Bureau of Corrections and to adjust their salary grades accordingly. Department Circular No. 39, issued by the Secretary of Justice, renamed the Bureau of Prisons to the present Bureau of Corrections to conform to ongoing trends in modern penology - shifting from antiquated punitive system of incarceration to the humanistic rehabilitation approach. The same principle is also applied in the bill.

name: HBN-00205
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING TWO (2) ADDITIONAL BRANCHES OF METROPOLITAN TRIAL COURTS OF THE NATIONAL CAPITAL REGION, INCREASING THE NUMBER OF METROPOLITAN TRIAL COURTS FROM EIGHTY-TWO (82) TO EIGHTY-FOUR (84), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE BATAS PAMBANSA BLG. 129, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE JUDICIARY REORGANIZATION ACT OF 1980, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00206
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING THE APPOINTMENT OF ELECTIVE GOVERNMENT OFFICIALS WITHIN ONE YEAR FROM RESIGNATION
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to prohibit the appointment of resigned elective government officials to posts in the government until after the lapse of one year from the resignation of the official concerned. This would deter elective officials from accepting offers to assume another position caused by vacancies or resignations of certain officials. Elected officials who assume vacancies in other posts in the government, in effect, abandon the mandate of the people who elected them.

name: HBN-00207
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING CIVIL SERVICE ELIGIBILITY TO CONTRACTUAL AND CO-TERMINUS GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES WHO HAVE RENDERED A TOTAL OF FIVE YEARS OF EFFICIENT SERVICE, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Contractual and co-terminous employees are part of the civil service. However, they can not be given permanent appointment due to lack of appropriate civil service eligibility. HB 207 proposes to grant civil service eligibility to contractual and co-terminous government employees who have rendered a total of five (5) years of efficient service. The proposal would promote efficiency in the service considering that these employees will have the assurance of security of tenure and prevent the frequent turnover of personnel.

name: HBN-00208
congress_website_title: AN ACT BROADENING THE SCOPE OF UNLAWFUL PRACTICES OF MATCHING FILIPINO WOMEN FOR MARRIAGE TO FOREIGN NATIONALS TO INCLUDE VARIOUS SCHEMES MADE AVAILABLE THROUGH THE INTERNET, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6955
congress_website_abstract: RA 6955 declared as unlawful the practice of matching Filipino women for marriage to foreign nationals on a mail-order basis and similar practices. However, RA 6955 failed to anticipate the evolution of the e-mail bride scheme. The bill aims to broaden the scope of unlawful and punishable schemes of matching Filipino women to foreign nationals to include those made through e-mail and/or websites in the internet. A number of syndicates use these schemes to recruit women into prostitution.

name: HBN-00209
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE PENALTY IMPOSED AGAINST POLLUTERS OF NAVIGABLE WATERS BY AMENDING SECTION 7 OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 979 PROVIDING FOR THE REVISION OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 600 GOVERNING MARINE POLLUTION
congress_website_abstract: It is imperative that stiff civil and criminal penalties be imposed against polluters of our marine environment since we are dependent on the sea and other bodies of water for our transportation, livelihood and other related activities. The bill seeks to increase the penalties being imposed under the present law to a fine of not less that five million pesos (PhP 5.0) or imprisonment of not less than 8 years and 1 day nor more than 12 years or both such fine and imprisonment.

name: HBN-00210
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING THE RIGHT TO FRANCHISEES TO DEVELOP THE RURAL AIRPORT INFRASTRUCTURE NETWORK IN THE PHILIPPINES FOR COMMERCIAL AND OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Our government places the highest priority on poverty alleviation, particularly in the countryside. As a strategy to achieve this, the government should focus on rural development coupled with private sector participation. The establishment of private rural airport infrastructures will encourage economic activity in the hinterlands, by serving not only as transportation hubs, but also as industrial, commercial and service centers which will provide the countryside employment to uplift the standard of living in the rural areas.

name: HBN-00211
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE LTO EXTENSION OFFICE LOCATED IN MUNTINLUPA CITY INTO A REGULAR LTO DISTRICT OFFICE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00212
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7926, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'THE CHARTER OF THE CITY OF MUNTINLUPA'

name: HBN-00213
title: HOLIDAY: ARAW NG KALAYAAN NG MUNTINLUPA
long_title: AN ACT DECLARING THE FOURTH DAY OF FEBRUARY OF EVERY YEAR AS A SPECIAL WORKING HOLIDAY IN THE CITY OF MUNTINLUPA TO BE KNOW AS ARAW NG KALAYAAN NG MUNTINLUPA
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING THE FOURTH DAY OF FEBRUARY OF EVERY YEAR AS A SPECIAL WORKING HOLIDAY IN THE CITY OF MUNTINLUPA TO BE KNOWN AS ARAW NG KALAYAAN NG MUNTINLUPA

name: HBN-00214
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE MUNTINLUPA SCIENCE HIGH SCHOOL IN THE CITY OF MUNTINLUPA INTO A NATIONAL SCIENCE SECONDARY SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE MUNTINLUPA NATIONAL SCIENCE HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00215
congress_website_title: AN ACT PENALIZING THE PROVISION OF FIREARMS, AMMUNITIONS, EXPLOSIVES, CHEMICAL, BIOLOGICAL OR NUCLEAR WEAPONS OR ANY PARTS OR COMPONENTS USED IN THE MANUFACTURE THEREOF TO ENEMIES OF THE STATE
congress_website_abstract: There has been an upsurge of crimes affecting public order and safety due to the proliferation of illegally possessed and manufactured firearms, ammunition and explosives. The bill renders as unlawful for any person, natural or juridical to knowingly sell, transfer, or in any manner provide, directly or indirectly, firearms, ammunitions, explosives, chemical, biological or nuclear weapons or any part or component used in the manufacture thereof, to enemies of the State. It also proposes a penalty of reclusion perpetua to death and a fine ranging from Php 500,000 to PhP 1.0 million for any violation of the proposed law.

name: HBN-00216
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ARTICLE 16 OF EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 209 AS AMENDED BY EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 227, OTHEWISE KNOWN AS THE FAMILY CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES, MAKING IT MANDATORY FOR COUPLES TO UNDERGO COUNSELING PRIOR TO THE ISSUANCE OF A MARRIAGE LICENSE
congress_website_abstract: \"Mandatory Marriage Counseling Act\". The present law only prescribes marriage counseling in cases where parental consent or parental advise is needed. This bill aims to provide for mandatory counseling for all couples intending to marry. It seeks to educate couples on the rigors of marriage and how to cope with the challenges that will face them everyday for the rest of their lives.

name: HBN-00217
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A MECHANISM FOR THE NATIONAL IDENTIFICATION SYSTEM IN THE PHILIPPINES, PROVIDING FOR BENEFITS AND RIGHTS AND CORRESPONDING OBLIGATIONS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Identification Card System (NICS) Act of 2001\". In some countries like Malaysia, Indonesia, Japan, Germany and South Africe, the adoption of an identification system has provided a useful tool in minimizing, if not eradicating, bureaucratic red tape. The bill seeks to establish an identification system in the Philippines with the aim of reaping the same benefits as those achieved in the abovementioned countries - efficient delivery of service and effective law enforcement.

name: HBN-00218
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE REORGANIZATION OF THE NATIONAL POLICE COMMISSION, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Police Commission Act of 2002\". The bill provides for the reorganization of the National Police Commission to enable it to effectively discharge its functions as described in the Constitution and in pertinent statutes. The Commission shall take charge of police operations relating to the public safety of the nation, administer affairs concerning police education, police commission, criminal identification, criminal statistics and police equipment.

name: HBN-00219
title: FIXED TERM FOR THE CHIEF OF STAFF/MAJOR SERVICE COMMANDERS (AFP)
long_title: AN ACT PRESCRIBING A FIXED TERM FOR THE CHIEF OF STAFF AND MAJOR SERVICE COMMANDERS OF THE ARMED FORCES OF THE PHILIPPINES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_title: AN ACT PRESCRIBING A FIXED TERM FOR THE CHIEF OF STAFF AND MAJOR SERVICE COMMANDERS OF THE ARMED FORCES OF THE PHILIPPINES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Programs of whatever nature usually require a certain length of time to materialize. The person at the helm should likewise enjoy a length of service or security of tenure, to allow him/her adequate time to plan, test and execute programs. This bill endeavors to shield the selection process for the Chief of Staff and Major Service Commanders of the Armed Forces of the Philippines from political and personal considerations by fixing their term of office to 3 years and 2 years, respectively. Extension of term shall not be allowed except for the Chief of Staff whose term may be extended in cases of war or national emergency.
subjects: ['AFP Chief of Staff Fixed Term', 'Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP)']

name: HBN-00220
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT, RATIONALIZING THE ORGANIZATION AND FUNCTIONS OF GOVERNMENT AGENCIES AND CORPORATIONS RELATED TO HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Omnibus Housing and Urban Development Act of 2004\". The country is beset by the problem of shelter. While at present there are government agencies tasked to address certain aspects of the shelter program, the government needs to focus its efforts and its limited resources to make it more efficient and effective. The bill seeks the creation of the Department of Housing and Urban Development to rationalize the shelter and urban development program. It is envisioned to streamline the bureaucracy and the process of formulating human settlement programs to meet the demand of our increasing population and decreasing space.

name: HBN-00221
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING THE MAGNA CARTA FOR DAY CARE WORKERS, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Magna Carta for Day Care Workers\". A large share in the responsibility of childhood development falls on the shoulders of day care workers. Hence, we consider them \"partners\" of many parents in providing early education to our children. However, they are not given the security of tenure, salary and compensation commensurate to the service they render. The proposed bill seeks to promote, protect and improve the social and economic welfare of day care workers. It also aims to professionalize the sector that provides substitute parenting to our children.

name: HBN-00222
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROMOTING THE WELFARE OF THE PHILIPPINE COAST GUARD BY INCREASING, INTEGRATING AND RATIONALIZING THE BASE PAY AND OTHER BENEFITS OF ITS OFFICERS AND ENLISTED PERSONNEL, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Magna Carta of the Philippine Coast Guard\". The bill seeks to provide additional benefits to members of the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) who are not covered under RA 9166 in view of its transfer as an attached agency of the Department of Transporation and Communications (DOTC). The PCG's transfer to the DOTC enhances the PCG's unique status as a uniformed commissioned service operating outside the military organization and working under the umbrella of a civilian department thus placing it in a category of its own.

name: HBN-00223
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A REWARDS FOR INFORMATION CONCERNING TERRORISM PROGRAM AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Rewards for Information Concerning Terrorism Act\". Terrorism has become a threat not only to the lives of individuals but also to peace and order, liberty, property and general welfare essential for the enjoyment of democracy. This bill seeks to provide a reward system for persons who provide information leading to the arrest or prosecution of terrorists or the prevention of terrorist acts.

name: HBN-00224
title: RANK OF FIRST CHIEF MASTER SERGEANT/FIRST MASTER CHIEF PETTY OFFICER (AFP)
long_title: AN ACT CREATING THE RANK OF FIRST CHIEF MASTER SERGEANT/FIRST MASTER CHIEF PETTY OFFICER IN THE ENLISTED RANKS OF THE ARMED FORCES OF THE PHILIPPINES (AFP), APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE RANK OF FIRST CHIEF MASTER SERGEANT/FIRST MASTER CHIEF PETTY OFFICER IN THE ENLISTED RANKS OF THE ARMED FORCES OF THE PHILIPPINES (AFP), APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) Sergeant Major is the most important adviser to the Chief of Staff on matters concerning the discipline and morale of the troops. To ensure that his authority and responsibility are distinct, the measure proposes to create the rank of First Chief Master Sergeant/First Master Chief Petty Officer (E-10) for the enlisted personnel with the highest rank in both position and grade to be held by the AFP Command Sergeant Major. The bill will also rationalize the pay grade of both the officers' corps and the enlisted personnel.
subjects: ['AFP Rank (First Chief Master Sergeant)', 'Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP)']

name: HBN-00225
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING A NATIONAL TRANSPORTATION SAFETY BOARD, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Transportation Safety Act of 2004\". To advance transportation safety consciousness, the bill proposed the creation of the National Transportation and Safety Board (NTSB), an independent, non-regulatory agency, to undertake thorough and impartial investigation of transporation accidents. It shall be responsible for the analysis, evaluation and prevention of air, highway and marine transportation accidents for the effective promotion of safety and prevention of loss of life and property.

name: HBN-00226
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A MEDICAL AND HEALTH CARE LIABILITY LAW, PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Medical and Health Care Liability Act of 2002\". While the present system provides three avenues that a claimant of medical malpractice may take - criminal court for penal sanction, civil court for damages and the Professional Regulation Commission to suspend or revoke a license - the system itself contributes to the delay, if not denial of justice for the victim. The bill seeks to provide the environment where patients will be provided with the best medical care they deserve by seeking to punish those who perform their jobs with negligence and incompetence and weeding them out of the profession.

name: HBN-00227
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING THE MAGNA CARTA FOR HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATIONS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Magna Carta for Homeowners' Associations\". Homeowners' associations aim to improve, in their own little way, the quality of life of Filipino citizens albeit such may be limited to their respective memberships only. The bill seeks to remove the ambiguities concerning the role, rights and powers of homeowners' associations by defining and delimiting the same. It will also define the relationship between members and officers of homeowners' associations, the relationships between homeowners' associations and local government units and the relationships between homeowners' associations and concerned national agencies.

name: HBN-00228
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO ORDAIN AND INSTITUTE THE MARITIME CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: \"Maritime Code of the Philippines\". The bill seeks to update and reform the shipping laws of the Philippines and codify the same into one code. It shall upgrade the laws dealing with registration of ships, maritime liens and ship mortgaged, maritime fraud, accidents at sea and shipowners' liability. It provides for a separate common carrier provision to apply specifically to vessels transporting passengers and goods, domestically or internationally. It updates the marine insurance provisions found in the Insurance Code of 1978. The bill also provides for an alternative dispute settlement in the form of arbitration.

name: HBN-00229
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONSOLIDATING ADMIRALTY AND MARITIME LAWS TO MODERNIZE THE MARITIME INDUSTRY, PROMOTE AND REGULATE MERCHANT MARINE ACTIVITIES, RATIONALIZE THE ORGANIZATION AND FUNCTIONS OF GOVERNMENT AGENCIES RELATED TO ADMIRALTY AND MARITIME MATTERS, AND INSURE THE PROTECTION OF THE MARINE WEALTH IN THE PHILIPPINE TERRITORY
congress_website_abstract: \"Maritime Administration Act of 2004\". At present, the maritime administration functions of the government are spread over seven (7) departments. This structure has resulted in bureaucratic entanglement, functional overlaps and conflicting maritime laws and regulations. These, in turn, has stymied the private sector's dynamism and virtually hamstrung the maritime industry. The bill seeks to put an end to the fragmentation and overlapping of functions of the different government agencies.

name: HBN-00230
congress_website_title: AN ACT ABOLISHING THE DEATH PENALTY IN THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: The measure seeks to abolish the death penalty and for the penalty to be substituted with reclusion perpetua or life imprisonment. Among the arguments for its abolition are: that the death penalty violates the right to life, that the death penalty is not a deterrent to crime as can be proven by scientific studies and that the death penalty is irrevocable and can be inflicted on the innocent.

name: HBN-00231
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 110 OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1529 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE PROPERTY REGISTRATION DECREE AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend Section 110 of PD 1529 (Property Registration Decree) to relieve property owners of the burdensome and onerous task of resorting to judicial actions for the reconstitution of their Torrens Certificate of Title that were lost or destroyed while in the custody of the Register of Deeds. It identifies the Register of Deeds as the proper party to initiate the filing of petitions for the reconstitution of lost or destroyed owner's certificate of title.

name: HBN-00232
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING BARANGAY BATO ROAD IN BARANGAY BATO PANAY, CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00233
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE HIPONA-CANAPIAN-QUINABONGLAN ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF PONTEVEDRA AND MAAYON, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00234
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE POBLACION-PAWA ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF PANAY, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00235
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE STA. FE-CASANAYAN ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF PILAR, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00236
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE PANITAN-TIMPAS-MIANAY ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF PANITAN AND SIGMA, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00237
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING THAT ALL CONTRACTUAL AND CASUAL EMPLOYEES IN THE GOVERNMENT SERVICE BE PAID THEIR CORRESPONDING WAGES DURING SPECIAL PUBLIC HOLIDAYS, AND APPROPRIATIG FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: All contractual and casual employees work under the principle of \"no work no pay\". This scheme may be viewed as unfair considering that they too are burdened by the same problems confronting regular employees such as rising cost of living, basic health care and a decent education for their children. The bill seeks to provide that all contractual and casual employees be paid their corresponding wages during special public holidays.

name: HBN-00238
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE ROXAS CITY-CAGAY-SIBAGUAN-BALIJUAGAN-CUDIAN-IVISAN ROAD IN ROXAS CITY AND THE MUNICIPALITY OF IVISAN, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00239
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE CONCIENCIA-TINIGBAN ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF PANITAN, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00240
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE ROXAS MEMORIAL GENERAL HOSPITAL IN ROXAS, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ, INTO A TERTIARY LEVEL II HOSPITAL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE PRESIDENT MANUEL A. ROXAS MEMORIAL REGIONAL HOSPITAL

name: HBN-00241
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO ESTABLISH A SOCIALIZED AND LOW-COST HOUSING LOAN RESTRUCTURING PROGRAM, PROVIDING THE MECHANISMS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Socialized and Low-Cost Housing Loan Restructuring Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to establish a mechanism that would provide a low-cost housing loan restructuring program. It provides incentives to those who pay monthly amortizations on time by giving them reasonable discounts on their loan interest. Under the bill, all penalties and surcharges shall be condoned upon approval of the application provided that all accrued interests shall be added to the remaining balance of the principal, the aggregate amount of which shall be considered as the new principal amount.

name: HBN-00242
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING FURTHER AND BROADENING THE COVERAGE OF THE SPECIAL PROGRAM FOR EMPLOYMENT OF STUDENTS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7323, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to strengthen the Special Program for Employment of Students (SPES) by allowing students to work not only during summer or Christmas vacations but also for the whole year. It also allows small and medium scale enterprises or entities with more than ten (10) employees to adopt the program. It further penalizes any person or entity who refuses or dishonors the educational vouchers paid by the government with imprisonment of not more than six months to one year and a fine of not less than PhP10,000.00.

name: HBN-00243
congress_website_title: AN ACT DEFINING THE VISION AND POLICIES OF GOVERNMENT IN THE HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT SECTOR, CREATING THE DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT, DEFINING ITS MANDATE, POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Department of Housing and Urban Development Act of 2004\". Ten years after the passage of RA 7279 (Urban Development and Housing Act), the lack of decent and affordable housing facilities remains to be one of the most pervasive socio-economic problems of the country. This bill seeks to create the Department of Housing and Urban Development that will be responsible for the management of housing and urban development. It aims to streamline the different housing agencies and corporations thereby integrating all housing and urban development polices, plans and programs within the purview of expediting the delivery of decent, affordable and sustainable housing and urban development plans.

name: HBN-00244
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE POLITICAL PARTY SYSTEM, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Political Party Development Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to strengthen the political party system and uphold party loyalty. It also seeks to establish a State Subsidy Fund to be used solely for campaign expenditures, operations and programs of accredited national political parties. It hopes to transform the Philippine political parties from personality-oriented to issue-oriented political organizations to provide our constitutents with a better brand of party politics.

name: HBN-00245
congress_website_title: AN ACT RATIONALIZING THE COMPOSITIONS AND FUNCTIONS OF THE NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS COMMISSION THEREBY AMENDING ARTICLES 213, 214, 215 AND 216, BOOK V, TITLE II OF THE LABOR CODE, AS AMENDED, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The National Labor Relations Commission (NLRC) and the labor arbiters play a vital role in the maintenance of industrial peace. The bill seeks to rationalize the composition and functions of the NLRC by granting the Chairman and members of the NLRC almost the same functions and duties as those of the Presiding Justice and Associate Justices of the Court of Appeals, respectively. It also places substantial parity in the structure, functions and duties of labor arbiters with those of Regional Trial Court judges.

name: HBN-00246
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE COMPULSORY REGISTRATION OF HEAVY EQUIPMENT AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Heavy Equipment Registration Act of 2004\". The measure provides for the mandatory registration of heavy equipment throughout the country. The need for the compulsory registration of heavy equipment is imperative in order to: a) monitor the availabity of construction equipment vis-a-vis the infrastructure needs of the country; b) generate substantial income the government; and c) prevent misrepresentation by unscrupulous contractors as to the ownership of heavy equipment.

name: HBN-00247
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING MEASURES TO ENHANCE THE ACCEPTABILITY OF AGRICULTURAL LANDS AS SECURITY FOR LOANS OBTAINED FROM LENDERS, BANKS AND OTHER FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS THEREBY PROMOTING ACCESS TO RURAL CREDIT, PROVIDING MECHANISMS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Enhanced Collateral Value of Farm Lands Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to provide measures to enhance the acceptability of agricultural lands as security for loans obtained from banks and other financial institutions. In the event that the acceptability of agricultural lands as collateral in loan applications is enhanced, access to rural credit will be upheld and encouraged.

name: HBN-00248
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY PAWA MUNICIPALITY OF PANAY, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ TO BE KNOWN AS THE PAWA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00249
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 463, CHAPTER 2, TITLE FOUR, BOOK III OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991 AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Many of our forests have been turned into virual savannas due to illegal logging activities. Recent events have also proven that uncontrolled and unregulated cutting of logs is the major cause of flooding. To preserve our precious forest reserves and to avoid flooding, the bill seeks the mandatory, instead of optional, appointment of the Provincial Environment and Natural Resources Officer (PENRO) who shall ensure that the forest lands in every province are protected from wanton and illegal cutting of logs and that reforestations are undertaken in areas where needed.

name: HBN-00250
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO IMPROVE EFFICIENCY IN THE DELIVERY OF GOVERNMENT SERVICES TO THE PUBLIC BY REDUCING BUREAUCRATIC RED TAPE AND PROVIDING GRAFT AND CORRUPTION
congress_website_abstract: \"Red Tape Reduction Act of 2004\". In order to expeditiously and effectively deliver basic government services to the public, the proposed measure limits the number of signatories required in government transactions and streamlines procedures to attain a smooth flow in the processing of papers and documents.

name: HBN-00251
congress_website_title: REVISED PUBLIC LAND ACT OF THE PHILIPPINE
congress_website_abstract: \"The Revised Public Land Act of the Philippines\". The measure amends the Public Land Act of 1936 to provide for an efficient management and disposition of public lands by intensifying the survey of lands, and hastening the disposition and titling of alienable lands, among others. It also provides for the integration of land surveying activities in order to avoid inconsistent land survey data. It seeks the creation of the Bureau of Land Management which shall be the sole government agency tasked to regulate and conduct all land surveys.

name: HBN-00252
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE COGON-PONTEVEDRA ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF PANITAN AND PONTEVEDRA, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00253
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE DUMULOG-GABUAN-MONGPONG-CULAJAO-BANICA ROAD IN ROXAS CITY, INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00254
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE ADLAWAN-LIONG-JUMAGICJIC-SAN JOSE-CABUGAO ROAD IN ROXAS CITY, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00255
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE LANOT-LOCTUGAN-PANITAN BOUNDARY ROAD IN ROXAS CITY AND THE MUNICIPALITY OF PANITAN, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00256
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE BANICA-BATO-MILIBILI-ARCABALO-BALO ROAD IN ROXAS CITY, PROVINCE OF CAPIZ INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00257
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE REGULATORY FUNCTIONS OF THE PHILIPPINE OVERSEAS EMPLOYMENT ADMINISTRATION (POEA) AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8042, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'MIGRANT WORKERS AND OVERSEAS FILIPINOS ACT OF 1995'
congress_website_abstract: Sections 29 and 30 of RA 8042 (Migrant Workers and Overseas Filipinos Act of 1995) provided for the deregulation of recruitment activities and the eventual phase-out of the regulatory functions of the Philippine Overseas Employment Administration (POEA) over the entertainment industry. Allowing the entertainment industry to run its own affairs in the deployment of overseas performing artists (OPAs) is tantamount to allowing OPAs to be exploited even more. This bill aims to put a stop to the exploitation of the OPAs by seeking the repeal of Sections 29 and 30 of said Act and thus give the POEA more teeth in protecting the welfare of OPAs.

name: HBN-00258
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE IMPOSITION OF THE PENALTY OF RECLUSION PERPETUA INSTEAD OF THE DEATH PENALTY IN CASES WHERE THE LAW PRESCRIBES THE DEATH PENALTY, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The imposition of the death penalty has never been and never will be a deterrent to the commission of crime. Heinous crime continue to increase despite the Death Penalty Law. The bill seeks to abolish the death penalty for the simple reason that the Constitution prohibits the imposition of a cruel and unusual punishment. It also provides for the imposition of reclusion perpetua or life imprisonment, as the case may be, in cases where the law prescribes the imposition of the death penalty.

name: HBN-00259
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING INDIVIDUALS, COOPERATIVES AND OTHER ENTERPRISES ENGAGED IN THE IMPORTATION OF ALL TYPES OF AGRICULTURE AND FISHERIES INPUTS, EQUIPMENT AND MACHINERY FROM PAYMENT OF TARIFFS AND DUTIES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED EIGHT THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED THIRTY-FIVE (RA 8435) OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'AGRICULTURE AND FISHERIES MODERNIZATION ACT OF 1997'
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend the Agriculture and Fisheries Modernization Act (AFMA) by exempting from the payment of tariffs and duties individuals, cooperatives and other enterprises engaged in the importation of all types of agriculture and fisheries inputs, equipment and machineries. At present, the said law only allows the exemption on condition that the imported agriculture supplies shall be used exclusively by the importing enterprise, i.e. the importers cannot sell these products to small farmers, fishers, cooperatives and other members of the agriculture and fisheries sector. Furthermore, the latter can not import directly because only business enterprises, not individuals, are allowed to import under the AFMA.

name: HBN-00260
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE EXEMPTION OF FIXED INCOME EARNERS FROM THE FILING OF INCOME TAX RETURNS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 51 (A)(2)(b) OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8424, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'TAX REFORM ACT OF 1997'
congress_website_abstract: The issuance of certificates of withholding tax to fixed income earners by their employers already serves as a proof of tax payment hence the filing of income tax returns would serve as an added burden not only to these people but also for the government. This bill proposes to simplify the system of tax collection and administration by exempting fixed income earners from the filing of income tax returns.

name: HBN-00261
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING THE RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS OF PATIENTS AND ESTABLISHING A GRIEVANCE MECHANISM FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to stop the common practice of hospitals and health care institutioners in detaining patients because of their failure to fully settle their financial obligations. It provides that patients in emergency cases be given medical attention without any deposit, pledge, mortgage or any form of advance payment for treatment. It also provides the patient the right to be informed of his/her illness in a manner and language understandable to the patient. It gives the legal guardian the right to examine and be given an itemized billing statement.

name: HBN-00262
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 31 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6770, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'THE OMBUDSMAN ACT OF 1989,' AS AMENDED, ALLOWING PRIVATE LAWYERS TO ACT AS PROSECUTORS ON BEHALF OF THE OMBUDSMAN
congress_website_abstract: The bill would give more teeth to the Office of the Ombudsman by allowing competent and well-trained private lawyers to act as prosecutors in cases involving former Presidents and Vice Presidents of the Philippines, incumbent and former cabinet secretaries and undersecretaires, among others.

name: HBN-00263
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE CATALUNAN GRANDE HIGH SCHOOL (MABINI ANNEX) IN BARANGAY CATALUNAN GRANDE, DAVAO CITY FROM THE MABINI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BANGKAL, TALOMO DISTRICT, DAVAO CITY AND ESTABLISHING THE SAME AS AN INDEPENDENT SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE JESUS J. SORIANO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL

name: HBN-00264
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE MABINI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL-GOVERNOR VICENTE DUTERTE ANNEX LOCATED IN BANGKAL, TALOMO DISTRICT, DAVAO CITY FROM THE MABINI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND CONVERTING THE SAME INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS GOVERNOR VICENTE DUTERTE NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00265
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING AN ELECTIVE OFFICIAL IPSO FACTO RESIGNED FROM HIS OFFICE UPON THE FILING OF HIS CERTIFICATE OF CANDIDACY
congress_website_abstract: Section 67 of the Omnibus Election Code provides that an elective official running for a public office other than the one he is currently holding in a permanent capacity, except for the positions of President and Vice President, is considered resigned from office upon filing of his certificate of candidacy. This law has been critized as unfair and unequal in the treatment of candidates. The proposed bill seeks to declare any elective official, whether national or local, running for any public office than the one which he is holding in a permanent capacity as ipso facto resigned from his office upon the filing of the certificate of candidacy.

name: HBN-00266
congress_website_title: AN ACT LIMITING THE PRACTICE OF COSMETIC DERMATOLOGY TO LICENSED AND QUALIFIED PHYSICIANS WITH RESIDENCY TRAINING IN DERMATOLOGY
congress_website_abstract: A significant number of facial care clinics and salons have sprouted and many of these do not have qualified cosmetic surgeons to perform services like facial restructuring and breast augmentation. There have been reports that the practice has resulted to death or caused facial contusions to patients. To protect and promote the right to health of the people, the bill seeks to limit the practice of cosmetic dermatology to licensed and qualified physicians with residency training in dermatology or plastic surgery.

name: HBN-00267
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO ESTABLISH A SOCIALIZED AND LOW-COST HOUSING LOAN RESTRUCTURING PROGRAM, PROVIDING THE MECHANISMS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Socialized and Low-Cost Housing Loan Restructuring Act of 2004\". Only 37,000 out of the delinquent 96,000 borrowers availed of the condonation package offered under the Housing Loan Condonation Act of 1998. However, because of another surge of financial crisis, the borrowers were still unable to compny with the terms and conditions of their restructured loan agreement. To provide relief to these borrowers until such time that our economic conditions improve, the bill seeks to establish a socialized and low-cost housing loan restructuring program for the next three years from the effectivity of the proposed act.

name: HBN-00268
congress_website_title: AN ACT PENALIZING THE APPROPRIATION, ENCROACHMENT, REGISTRATION AND TITLING OF CREEKS, ESTEROS, STREAMS, CANALS, RIVER BANKS AND OTHER PUBLIC WATERWAYS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Numerous statutes and rulings have been issued to the effect that canals, esteros, creeks and similar waterways are of public domain and as such, may not be acquired by prescription and therefore not registrable. However, despite these laws, encroachment, titling and/or registration of these waterways are prevalent. The bill seeks to penalize the appropriation, encroachment, titling, registration of creeks, esteros, streams, canals, river banks and other public waterways in order to prevent or curtain these anomalous acts.

name: HBN-00269
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED EIGHTY FOUR HUNDRED AND TWENTY FOUR, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'TAX REFORM ACT OF 1997', AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Despite the efforts of the government to provide free quality education, a great number of our citizens are unable to enroll in tertiary and vocational schools because of financial constraints. To help cushion the heavy monetary requirement of pursuing higher and technical education, this bill proposes to allow as a deduction from gross income a portion of the matriculation fee paid by the taxpayer for a qualified dependent. The savings generated can be used by the taxpayer to defray other necessities such as food, transportation and school supplies.

name: HBN-00270
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE PRE-NEED PLAN CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: \"Pre-Need Plan Code of the Philippines\". The proliferation of pre-need corporations has raised questions on the industry's capability to provide security to its investors and planholders. There is a need to regulate the industry to protect its investors and planholders from fraud. This bill seeks to provide the legislative framework that will govern the pre-need industry, provide the regulatory standards within the industry to level the playing field, strengthen the Insurance Commission's regulatory capabilities to insure that the indsutry does not become a forum for abuse.

name: HBN-00271
congress_website_title: AN ACT HARMONIZING AND RATIONALIZING THE ADMINISTRATION AND GRANT OF FISCAL AND NON-FISCAL INCENTIVES, REPEALING FOR THIS PURPOSE EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 226, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE OMNIBUS INVESTMENTS CODE OF 1987
congress_website_abstract: \"The Investments and Incentives Code of the Philippines\". The proposed bill seeks to: a) integrate and consolidate the various laws granting incentives into an Omnibus Investments Code; b) set clear guidelines in the selection of investment champions eg. high export potential, etc.; c) set a cap or ceiling on incentives that the government may offer; d) mandate the Board of Investments to oversee the implementation of the Code; e) provide for a harmonized process for application, registration and validation of tax credits; and f) require double entry accounting for transparency purposes.

name: HBN-00272
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING A NATIONAL LAND USE CODE, PROVIDING IMPLEMENTING MECHANISMS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Land Use Code of 2004\". There is an urgent need for a land use code that will allocate land to various competing uses, preserve prime farm lands specially irrigated fields for agricultural purposes, and ensure community participation in defining local land use. The bill provides that a national land use framework be established to define the indicative priorities for land utilization and allocation. A land use policy administration will also be created to set national land use planning and zoning guidelines and standards.

name: HBN-00273
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PLACE SAFETY NETS FOR FILIPINO RICE PRODUCERS BY IMPOSING TARIFFS IN LIEU OF QUANTITATIVE RESTRICTIONS ON RICE IMPORTS, DIRECTING TARIFF COLLECTIONS FROM RICE IMPORTS TO PROJECTS AND PROGRAMS THAT ENHANCE RICE PRODUCTIVITY AND INCREASE FARMERS'INCOMES, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Rice Safety Net Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to adopt the use of tariffs in lieu of non-tariff import restrictions to protect local rice producers in a direct and transparent manner and at the same time, generate revenue necessary to provide the infrastructures and finance the programs that would improve farmers' productivity. The tarrification of rice will stop the destabilizing burden of NFAs intervention mechanisms on the Philippine budget and, at 90% to 100% tariff rate, generate PhP 1.38 billion annually in additional revenues for the government.

name: HBN-00274
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A PROVIDENT PERSONAL SAVINGS PLAN KNOWN AS THE PERSONAL EQUITY AND RETIREMENT ACCOUNT
congress_website_abstract: \"Personal Equity and Retirement Account (PERA) Act of 2004\". About 20% of the country's total labor force are not covered by any form of retirement plan. Savings account cannot be depended upon to provide financial security to those not covered by retirement plans. Moreover, retirement or pension plans offered by pre-need companies are too expensive. A savings scheme is needed to encourage Filipinos to save for their retirement years. The bill provides for the establishment of a provident equity retirement account (PERA) which is a form of savings scheme that help improve the country's saving rate. It proposes to make contributions to the PERA 100% deductible from the taxable income of the contributor.

name: HBN-00275
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO REGULATE THE CABLE TELEVISION INDUSTRY IN THE PHILIPPINES, PROVIDING IMPLEMENTING MECHANISMS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Cable TV Act of 2004\". Ar present, cable TV is classified under the broadcast industry although some sectors claim that it belongs to the telecommunications sector. But cable TV can neither be classified as public utility nor media. The proposed bill tries to resolve this issue by recognizing it as a separate and distinct industry meant to supplement and complement the telecommunications and braodcast sectors. The upgrading of the cable TV industry will allow for alternative modes of communication such as cable internet and cable telephony. These modes of communication will help facilitate exchange and delivery of information in the country.

name: HBN-00276
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE NATIONAL REVENUE AUTHORITY, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Revenue Authority Act of 2004\". The national government's deficit increased from 1.9% of the gross domestic product in 1998 to 4.6% in 2003. To reduce the deficit without compromising development spending, the government needs to improve tax collection, particularly in the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR), which accounts for about 80% of total tax collection. The bill seeks the creation of the National Revenue Authority (NRA) to address the taxpayers' growing dissatisfaction particularly over frontline services, high level of tax evasion/avoidance and increasing perception of corruption in the BIR. The NRA is envisioned to be free from political interference.

name: HBN-00277
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE MANDATE ON THE INTERCONNECTION BETWEEN AND AMONG PUBLIC TELECOMMUNICATIONS ENTITIES, PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR ITS VIOLATION AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill provides punishment for refusal or failure of any telecommunications entity to comply with interconnection regulation after having been required by lawful written order of the National Telecommunications Commission. Acts that result in the termination, disconnection, discontinuance, interruption, blocking and/disruption of interconnection by any public telecommunications entity will be prohibited.

name: HBN-00278
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTIONS 212 AND 214 OF REPUBLIC ACT 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Real property tax (RPT) accounts for about 36% of the total locally-generated income of local government units. However, based on a Commission on Audit report, only 54% of the total potential tax take are actually collected. To improve collection efficiency on RPT, the bill proposes to transfer the approval of the schedule of fair market values from the local Sanggunian to the Department of Finance upon review and recommendation of a regional technical valuation committee. The power to determine the assessment levels and tax rates shall remain with the local Sanggunian.

name: HBN-00279
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING MEASURES TO ENHANCE THE ACCEPTABILITY OF AGRICULTURAL LANDS AS SECURITY FOR LOANS OBTAINED FROM LENDERS, BANKS AND OTHER FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS THEREBY PROMOTING ACCESS TO RURAL CREDIT, PROVIDING MECHANISMS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Enhanced Collateral Value of Farm Lands Act of 2004\". Financial institutions accept only Certificate of Land Titles as collateral for loans. The Certificate of Land Ownership Award (CLOA) given by the Department of Agrarian Reform to amortizing farmers are proof that they are beneficiaries of the Comprehensive Agrarian Reform Program (CARP). However, since CLOAs are not yet Certificates of Land Titles, financial institutions are reluctant to grant loans to beneficiaries of the CARP. The bill seeks to authorize financial institutions to accept CLOAs issued under the CARP as collateral for loans. Granting farmers access to financial institutions will surely promote an increase in agricultural production.

name: HBN-00280
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO SPUR THE PLANTING OF A BILLION TREES THROUGH COMPLEMENTARY TREE PLANTING PROGRAMS OF THE DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES AND VARIOUS GOVERNMENT AGENCIES, LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS, COMMUNITIES, SCHOOLS AND UNIVERSITIES, AND CIVIC AND NONGOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS, ESTABLISHING MINI-FOREST RESERVES IN CITIES, MUNICIPALITIES AND SUBDIVISION PROJECTS, PROMOTING FOREST PLANTATIONS, AND PROTECTING THE REMAINING NATURAL FORESTS, CREATING A FUND THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Billion Trees Act of 2004\". Of the country's forestland of 15.9 million hectares in the 1950s, only about 6.6 million hectares are left today. Thus, there is an urgent need to rehabilitate and reforest our mountains, especially our watershed areas. The bill aims to target the planting of a billion trees covering about 1.0 million hectares over a period of five years. It proposes to tap the police, local government units, military, upland communities, the private sector, NGOs and civic organizations in the reforestation program.

name: HBN-00281
title: MESAOY NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL (DAVAO DEL NORTE)
long_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY MESAOY, MUNICIPALITY OF NEW CORELLA, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE TO BE KNOWN AS THE MESAOY NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY MESAOY, MUNICIPALITY OF NEW CORELLA, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE, TO BE KNOWN AS MESAOY NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
subjects: ['Schools, Establishment (Davao Del Norte)', 'Mesaoy National High School (Davao Del Norte)']

name: HBN-00282
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE DAVAO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX IN BARANGAY SAN MIGUEL, TAGUM CITY, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE, INTO A SEPARATE AND INDEPENDENT HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS LAURETA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00283
title: SAGAYEN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL (DAVAO DEL NORTE)
long_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY SAGAYEN, MUNICIPALITY OF ASUNCION, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE TO BE KNOWN AS THE SAGAYEN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY SAGAYEN, MUNICIPALITY OF ASUNCION, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE, TO BE KNOWN AS SAGAYEN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
subjects: ['Schools, Establishment (Davao Del Norte)', 'Sagayen National High School (Davao Del Norte)']

name: HBN-00284
title: SEMONG NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL (DAVAO DEL NORTE)
long_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY SEMONG, MUNICIPALITY OF KAPALONG, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE TO BE KNOWN AS THE SEMONG NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY SEMONG, MUNICIPALITY OF KAPALONG, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE, TO BE KNOWN AS SEMONG NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
subjects: ['Schools, Establishment (Davao Del Norte)', 'Semong National High School (Davao Del Norte)']

name: HBN-00285
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE TAGUM NATIONAL TRADE SCHOOL IN THE CITY OF TAGUM, PROVINCE OF DAVAO DEL NORTE INTO A COLLEGE OF TECHNOLOGY TO BE KNOWN AS THE DAVAO DEL NORTE COLLEGE OF TECHNOLOGY

name: HBN-00286
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR CORPORATE ACCOUNTABILITY, FIXING THE PENALTIES FOR ANY VIOLATION THEREOF, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Corporate Reform Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to regulate corporate abuses by providing, among others: a) the banning of accounting firms from providing consulting services to companies they are auditing; b) the rotation of an accounting partner overseeing the audits of a specific company every 5 years; c) requiring CEOs to certify to the accuracy of their financial reports under threat of punishment.

name: HBN-00287
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE PRE-NEED PLAN CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: \"Pre-Need Plan Code of the Philippines\". The proliferation of pre-need corporations has raised questions on the industry's capability to provide security to its investors and planholders. There is a need to regulate the industry to protect its investors and planholders from fraud. This bill seeks to provide the legislative framework that will govern the pre-need industry, provide the regulatory standards within the industry to level the playing field, strengthen the Insurance Commission's regulatory capabilities to insure that the industry does not become a forum for abuse and provide for the effective protection of planholders.

name: HBN-00288
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE LIVELIHOOD AND TECHNOLOGY RELATED LOAN CONDONATION ACT OF THE PHILIPPINES

name: HBN-00289
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE GOVERNMENT ASSET MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Government Asset Management Act of 2003\". The bill seeks to establish the Government Asset Management Authority as an independent body responsible for the privatization and disposition of non-performing government assets and the recovery ill-gotten wealth of government officials. The proposed government agency is envisioned to take over and continue the powers, functions and duties of the Presidential Commission on Good Government, the Privatization Council and the Privatization Management Office.

name: HBN-00290
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO AMEND ARTICLE 217 OF THE REVISED PENAL CODE MAKING THE FAILURE TO LIQUIDATE CASH ADVANCE WITHIN THE PERIOD PROVIDED THEREIN A PRIMA FACIE PROOF OF MALVERSATION
congress_website_abstract: The proposed measure seeks to correct the practice of government officials and employees of disregarding the requirement under several laws and regulations to liquidate promptly cash advances granted in the pursuit of official business. The bill provides that the failure of an accountable officer to liquidate his cash advance shall be a prima facie presumption of malversation or misappropriation of such fund.

name: HBN-00291
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING THE DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES TO CONDUCT A RE-SURVEY OF CADASTRAL LOTS IN LAHAR-AFFECTED AREAS, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00292
congress_website_title: AN ACT REDUCING TO FIFTEEN PERCENT (15%) THE AMUSEMENT TAX ON GROSS RECEIPTS FROM ADMISSION FEES OF THEATERS, CINEMAS, CONCERT HALLS, CIRCUSES AND OTHER PLACES OF AMUSEMENT, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 140 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The entertainment industry, which is saddled with numerous taxes, is on the brink of economic collapse and loss of identity. To encourage economic growth, create new jobs, exports and make accessible to consumers a wide range of new media content and services at competitive prices, the bill seeks to reduce the amusement tax on gross receipts from admission fees from 30% to 15%. The bill further seeks to exempt pop, rock or similar concerts from the payment of amusement tax.

name: HBN-00293
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES TO CONVEY THE TITLES OF THE LOTS OCCUPIED BY THE VICTIMS OF THE MT. PINATUBO ERUPTION IN THE RESETTLEMENT AREAS LOCATED IN THE PROVINCES OF PAMPANGA, TARLAC, AND ZAMBALES, DECLARING FOR THE PURPOSE ALL RESIDENTIAL LOTS IN THE RESETTLEMENT AREAS AS ALIENABLE AND DISPOSABLE, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00294
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1986, ENTITLED 'CREATING THE MOVIE AND TELEVISION REVIEW AND CLASSIFICATION BOARD'
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to require all foreign or imported theatrical films to be printed and subtitled in the Filipino national language before the movies can be allowed for distribution and/or exhibition in local theaters or movie houses. The passage of the bill will result in lower laboratory costs, such as processing and printing charges. Moreover, deaf persons or hearing-impaired individuals will benefit from this bill because they can now have equal access to theatrical or cinematographic entertainment.

name: HBN-00295
congress_website_title: AN ACT UPGRADING THE TARLAC SUBDISTRICT ENGINEERING OFFICE INTO A REGULAR DISTRICT ENGINEERING OFFICE TO BE KNOWN AS THE TARLAC SECOND DISTRICT ENGINEERING OFFICE AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00296
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING DRIVERS OF MOTOR OR NON-MOTOR PRIVATE AND PUBLIC VEHICLES FROM SMOKING AND/OR USING CELLULAR PHONE WHILE DRIVING, AND PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: \"No Smoking and Non-Use of Cellular Phone While Driving Act of 2004\". A growing number of drivers use their cell phones while driving, not taking into consideration the consequences it will bring not only to them but also to their passengers and to pedestrians. On the other hand, the dangers of smoking to health and safety need not be elaborated on. The bill seeks to prohibit all drivers of private and public motor or non-motor vehicles from smoking or using cellular phones while driving in order to protect and secure the safety of drivers, passengers and pedestrians.

name: HBN-00297
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR ADDITIONAL BENEFITS TO SENIOR CITIZENS AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED SEVENTY FOUR HUNDRED AND THIRTY-TWO
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to provide additional benefits to senior citizens by granting them a 20% discount on purchases of funeral services (on a pre-need basis) to lessen the burden on the family left behind when the elderly passes on. It proposes the establishment of the Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs (OSCA) at the office of the Governor and city Mayor to be headed by a provincial board member and a city councilor, respectively.

name: HBN-00298
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL TOURISM POLICY AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"National Tourism Policy Act of 2004\". Our tourism industry has grown over the years at an impressive rate. However, the impressive tourism record should not blind us to the alarming state of degradation to our environment and our cultural traditions. This measure aims to guide the course and pace of tourism development in the country and to ensure the continuity of tourism programs.
