It promotes ecotourism as a strategy for low-impact, environmentally sound and community participatory programs that promotes a balance between economic development and environment and natural resource protection.

name: HBN-00299
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING ALL OWNERS OR OPERATORS OF TELEVISION NETWORKS OR STATIONS AND PRODUCERS OF TELEVISION NEWS PROGRAMS TO BROADCAST THEIR NEWS PROGRAMS WITH SUBTITLES AND PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATION THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: Television is the most popular means of communication. Unfortunatley, it denies hearing-impaired individuals the full audio-visual experience to be informed of current happenings in the world. This bill seeks to require all franchise holders, operators of television networks or stations and producers of television programs to broadcast their newscast or new programs, which are national and regional in scope, with subtitles in order to benefit persons with hearing disabilities.

name: HBN-00300
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8435, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT PRESCRIBING URGENT RELATED MEASURES TO MODERNIZE THE AGRICULTURE AND FISHERIES SECTORS OF THE COUNTRY IN ORDER TO ENHANCE THEIR PROFITABILITY, AND PREPARE SAID SECTORS FOR THE CHALLENGES OF GLOBALIZATION THROUGH AN ADEQUATE, FOCUSED AND RATIONAL DELIVERY OF NECESSARY SUPPORT SERVICES, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The enactment of RA 8435 (Agriculture and Fisheries Modernization Act) effectively deprived agro-processors of the importation incentives they used to enjoy under the Investments Priority Program (IPP). Believing that agro-processors have already been covered under RA 8435, the Board of Investments excluded them from its Investment Incentive Program. The lost incentive resulted in a decline in investments in agro-processing activities. The bill seeks to grant tariff-free importation incentive to the agricultrual and fisheries sectors. To level the playing field for local and imported inputs and equipment, purchase of the former shall likewise be given an incentive by way of a tax credit equal to the amount of tariff that should have been paid if it were imported.

name: HBN-00301
congress_website_title: AN ACT FIXING THE SPECIFIC LIMITS OF FORESTLANDS AND NON-FORESTLANDS FOR THE PURPOSE OF PROTECTING, CONSERVING AND ENHANCING THE FOREST RESOURCES OF THE COUNTRY TO ATTAIN ECOLOGICAL BALANCE
congress_website_abstract: Todate, there is no law fixing the total area in the country as forestlands. It is imperative that a specific area for forestlands be established otherwise lands previously considered as forests will gradually vanish. This bill establishes a 50% ratio between forest and non-forest lands in each geographical region. It provides for the establishment of boundaries in all provinces to determine where the forest begins and ends. It represents a concerted, organized and determined action to save and preserve our forests.

name: HBN-00302
congress_website_title: AN ACT RESTRUCTURING THE FISCAL INCENTIVES UNDER EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 226, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE OMNIBUS INVESTMENTS CODE OF 1987, AS AMENDED, AMENDING PERTINENT ARTICLES OF THE CODE, PROVIDING FOR PENALTIES IN VIOLATION THEREOF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to rationalize the country's investment incentives to pave the way for an incentive system that is consolidated and highly focused, cost-effective in promoting investments, time-bound and simple to administer. Rationalization is necessary in order to align our incentive system with those of our ASEAN neighbors and harmonize the same with the WTO (World Trade Organization) agreements particularly on subsidies and countervailing measures. Among the incentives being proposed to be granted are: a) net-operating loss carry-over (NOLCO), b) deferred imposition of the 2% minimum corporate income tax, c) accelerated depreciation, d) double deduction for training expenses and e) double deduction for research and development.

name: HBN-00303
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING FROM INCOME TAXATION THE FIRST THREE YEARS SALARY OF SCHOOL TEACHERS, BOTH IN PUBLIC AND PRIVATE ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY SCHOOLS
congress_website_abstract: Teachers are important instruments in the development of the youth. Unfortunately, the teaching profession is among the lowest income earning professions. As a result, many qualified teachers often seek employment abroad. This bill seeks to exempt from the payment of income taxes the first three years salary of school teachers. The incentive will not only help solve the problem on teacher shortage but it will also help to improve the quality of elementary and high school education in public and private schools.

name: HBN-00304
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE POLITICAL PARTY SYSTEM AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Political Party Act of 2004\". The main thrust of the bill is the establishment of a Presidential Election Campaign Fund in order to curb excessive campaign finance, prevent narco-politics and address the ill effects of prohibited contributions and expenditures. It is designed to minimize politics of patronage, to develop a strong multi-party system and to ensure that the people will vote based on the political platform and the ideologies of the party.

name: HBN-00305
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTING MEASURES TO LOWER THE COST OF MEDICINES BY RATIONALIZING THE COMPETITIVE POSITION OF LOCAL DRUG COMPANIES, PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR ANY VIOLATIONS THEREOF AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Drug Price Act\". This bill seeks to institute measures to lower the cost of medicines by establishing and maintaining an effective drug regulatory system responsive to the Filipino's need for quality healthcare. It allows parallel importation wherein more than one Philippine importer will be granted the right enter into a foreign Agency Agreement with a foreign supplier. If passed, it will enable Filipinos to purchase pharmaceutical products at the lowest international price available and assure them of better quality medicines that will improve their health and the quality of their lives.

name: HBN-00306
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO EXPAND THE POWERS, DUTIES AND FUNCTIONS OF A PUNONG BARANGAY AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 389, CHAPTER 3 OF THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to expand the powers, duties and functions of the punong barangay to include a) the authority to enforce all laws and regulations relating to the prevention, use and sale of illegal drugs, b) perform minor repair and maintenance of municipal and barangay roads in coordination with the mayors of their respective municipalities, and c) conduct annually a census of the population of the barangay and submit the result of the census to the national Statistics Office.

name: HBN-00307
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE FIELD OF LAND TITLE INSURANCE AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 612 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE INSURANCE CODE OF 1978 AS AMENDED BY PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1455 AND 1460 AND BATAS PAMBANSA BLG. 874
congress_website_abstract: In recent months, the Registry of Deeds in many cities and municipalities were rocked by widespread incidents of faking of land titles. These incidents have shaken the very foundation of the Torrens System of land titling. This bill seeks to amend the Insurance Code by creating the field of land title insurance. Title insurance protects whoever is dealing with land titles from any loss which may be incurred should the title later on turn out to be fraudulent or fake. With the introduction of this insurance, those who transact in real estate are compelled to declare the true value of their transaction.

name: HBN-00308
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING SAVINGS DEPOSITS AMOUNTING TO FIFTY THOUSAND PESOS OR LESS FROM WITHHOLDING TAX, AMENDING SECTION 21 (C)(1) OF THE NATIONAL INTERNAL REVENUE CODE THEREBY, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Small Deposits Exemption Law\". The bill seeks to help low income depositors neutralize the effects of inflation by exempting them from the imposition of withholding taxes so that they are not unduly deprived of the use of the interests earned by their deposited money. If no deduction is done, more people can put their extra money in the banking institutions. Consequently, more money will be available for the banks to invest in worthwhile projects to boost our economy.

name: HBN-00309
congress_website_title: AN ACT DEFINING TERRORISM, PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Terrorism Act of 2004\". The security of nations all over the world is gravely threatened by the rise in transnational crimes (TNC). TNCs are grave crimes with transnational dimension perpetrated by organized criminal groups across and within borders. The Philippines has not been spared from these criminal activities. The bill seeks to address the threat of terrorism and provide stiff penalties on persons or groups of persons convicted of terrorism. It also aims to strengthen and expand the organization and operations of the Philippine Center for Transnational Crimes.

name: HBN-00310
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE MOVIE AND TELEVISION REVIEW AND CLASSIFICATION BOARD, REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1986 ENTITLED 'CREATING THE MOVIE AND TELEVISION REVIEW AND CLASSIFICATION BOARD,' AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Movie and Television Review And Classification Board or MTRCB Reorganization Act of 2004\". The bill sets out to create a new Board with the primary functions of classification, review and limited censorship. It further seeks to pave the way for the film industry to undertake self-regulatory functions.

name: HBN-00311
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PROMOTE THE EFFICIENT AND EFFECTIVE DELIVERY OF CONVERGING COMMUNICATION SERVICES
congress_website_abstract: \"Convergence Policy Act of the Philippines 2001\". The advent of internet technology, cable television and other forms of telecommunications have opened the avenue for greater knowledge, education, faster and more efficient transacting and so much more. We may see in the future the possibility of using a single device for various kinds of activities. The bill aims to propose a policy on convergence of the various technologies and means of communications and a long term strategic national plan for the country's communications program.

name: HBN-00312
congress_website_title: AN ACT REGULATING THE USE OF ELECTRIC POLES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Pole Regulation Act of 2004\". Cable companies have found it convenient to lease space for their cables on telephone and electric utility poles. Utilities, in turn, have found it convenient to charge monopoly rates. These rentals are, subsequently, passed on by the cable companies to cable television subscribers. The bill aims to regulate the use of electric poles and other support structures by cable television companies for a pole attachment. The passage of the bill is expected to encourage the growth and development of cable and telecommunications systems, encourage them to provide the widest possible diversity of information sources and services to the public and promote competition in cable communications.

name: HBN-00313
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING THE RELEASE OF FUNDS DIRECTLY TO THE SANGGUNIANG BARANGAY, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The Sangguniang Barangays, more often than not, consider it wiser to use scarce resources on projects and activities that are deemed \"of greater benefit to the barangay\" than Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) concerns. To correct the situation, the bill seeks to give the SK a free hand in the administration of its funds by authorizing the release of funds directly to the Sangguniang Barangay provided that the share of the SK shall likewise be released directly to the SK Treasurer. Releasing the funds directly to the SK give our youth some measure of autonomy that will empower them to implement projects and engage in activities that will build their character, mold them into becoming responsible citizens and equip them with the necessary skills to prepare them for their future role as community leaders.

name: HBN-00314
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROFESSIONALIZING THE BARANGAY TANOD BRIGADES BY UPGRADING THEIR BENEFITS AND INCENTIVES AND MANDATING TERMS TO ENHANCE THEIR FUNCTIONAL EFFICIENCY
congress_website_abstract: Barangay Tanods play an important role in the maintenance of peace and order. Despite their important role, Tanods remain neglected. This measure seeks to professionalize the Barangay Tanod brigades by providing legal standards to enhance their functional efficiency. It proposes to grant hazard pay to Tanods corresponding to the nature of their functions and services. The bill further provides Tanods free legal assistance for criminal and civil cases which may be filed against them in the performance of their duties.

name: HBN-00315
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE REGULAR REMITTANCE OF PREMIUM CONTRIBUTIONS OF BARANGAY OFFICIALS TO THE GOVERNMENT SERVICE INSURANCE SYSTEM (GSIS)
congress_website_abstract: In the past few years, there have been several incidents wherein barangay officials were unable to collect any insurance claim from the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) primarily because of the failure on the part of the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) to remit contributions to the GSIS. The bill proposes to charge the DILG with the duty of consistently remitting premiums to the GSIS on behalf of barangay officials.

name: HBN-00316
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE FAIR AND EQUITABLE ALLOCATION OF THE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION, CULTURE AND SPORTS ITEMS FOR TEACHING AND NON-TEACHING POSITIONS
congress_website_abstract: Although Congress has addressed the urgency of providing for the equitable allocation of the Department of Education (DepEd) with regard to the budget for capital outlay, it failed to consider the equally important items for teaching and non-teaching positions. The bill seeks to provide a mechanism that will guide the DepEd in the hiring and deployment of new teachers and non-teaching personnel. It mandates the DepEd to promulgate the necessary rules and regulations for the equitable allocation of the items for teaching and non-teaching positions among the legislative districts.

name: HBN-00317
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A MAGNA CARTA OF STUDENTS
congress_website_abstract: The bill aims to promote the students' rights and welfare and to enhance their role and potentials towards social change and development. The proposed Magna Carta of Students guarantees the students' right to be represented in policy-making bodies of the school, to govern their own activities, to discipline their own rank and to be accorded due process in disciplinary proceedings.

name: HBN-00318
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING BENEFITS TO COLLEGE AND POST-GRADUATE STUDENTS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Because of the economic crisis that plagued the country, many children quit school thus adding to the increasing number of out-of-school youth. The bill aims to provide some benefits to bona fide college and post-graduate students in public and private academic institutions. By providing some benefits to students, the economic pressure on parents are greatly reduced.

name: HBN-00319
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBERED SIXTEEN HUNDRED AND NINETY FOUR (P.D. 1694), AS AMENDED, CREATING THE WELFARE FUND FOR OVERSEAS WORKERS
congress_website_abstract: The Overseas Workers Welfare Administration Fund (OWWA fund) was created to provide social and welfare services overseas to Filipino workers (OFWs). The funds are held in trust by the OWWA to be utilized, theoretically, on programs and investments favorable to OFWs. However, information has filtered out that the OWWA has been transacting unfavorably to the welfare of OFWs. This bill provides for greater participation by OFWs in the administration and management of their funds. It also seeks to require the prior approval of OFWs in the utilization of OWWA funds.

name: HBN-00320
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 45, CHAPTER 10 AND SECTION 17, CHAPTER 1 OF SUBTITLE II, TITLE VIII OF EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 292, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE '1987 ADMINISTRATIVE CODE BY PROHIBITING A PERSON FROM BEING APPOINTED AS SECRETARY OF NATIONAL DEFENSE WITHIN THREE YEARS AFTER RELIEF FROM ACTIVE DUTY AS COMMISSIONED OFFICER OF THE ARMED FORCES OF THE PHILIPPINES'
congress_website_abstract: The Consitution provides that civilian authority is, at all times, supreme over the military. It may be concluded from this provision that the framers of the Constitution intends to give the position of secretary of national defense a civilian character. The bill seeks to disqualify all military officers from serving as secretary of the Department of National Defense within three years after relief from active duty as a commissioned officer of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP). Three years is deemed sufficient time for relieved members of the AFP to adapt to civilian life.

name: HBN-00321
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING THE MEMBERS OF THE LUPONG TAGAPAMAYAPA MONTHLY HONORARIUM IN THE AMOUNT OF ONE THOUSAND PESOS (P1,000.00), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 406 OF THE 'LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991'
congress_website_abstract: The Local Government Code provides for a system whereby disputes among family members and members of the barangay can be settled amicably at the barangay level, through the Lupong Tagapamayapa (Lupon), without judicial recourse. The system promoted the speedy administration of justice and helped to relieve the courts of docket congestion. To give recognition to the efforts and time exerted by members of the Lupon so that peace and order would reign in the barangay, the bill seeks to grant every member of the Lupon a monthly honorarium of PhP 1,000.00.

name: HBN-00322
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8293, ENTITLED 'AN ACT PRESCRIBING THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY CODE AND ESTABLISHING THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY OFFICE, PROVIDING FOR ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend the Intellectual Property Code through the integration of comprehensive, efficient and adequate strategies designed to respond to internet piracy. It gives as much concern to the rights of performers, phonogram producers and broadcasters. It also seeks to recognize their right to distribution and rental, and their right to receive payment for certain forms of broadcasting or communication.

name: HBN-00323
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING STIFFER PENALTIES TO MAKERS, DRAWERS AND ISSUERS OF BOUNCING CHECKS
congress_website_abstract: At present, the number of cases involving violations of BP 22 have unduly clogged the court dockets. This is because of the \"light\" penalty provided under the existing law (BP 22). To deter individuals from issuing bouncing checks, the bill seeks to impose stiffer penalties to makers, drawers and issuers of bouncing checks.

name: HBN-00324
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBER FOUR HUNDRED FORTY TWO (PD 442), AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LABOR CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES, BY DELETING ARTICLE 130 AND 131 THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: Women's participation in the labor force has increased dramatically in the past decades. Statistics show that the number of women employed in country has increased by 51% from 1980 to 2001. To further increase women's work-participation rate and to channel female labor into genuinely productive tasks, this bill seeks to delete Section 130 and 131 of the Labor Code which prohibit women from rendering nighwork. Such prohibition is prejudicial to women workers.

name: HBN-00325
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO UPDATE THE LAW ON SECURITY DEVICES BY AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 386, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE CIVIL CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES, AND ACT NO. 1508, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE CHATTEL MORTGAGE LAW
congress_website_abstract: \"Security Devise Act of 2001\". The bill seeks to update the following areas in the Law on Security Devices: a) assignment by way of security, b) chattel mortgage and c) pledge.

name: HBN-00326
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING A SYSTEM OF REWARD AND OTHER INCENTIVES FOR PERSONS WHO COULD HELP IDENTIFY, LOCATE, PROVIDE INFORMATION, TESTIFY AND PROSECUTE VIOLATORS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 3019, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE ANTI-GRAFT AND CORRUPT PRACTICES ACT, AND OTHER CRIMINAL OFFENSES COMMITTED BY REASON OF THE FUNCTION OF PUBLIC OFFICE AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREOF, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 3019
congress_website_abstract: The worsening condition of graft and corruption in the country calls for a need to establish a device to fight this menace. The bill seeks to establish a reward mechanism for informants of crimes committed by reason of public office. The reward mechanism provides incentives such as cash rewards, protection, security and other benefits to encourage ordinary citizens to participate in the government's crusade against graft and corruption.

name: HBN-00327
congress_website_title: AN ACT REDEFINING THE WORD 'PROSTITUTES' THEREBY AMENDING ARTICLE 202 NUMBER 5 OF THE REVISED PENAL CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: Prostitution is a social malady that must be curbed for the public good. The Revised Penal Code imposes sanctions on women who engage in this morally degrading occupation but ignores men who, for money or profit, habitually engage in sex or lascivious acts for a fee. The bill seeks to include within the ambit of the Penal Code, all persons (not just women) who engage in prostitution.

name: HBN-00328
congress_website_title: AN ACT SUPPLEMENTING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7080, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'ANTI-PLUNDER ACT'
congress_website_abstract: The Administration's offer of \"house arrest\" to former Pres. Estrada stirred much public controversy. The Sandiganbayan, however, denied such request. The bill seeks to amend the Anti-Plunder Act by inserting a new section - Sec. 3-A. SPECIAL SECURITY ARRANGEMENTS. The proposed provision shall allow the court to, either motu propio or upon motion of the accused, in the interest of national security and public order, undertake special arrangements with the PNP, the military and other concerned agencies.

name: HBN-00329
congress_website_title: AN ACT DEFINING ORGANIZED CRIME, PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Organized Crime Act of 2004\". Organized crime has become pervasive in the country. This bill aims to arm the government with a powerful legal weapon to combat organized crime and its pernicious effects on society. Aside from securing the criminal conviction of members of crime syndicates, it aims to deny them the fruits of their illegal activities.

name: HBN-00330
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ARTICLE 355 OF THE REVISED PENAL CODE BY EXTINGUISHING THE PENALTY OF IMPRISONMENT AND INCREASING THE PENALTY OF FINE
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to extinguish the penalty of imprisonment and to impose higher fines for offenders of the crime of libel. The imposition of a more reasonable set of fines for offenders of the crime of libel is more in keeping with the protection of the right of individuals to freedom of speech and expression rather than the imposition the penalty of imprisonment. Furthermore, considering the present value of the peso, the fines currently imposed are inadequate and must therefore be increased to be commensurate to its purpose.

name: HBN-00331
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ACT NO. 4103, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE INDETERMINATE SENTENCE LAW
congress_website_abstract: Penal laws are viewed as reformatory, retributory and deterrent. These three functions are now quite irrelevant in the case of septuagenarians detained in prison. Their reformation and rehabilitaion would be of no value to themselves, their family and even to society if they are not given a chance to live with their families. Considering their age and mounting mental, physical and emotional incapacity, the bill seeks to make eligible for parole prisoners who are 75 years of age or above, notwithstanding the minimum penalty imposed upon them.

name: HBN-00332
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ARTICLE 266-B OF REPUBLIC ACT NUMBER 8353, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE ANTI-RAPE LAW OF 1997, BY REDUCING THE PENALTY ON INCESTUOUS RAPE FROM DEATH TO RECLUSION PERPETUA
congress_website_abstract: Death penalty is imposed if the crime of rape is committed when the victim is below 18 years old and the offender is a parent, descendant, step-parent, guardian, relative by consanguinity or affinity within the 3rd civil degree or the common-law spouse of the parent of the victim. In most cases, the tendency of the victim is not to report the incident or file charges against her parent who raped her because the penalty is death. This bill seeks to lower the penalty on incestuous rape from death to reclusion perpetua to encourage victims to report the incident, file appropriate charges and testify in court.

name: HBN-00333
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO AMEND ARTICLE 217 OF THE REVISED PENAL CODE MAKING THE FAILURE TO LIQUIDATE CASH ADVANCE WITHIN THE PERIOD PROVIDED THEREIN A PRIMA FACIE PROOF OF MALVERSATION
congress_website_abstract: The Commission on Audit has been looking into possible ways to enjoin government officials and employees to settle their outstanding cash advances promptly. In order to enforce compliance with existing laws and regulations, this bill proposes to make the failure to liquidate cash advances within the specified periods a prima facie presumption of malversation.

name: HBN-00334
congress_website_title: AN ACT REPEALING ARTICLES 333 AND 334 AND AMENDING ARTICLE 344 OF ACT 3815, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED PENAL CODE, AND DEFINING THE CRIME OF MARITAL INFIDELITY
congress_website_abstract: Articles 333 and 334 of the Revised Penal Code penalizes adultery and concubinage, respectively. Under the Code, the wife is meted a heavier penalty for a single act of infidelity. There is thus a perceived disparity in the treatment of husbands, on the one hand, and wives, on the other, insofar as the imposable penalty is concerned. This bill provides for a definition to \"marital infidelity\" and to provide the penalty for the commission thereof. It seeks to correct the seeming disparity in the treatment of husband and wives.

name: HBN-00335
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING THE ROLL-ON/ROLL-OFF TERMINAL SYSTEM, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: With the advent of the roll-on/roll-off terminal system (RRTS), documentary requirements have been simplified thereby reducing red tape. It has resulted in lower prices of basic commodities and an improved food supply at the local, regional and national levels. The institutionalization of the RRTS is expected to spur greater economic growth in the countryside.

name: HBN-00336
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTIONS 67 AND 77 OF BATAS PAMBANSA 881, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'OMNIBUS ELECTION CODE'
congress_website_abstract: The Omnibus Election Code sanctions the substitution of candidates who died, are disqualified or have withdrawn their candidacy until noon on election day. The measure seeks to impose a period within which substitution may be effected. It proposes to require the substitute candidate to file his certificate of candidacy not later than 30 days before election day. It also pushes for the automatic relinquishment of office upon filing of certificate of candidacy of any elective official running for any office, including for President or Vice President, other than the one which he/she is holding in a permanent capacity.

name: HBN-00337
congress_website_title: AN ACT PREVENTING AND PENALIZING COMPUTER-RELATED CRIMES, FURTHER AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF ACT NO. 3815, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED PENAL CODE
congress_website_abstract: The proliferation of advanced and state-of-the art technologies necessitates the enactment of laws to regulate human conduct in this fast changing world. The bill seeks to supplement and update the Revised Penal Code (RPC) to cover computer-related offenses which are not much different from crimes classifically punished under the RPC.

name: HBN-00338
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE PHILIPPINE COMMISSION ON WOMEN, DEFINING ITS POWERS, FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW) was created in 1975 primarily to prepate a national program for the International Women's Year observed in the same year. Its organization is manifestly ad hoc in nature. The bill seeks creation of the Philippine Commission on Women which will absorb the functions of the NCRFW, monitor the implementation of the laws on women, review and evaluate the extent to which women are integrated in all sectors of economic and social life.

name: HBN-00339
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7392, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE PHILIPPINE MIDWIFERY ACT OF 1992, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Midwifery has a significant role in health care and education not only of pregnant women but also of their family and community. However, midwives and their role in the central care of women remain unrecognized and unappreciated. The bill intends to professionalize the practice of midwifery - strengthen its education and training through a revision of its standard curriculum and through a midwifery continuing education. It also provides for more work opportunities for midwives through the step-ladder approach of midwifery education.

name: HBN-00340
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE MIDWIFERY DEVELOPMENT ACADEMY, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes to establish a Midwifery Development Academy which shall serve as a training school and research institute for practicing midwives and midwife-nurses. It shall be responsible for the provision and implementation of a program for the continuing education of midwifery and the conduct of seminars, workshops, conventions and other training programs.

name: HBN-00341
congress_website_title: AN ACT RATIONALIZING THE PARTY-LIST SYSTEM OF REPRESENTATION IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 5 AND SECTION 10 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7941, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'PARTY-LIST SYSTEM ACT'
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend RA 7941 by requiring the sectoral party, as a prerequisite to registration, to show proof that: a) its constituency is spread over the geographical territory of at least a majority of the regions or at least a majority of the provinces or cities in a region; and b) it represents the concerns of at least 300,000 Filipino citizens.

name: HBN-00342
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING A PERSON WHO IS A PERMANENT RESIDENT OR IMMIGRANT OF A FOREIGN COUNTRY FROM BEING APPOINTED TO ANY PUBLIC OFFICE OR POSITION AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBERED EIGHT HUNDRED SEVEN (PD 807) OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE CIVIL SERVICE DECREE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: The Constitution explicitly provides that any public officer who acquires the status of an immigrant of another country during his tenure shall be dealt with by the law. Resident aliens of foreign countries are deemed incapable of placing 100% devotion to the interest and welfare of their homeland since they must also see to their duties as permanent residents of that country. The bill seeks to amend the Civil Service Decree of the Philippines with a provision that no person who is permanent resident or immigrant of a foreign country shall be eligible for appointment to any public office.

name: HBN-00343
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING POP, ROCK OR SIMILAR CONCERTS FROM THE PAYMENT OF AMUSEMENT TAX ON GROSS RECEIPTS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PARAGRAPH (c) SECTION 140 OF REPUBLIC ACT 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The Local Government Code exempted from the payment of amusement taxes the holding of operas, concerts, dramas, recital, painting and art exhibitions, flower shows, musical programs, literary and oratorical presentation exept pop, rock or similar concerts. The imposition of amusement taxes on these live concerts has discouraged organizers and the artists from holding concerts in various venues nationwide. In line with the policy of the State to promote, support and strengthen the development of the entertainment industry, this bill seeks to exempt pop, rock and similar concerts from the payment of amusement tax.

name: HBN-00344
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING ALL RADIO STATIONS WITH MUSICAL FORMAT PROGRAMS TO BROADCAST A TOTAL OF FIFTY (50%) PERCENT OF MUSIC PLAYED IN EVERY CLOCKHOUR ORIGINAL PILIPINO MUSIC (OPM), AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Original Pilipino Music Act of 2004\". The Philippine music industry has suffered enormously from the onslaught of foreign media giants. 80% of the total music played in the airwaves are imported music. There is a need to increase the airplay of original Pilpino music (OPM) to ensure the survival of our local industry. The bill seeks to require all radio stations with musical format programs to devote at least half of their time to broadcasting OPM. The export potential in the international market for Filipino music would be encouraged with such support from the government.

name: HBN-00345
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A P125.00 DAILY ACROSS-THE-BOARD INCREASE IN THE SALARY RATES OF EMPLOYEES AND WORKERS IN THE PRIVATE SECTOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The P125.00 Daily Across-the-Board Wage Increase Act\". The current minimum wages are grossly inadequate to support a decent standard of living hence the proposal to grant a P125.00 daily across-the-board increase for all employees in the private sector, whether agricultural or non-agricultural.

name: HBN-00346
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE REGULATORY FUNCTIONS OF THE PHILIPPINE OVERSEAS EMPLOYMENT ADMINISTRATION (POEA) AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8042, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'MIGRANT WORKERS AND OVERSEAS FILIPINOS ACT OF 1995'
congress_website_abstract: Deregulation of the labor export industry, where \"migration of workers becomes strictly a matter between the worker and his foreign employer\", places migrant workers at greater risk of their rights being violated. This bill seeks to repeal the entire deregulation and phase-out provisions of Sections 29 and 30 of RA 8042 (Migrant Workers and Overseas Filipinos Act of 1995). It will prove the firm commitment of the government to uphold the interest and welfare of overseas Filipino workers.

name: HBN-00347
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PROHIBIT THE PRACTICE OF BLACKLISTING BY RECRUITMENT AND PLACEMENT SERVICES THROUGH THE USE OF ANY MEANS, MECHANISMS OR LISTS WHICH INTEND TO SPECIFICALLY PREVENT OR DETER OVERSEAS FILIPINO WORKERS (OFWs) FROM GAINING PRODUCTIVE EMPLOYMENT, PROVIDING FOR STIFFER PENALTIES THEREOF AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Blacklisting in Overseas Job Applications Act\". The proposed bill seeks to prohibit the practice of recruitment and placement agencies to blacklist \"undesirable\" overseas Filipino workers (OFWa) by any means to prevent or deter them from further gaining productive employment abroad. The process of blacklisting in the labor industry, especially among seafarers, through whatever mode, is violative of the 1987 Constitution and the Labor Code. The bill pursues to penalize any recruitment and placement agency that requires, as a condition of employment or reemployment, an OFW to refrain from lodging any protest, complaint or any similar mode of expression of one's grievance.

name: HBN-00348
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING ADDITIONAL INSURANCE BENEFITS TO ALL PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS, PROVIDING FUNDS FOR THE PAYMENT OF ITS PREMIUMS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Teachers form the backbone of the nation's educational system. Efforts should be undertaken to uplift their welfare. The bill seeks to grant additional insurance benefits to all public school teachers over and above the insurance benefits they currently enjoy under the Government Service Insurance System. The proposal is designed to promtoe the morale of teachers, attract and encourage new graduates to enter the teaching profession and keep qualified teachers from living.

name: HBN-00349
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE MONTHLY PENSION OF RETIRED PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS AND NON-TEACHING PERSONNEL OF THE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: This bill directs the GSIS to increase the monthly pension of retired teachers and the non-teaching personnel of the Department of Education by 100% and their beneficiaries by 50%.

name: HBN-00350
title: PRODUCTIVITY AND PERFORMANCE INCENTIVES AND GAINSHARING ACT OF 2006
long_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A PRODUCTIVITY AND PERFORMANCE INCENTIVES AND GAINSHARING PROGRAM, REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6971, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE PRODUCTIVITY INCENTIVES ACT OF 1990
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A PRODUCTIVITY AND PERFORMANCE INCENTIVES AND GAINSHARING PROGRAM, REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6971, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE PRODUCTIVITY INCENTIVES ACT OF 1990
congress_website_abstract: \"Productivity and Performance Incentives and Gainsharing Act of 2004\".\" RA 6971 was widely ignored by target sectors because of its seemingly unrealistic provisions. To liberalize the law and therefore, make it more attractive to its intended beneficiaries, this bill seeks to establish a productivity and performance incentives and gainsharing program. It aims to eliminate the conditions that unduly limit or regulate the process of productivity improvement.

name: HBN-00351
title: BARANGAY MICRO BUSINESS ENTERPRISES
long_title: AN ACT TO MANDATE BARANGAY MICRO BUSINESS ENTERPRISES (BMBES) TO IMPLEMENT THE MINIMUM WAGE LAW, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9178, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS AN ACT TO PROMOTE THE ESTABLISHMENT OF BARANGAY MICRO BUSINESS ENTERPRISES (BMBES), PROVIDING INCENTIVES AND BENEFITS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO MANDATE BARANGAY MICRO BUSINESS ENTERPRISES (BMBEs) TO IMPLEMENT THE MINIMUM WAGE LAW, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9178, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT TO PROMOTE THE ESTABLISHMENT OF BARANGAY MICRO BUSINESS ENTERPRISES (BMBEs), PROVIDING INCENTIVES AND BENEFITS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES'
congress_website_abstract: Section 8 of RA 9178 [Barangay Micro business Enterprises (BMBEs) Act of 2002] exempt all BMBEs from the coverage of the Minimum Wage Law. Such section is contrary to the constitutional provision that ensures the right of labor to a decent living. This bill seeks to mandate the BMBEs to implement the Minimum Wage Law. It simply reiterates the workers'guarantee to minimum wage to ensure that BNBE workers can economically survive just like the rest of the workers who are receiving the mandated minimum wage.

name: HBN-00352
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO GRANT ALL DULY ELECTED BARANGAY OFFICIALS A MONTHLY COMPENSATION
congress_website_abstract: Barangay Officials Compensation Act of 2001\". Barangay officials play a significant role in ensuring peace, order, harmony and camaraderie in the community. However, the government has not given them proper compensation for their invaluable contributions. This bill proposes to grant duly elected barangay officials a monthly compensation based on the internal revenue allotment of their respective barangays.

name: HBN-00353
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING HEADS AND CHIEFS OF HOSPITALS TO APPROVE PURCHASES OF MEDICINES AND OTHER MEDICAL SUPPLIES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 344 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991' AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Delays in the purchase of medicines and other medical supplies and equipment among local hospitals have resulted in the inefficient delivery of health services. Under the present procedure, vouchers for any purchase have to be signed and approved by concerned local chief executives. This bill seeks to allow heads of departments and chiefs of hospitals to approve the purchase of medicines and other medical supplies necessary for the delivery of basic health services.

name: HBN-00354
congress_website_title: AN ACT ALLOCATING NEWLY CREATED BARANGAYS WITH A SHARE IN THE INTERNAL REVENUE ALLOTMENT, AND AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 285 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to correct the ambiguity and inequity established under Section 285 of the Local Government Code which denies newly created barangays a share in the internal revenue allotment (IRA). It provides for a mechanism whereby the share of the mother barangay will be recomputed according to its reduced population and area and the difference between the former and the recomputed share will be released to the new barangay.

name: HBN-00355
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE AUTOMATIC RETENTION BY THE BARANGAY OF ITS SHARE OF FIFTY PERCENT (50%) IN THE COMMUNITY TAX COLLECTED BY SAID BARANGAY, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 164 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Section 164 (c) of the Local Government Code (LGC) provides that the barangay is entitled to a share of up to 50% of its community tax collection. However, said section also provides that the share of the national government from the community tax collection shall be remitted by the city or municipality concerned within 10 days after the end of each quarter. It is silent as to when the barangay shall receive its share. To correct this situation, the bill proposes to amend the LGC by allowing the barangay treasurer to automatically retain the 50% share of the barangay in the community tax collection and to remit the share of the city or municipality within 10 days after the end of each month.

name: HBN-00356
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING RURAL ELECTRIC COOPERATIVES FROM THE PAYMENT OF THE FRANCHISE, BUSINESS AND REAL PROPERTY TAXES IMPOSED BY THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160
congress_website_abstract: The rural electric cooperatives (RECs) are the partners of the government in the implementation of certain social programs geared to the improvement of the quality of life for rural dwellers. However, the government does not provide the RECs with adequate funds and operating subsidies to support the cash flow requirement their programs. The bill seeks to exempt RECs from the payment of franchise, business and real property taxes to enable them to pursue their missionary of rural electrification.

name: HBN-00357
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF CHAPTER 2, TITLE THREE, BOOK II OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend the sharing scheme of the national wealth vis-a-vis the local government by increasing the share of LGUs in the gross tax collection levied in the utilization and development of the national wealth from 40% to 60%.

name: HBN-00358
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE APPOINTMENT OF A REPRESENTATION FROM THE SENIOR CITIZEN SECTOR IN EVERY SANGGUNIANG BARANGAY, SANGGUNIANG BAYAN, SANGGUNIANG PANLUNSOD AND SANGGUNIANG PANLALAWIGAN
congress_website_abstract: While the Local Government Code provided for representation of the youth in every sanggunian from the barangay to the provincial levels, it omitted to include the elderly or senior citizens. Now numbering about 14.5 million, this bill provides for the appointment of a representative from the senior citizen sector in every sangguniang barangay, sangguniang bayan, sangguniang panlunsod and sangguniang panlalawigan.

name: HBN-00359
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO ENSURE THE AFFORDABILITY OF MEDICINES AND HEALTH CARE IN THE PHILIPPINES BY EXEMPTING FROM ALL DUTIES AND TAXES THE SALE, IMPORTATION, PROCESSING, REPACKING OR MANUFACTURING OF ALL KINDS OF PHARMACEUTICAL PRODUCTS AND THEIR RAW MATERIALS USED IN THE MANUFACTURE THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: In order to reduce health care costs to an affordable level, this bill seeks to exempt from all duties and taxes the importation, sale, manufacturing, repacking or processing of all kinds of pharmaceutical products and raw materials used in the manufacture thereof. If duties and taxes of these products are eliminated, the cost of goods directly related to health care will be lowered and the manufacturers and distributors can pass on the benefit to consumers.

name: HBN-00360
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING A SYSTEM FOR INSURING THE FUNDING OF FINANCIAL BENEFITS AND PRIVILEGES EXTENDED TO PUBLIC SERVICE WORKERS BY LAWS, EXECUTIVE ORDERS, ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS, MEMORANDUM CIRCULARS AND OTHER ADMINISTRATIVE ISSUANCES
congress_website_abstract: There are several existing laws and administrative issuances providing for benefits and privileges for public service workers which have not been fully implemented because of the escape clause \"subject to the availability of funds\". The bill seeks to put a stop to this and to ensure that henceforth all benefits and privileges accorded to public service workers by law and by Executive Orders, Memorandum Orders, Memorandum Circulars and other administrative issuances would be enjoyed by them without any problem.

name: HBN-00361
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO DEFINE AND GUARANTEE PUBLIC SECTOR UNIONISM IN THE PHILIPPINES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Public Sector Unionism Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to promote the welfare of government employees and recognize their right to self-organization and collective bargaining, the right to peaceful assembly and the right to petition the government to raise and ease their grievances. It provides for the creation of a Public Sector Labor Relations Board which shall have jurisdiction to hear and decide over strike cases when these adversely affect the delivery of essential services.

name: HBN-00362
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7432, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT TO MAXIMIZE THE CONTRIBUTION OF SENIOR CITIZENS TO NATION BUILDING, GRANT BENEFITS AND SPECIAL PRIVILEGES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to grant additional privileges to senior citizens by including electric and water utilities and funeral expenses in the list of items subject to the 20% discount privilege. The measure also provides for the exemption of the family homes of senior citizens from the payment of real property taxes. It exempts from the payment of 20% withholding tax interest income derived from the bank deposits of senior citizens.

name: HBN-00363
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR ADDITIONAL BENEFITS TO SENIOR CITIZENS AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED SEVENTY FOUR HUNDRED AND THIRTY TWO
congress_website_abstract: The Constitution provides that \"the family has the duty to care for its elderly members but the state may also do so through just programs of social security\". The bill seeks to grant senior citizens a 20% discount on the purchase of funeral services (on the pre-need basis) to lessen the burden of the family left behind by the elderly when he passes on. It further proposes the establishment of an Office of the Senior Citizen Affairs (OSCA) in the Office of the Governor and City Mayor to be headed by a provincial board member and city councilor, respectively.

name: HBN-00364
congress_website_title: AN ACT ABOLISHING THE DEATH PENALTY AND IMPOSING THE PENALTY OF QUALIFIED RECLUSION PERPETUA IN LIEU THEREOF, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7659 OR THE DEATH PENALTY LAW, ACT 3815, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED PENAL CODE, AS AMENDED, AND OTHER SPECIAL LAWS
congress_website_abstract: The death penalty has not proven to be a deterrent to the commission of crime. The bill seeks to abolish the death penalty and to impose the penalty of qualified reclusion perpetua in lieu thereof.

name: HBN-00365
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTHORIZING GOVERNMENT HOSPITALS TO UTILIZE ALL ITS INCOME FOR THEIR MAINTENANCE AND OTHER OPERATING EXPENSES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Government hospitals are often critized for being poorly maintained. The bill proposes to authorize government hospitals to utilize their income from pharmacy, fees from in-house services and facilities including but not limited to laboratory, radiology, operating room and nursery facilities and income derived from non-patient related services such as parking fees, tour fees, and interview fees for their maintenance and other operating expenditures including capital outlay and for the acquisition of necessary equipment.

name: HBN-00366
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO DEFINE THE PARAMETERS AND LIMITS OF AVAILABILITY OF CIGARETTES AND OTHER TOBACCO PRODUCTS TO MINORS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Non-Smokers Health Protection Act\". The bill prohinbits any person from supplying tobacco or tobacco-related products to children. It also prohibits the display and promotion of tobacco and tobacco-related products, where they can be seen by children, in places where tobacco and tobacco-related products are sold.

name: HBN-00367
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PRESCRIBE A GENERAL RULE IN THE ADVERTISEMENT AND/OR PROMOTION OF CIGARETTE AND/OR TOBACCO PRODUCTS AND ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGES SOLD WITHIN THE PHILIPPINES, IMPOSE CORRESPONDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"General Advertising Act of 2004\". The bill requires the advertisement of cigarettes, tobacco, liquor and beer not to: a) encourage the consumption or promote the increase consumption of the above products; b) depict the act of smoking or drinking; c) appeal directly to minors; d) advertise promotional schemes like \"happy hour\", among others.

name: HBN-00368
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE MOVIE AND TELEVISION REVIEW AND CLASSIFICATION BOARD, REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1986, 'CREATING THE MOVIE AND TELEVISION REVIEW AND CLASSIFICATION BOARD,' AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Movie and Television Review and Classification Board or MTRCB Reorganization Act of 2004\". House Bill 368 proposes to create a new MTRCB tasked to classify and review of motion pictures and television programs, and to impose limited censorhip of the same. It paves the way for the industry to undertake self-regulation. The bill grants the motion picture industry 6 years to start and undertake self-regulation activities.

name: HBN-00369
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PENALIZE A PARENT OR ANY PERSON IN CHARGE OF A MINOR FOUND TO BE RESPONSIBLE FOR PROMOTING, INDULGING, FORCING OR INSENUATING SUCH MINOR TO GET INVOLVED IN SEX ACTIVITIES IN EXCHANGE OF MONETARY OR ANY MATERIAL CONSIDERATION, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SUB-SECTION 3, ARTICLE 335 OF THE REVISED PENAL CODE
congress_website_abstract: According to the Revised Penal Code and to People of the Philippines vs. Perez, rape is committed even if the girl under 12 years old is a prostitute. The bill seeks to hold a parent or any person in charge of a minor, who forces such minor to get involved in sex activities in exchange for monetary or any material consideration, liable for rape and not the person who had sex relation with the minor.

name: HBN-00370
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ARTICLE 96, CHAPTER 3, AND ARTICLE 124, CHAPTER 4, TITLE IV OF EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 209 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE FAMILY CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend Section 96 (on ownerhsip, administration, enjoyment and disposition of community propery) and Section 124 (on conjugal partnership of gains) of the Family Code to grant the wife equal standing with the husband with regard to the administration and enjoyment of community property or conjugal partnership property. The passage of the bill would align the Family Code with the Constitutional guarantee of fundamental equality of men and women before the law.

name: HBN-00371
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7610 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE SPECIAL PROTECTION OF CHILDREN AGAINST CHILD ABUSE, EXPLOITATION AND DISCRIMINATION ACT, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The cases of child rape and sexual molestation remain unabated despite the enactment of RA 7610. The manner in which government agencies and law enforcement officers handle these cases are outrageous. To correct the situation, the bill seeks to amend RA 7610, as follows: a) cause a mandatory hold departure order for these accused on cases involving children below 12 years old; and b)make law enforcers and government officials criminally culpable for willfully abetting these crimes or causing or assisting in the plight of the accused.

name: HBN-00372
title: NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS COMMISSION/LABOR ARBITERS
long_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR RELIEFS FROM AND EXECUTION OF FINAL DECISIONS AND AWARDS OF THE NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS COMMISSION AND THE LABOR ARBITERS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE ARTICLES 223 AND 224 OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 442, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LABOR CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR RELIEFS FROM AND EXECUTION OF FINAL DECISIONS AND AWARDS OF THE NATIONAL LABOR RELATIONS COMMISSION AND THE LABOR ARBITERS AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE ARTICLES 223 AND 224 OF P.D. 442, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LABOR CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: The objective of the bill is to eliminate one layer of appellate body - the Court of Appeals, in relation to the decisions of the National Labor Relation Commission (NLRC). The bill provides that \"IN CASE A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS FILED, THE DECISION SHALL BE EXECUTORY AFTER TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM RECEIPT OF THE RESOLUTION OF SUCH MOTION\". It also provides that \"DECISIONS, RESOLUTIONS OR AWARDS OF THE COMMISSION, EXCEPT THOSE ON CERTIFIED CASES, SHALL BE APPEALABLE ONLY TO THE SUPREME COURT ON CERTIORARI UNDER RULE 65 OF THE RULES OF COURT SOLELY ON QUESTION OF LAW OR JURISDICTION\".
subjects: ['National Labor Relations Commission/Nlrc', 'Labor Arbiters', 'Labor Code of the Philippines (Pd 442)']

name: HBN-00373
congress_website_title: AN ACT ABOLISHING THE DEATH PENALTY AND IMPOSING THE PENALTY OF QUALIFIED RECLUSION PERPETUA IN LIEU THEREOF, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7659 OR THE DEATH PENALTY LAW, ACT 3815, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED PENAL CODE, AS AMENDED, AND OTHER SPECIAL LAWS
congress_website_abstract: The death penalty has not proven to be a deterrent to the commission of crime. If the death penalty is seen as a retributive act of society, keeping a convict in jail for the rest of his is a civilized substitute for it. The bill seeks to abolish the death penalty and to impose the penalty of qualified reclusion perpetua in lieu thereof.

name: HBN-00374
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROTECTING THE RIGHTS TO LABOR OF THE WORKERS OF THE INTERNATIONAL RICE RESEARCH INSTITUTE (IRRI) AS ENSHRINED IN THE 1987 CONSTITUTION, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE ARTICLE 3 OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1620 AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to ensure the protection of the basic rights of the workers of the International Rice Research Institute (IRRI). The bill once enacted into law will correct an injustice done to the workers who have been deprived of their labor rights since the signing of the Marcos decree on April 19, 1979 which granted the IRRI immunity from any penal, civil and administrative proceedings.

name: HBN-00375
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CREATION OF TRAINING, LIVELIHOOD, AND EMPLOYMENT CENTERS IN CITIES AND MUNICIPALITIES NATIONWIDE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to bring education to the grasroots level and promote employment opportunities by creating training, livelihood and employment centers in cities and selected municipalities nationwide. The centers will act as venues for skills development, job assistance and placement, employment generation and acquisition of technology and market information.

name: HBN-00376
title: EMPLOYEES RIGHTS/BENEFITS AND PRIVILEGES
long_title: AN ACT REQUIRING EMPLOYERS TO INFORM THEIR EMPLOYEES OF THEIR RIGHTS, BENEFITS AND PRIVILEGES UNDER EXISTING LAWS, AND COMPANY POLICIES OR EMPLOYMENT CONTRACTS
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING EMPLOYERS TO INFORM THEIR EMPLOYEES OF THEIR RIGHTS, BENEFITS AND PRIVILEGES UNDER EXISTING LAWS, AND COMPANY POLICIES OR EMPLOYMENT CONTRACTS
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes to require employers to inform their employees of their rights, benefits and privileges upon hiring and every time a compensable contringency occurs. It also proposes that the Department of Labor and Employment, with the support of other government agencies, unions, industry organizations, labor federations/centers and non-governmental organizations, launch education programs for workers on a regular basis.

name: HBN-00377
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXPANDING THE PROHIBITED ACTS OF DISCRIMINATION AGAINST WOMEN ON ACCOUNT OF SEX, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE ARTICLES 135 AND 137 OF P.D. 442, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LABOR CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: While there has been a marked increase in the number of working women, there are still occasions when their entry and work opportunities are dampened by discrimination. The bill seeks to amend Articles 135 and 137 of the Labor Code by making it unlawful for any employer to deny any women the benefits of employment or other statutory benefits under our laws by reason of sex, age, ethnic origin or beliefs or relations by affinity or consanguinity.

name: HBN-00378
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING FURTHER AND BROADENING THE COVERAGE OF THE SPECIAL PROGRAM FOR EMPLOYMENT OF STUDENTS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7323, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: To strengthen the Special Program for Employment of Students (SPES), the bill proposes to accomplish the following: a) adjust the income requirement for coverage under the law; b) provide a non-dimunition and automatic increase for its allocation; c) penalize those who dishonor the education vouchers; d) lessen the coverage of employment size of estalishments; and c) make the program a year-round activity.

name: HBN-00379
congress_website_title: AN ACT PENALIZING THE WILLFUL ACT OF CAUSING FORCE OR INTIMIDATION OR HARRASSMENT OR INSENUATION OR ANY SORT OF PROPOSAL TO COMPEL AND CONVINCE ANY PERSON UNDER ONE'S CHARGE TO RENDER SERVICES IN PAYMENT OF DEBTS, PROVIDING STIFFER PENALTIES THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Household Services Protection Act of 2004\". The law prohibits involuntary servitude in any form. The bill attempts to establish a mechanism to stop the prevailing involuntary servitude especially among women and children. It proposes the imposition of the penalty of arresto mayor in its maximum period to prision correctional in its minimum period on any person who compels a debtor to work for him against the debtor's will without any due compensation.

name: HBN-00380
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXPANDING THE LIABILITIES OF INDIRECT EMPLOYERS IN JOB CONTRACTING, COVERING OTHER BENEFITS DUE THEIR EMPLOYEES, FOR THIS PURPOSE AMENDING ARTICLE 106 OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE 442, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LABOR CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: Workers hired by agencies through the job contracting scheme are the most underpaid, neglected and oppressed among the nation's labor force. Many of them do not get the legally mandated benefits. The bill mandates indirect employers to ensure that all benefits hired through job contractors are paid. It provides that if the employees can not get their benefits from their agencies, they can turn to the company for relief.

name: HBN-00381
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO DEFINE THE PARAMETERS OF RESPONSIBILITY OF AN EMPLOYER IN CASES OF SEXUAL HARASSMENT IN THE WORKPLACE
congress_website_abstract: The proposed measure seeks to define the parameters of responsibility of an employer in cases of sexual harassment in the workplace. It obliges the employer to ensure that the company's policy on sexual harassment is known to all employees from the start of their employment. It proposes to strengthen the Sexual Harassment Law by adding in the definition of sexual harassment the notion of the act as \"an unwelcome behavior of a sexual nature or other unwelcome behavior based on sex, that violated the employees integrity at work\".

name: HBN-00382
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO MANDATE THE ESTABLISHMENT OF POEA REGIONAL OFFICES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 247, ENTITLED 'REORGANIZING THE PHILIPPINE OVERSEAS EMPLOYMENT ADMINISTRATION AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES'
congress_website_abstract: The proposed measure mandates the establishment of Philippine Oberseas Employment Agency (POEA) Regional Offices in all regions of the country. The enactment of the bill is seen to enahnce the effectiveness and efficiency of the POEA in responding to changing market and economic conditions and to the call of the national development plan to strengthen the worker protection and regulation components of the overseas employment program.

name: HBN-00383
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PROHIBIT THE USE OF OWWA EMERGENCY REPATRIATION FUND FOR THE REPATRIATION OF UNREGISTERED AND ILLEGAL OVERSEAS FILIPINO WORKERS (OFWs), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 15 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8042 AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to prohibit the Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) from using the OWWA Emergency Repatriation Fund (OERF) to fund the repatriation of overseas Filipino workers (OFW) who are unregistered and illegal. It mandates the use of the Fund for the regiatration of registered and legal OFWs only. The measure will not deny unregistered and illegal OFWs of their right to be repatriated since the Department of Foreign Affairs has its own budget for this purpose.

name: HBN-00384
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO AMEND THE COMPOSITION OF THE POEA GOVERNING BOARD, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 247, ENTITLED 'REORGANIZING THE PHILIPPINE OVERSEAS EMPLOYMENT ADMINISTRATION AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES'
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to expand the composition and membership of the POEA Governing Board to include the OWWA Administrator, the Chairpersons of the Committee on Labor and Employment of both House of Congress, as ex oficio members, a representative from the management sector, one (1) from the sector of land-based OFWs, one (1) from the sector of sea-based OFWs, and one (1) from the OFW-women sector.

name: HBN-00385
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE PHILIPPINE OVERSEAS EMPLOYMENT AGENCY (POEA) TO INCLUDE IN ITS PROGRAM FOR OVERSEAS EMPLOYMENT THE MANDATORY TEACHING OF BASIC SELF-DEFENSE TECHNIQUES FOR WOMEN APPLICANTS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: There has been an alarming upsurge of violence especially against women including women employees working abroad. The bill requires the Philippine Overseas Employment Agency (POEA) to include in its program for overseas employment the mandatory teaching of basic self-defense techniques for women applicants. The lessons will be free of charge. With these lessons, they will not only be physically prepared but they will, at the same time, develop the \"martial arts discipline\".

name: HBN-00386
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE MOVEMENT TO HELP ERADICATE RAPE AND OTHER FEMALE ABUSES '(MOTHER)', MANDATING THE COMPULSORY TEACHING OF SELF-DEFENSE TO ALL FEMALE CONSTITUENTS, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Movement to Eradicate Rape and Other Female Abuses (MOTHER) Act\". Violence against women has a devastating effect on the physical and emotional well-being not only of the victim but also the people around them as well as future generation. To address this concern, the bill seeks to mandate that basic self-defense techniques be taught to enable females to protect themselves in the event of imminent danger. Equipping women with self-defense techniques will also address another sensitive issue - heinous crimes.

name: HBN-00387
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PROVIDE FOR A THREE THOUSAND PESOS (PHP 3,000.00) DAILY ACROSS-THE-BOARD WAGE RATE INCREASE FOR EMPLOYEES AND WORKERS IN THE PUBLIC SECTOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Public Sector Workers' Wage Rate Increase Act of 2004\". The measure seeks to grant all public sector employees and workers a PhP 3,000.00 daily across-the-board wage increase effective immediately. The proposed wage increase would partially restore the lost purchasing power of the workers' wages.

name: HBN-00388
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PROVIDE FOR A FIVE-YEAR PROGRAM FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF SCHOOLBUILDINGS NATIONWIDE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"School Building Act of 2004\". The backlog in the availability of classrooms and school buildings all over the country is severe. The bill seeks to provide for a five-year program for the construction of school buildings nationwide. With innovative building schemes such as build-operate-and-transfer and through partnerships with the private sector and local government units, additional funds could be sourced in order to ease the critical classroom deficiency.

name: HBN-00389
congress_website_title: AN ACT AUTOMATICALLY INCLUDING RETIRED SCHOOL TEACHERS TO BENEFIT FROM SALARY INCREASES GRANTED TO IN-SERVICE TEACHERS, EFFECTING CORRESPONDING ADJUSTMENTS IN THEIR RETIREMENT PENSION
congress_website_abstract: After devoting their prime years and talents to the education of our children and future leaders, school teachers live their retirement years in penury because their retirement benefits are inadequate to cover their basic needs. The bill provides for the automatic adjustment in the pension benefits given to retired school teachers and to retired private school teachers to correspond to increases granted to in-service teachers.

name: HBN-00390
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE NATIONAL AGRARIAN REFORM ADJUDICATION COMMISSION, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The measure seeks the creation of the National Agrarian Reform Adjudication Commission (NARAC) which shall have jurisdiction over all agrarian disputes, including the determination of just compensation. Its adjudicatory functions shall be exercised through five divisions that shall act independently of each other. Each division shall have Provincial Agrarian Reform Adjudicators (PARADs) who shall have exclusive original jurisdiction over all agrarian disputes.

name: HBN-00391
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE PHILIPPINE COCONUT AUTHORITY, INCREASING THE SMALL COCONUT FARMERS' PARTICIPATION IN, AND THEIR BENEFITS FROM, THE COCONUT INDUSTRY, AND BOLSTERING THE INDUSTRY'S DEVELOPMENT, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBERED FOURTEEN HUNDRED AND SIXTY EIGHT, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED COCONUT INDUSTRY CODE
congress_website_abstract: The bill provides for the sustainable development and growth of the coconut and other palm oil industries and ensures that the coconut farmers, especially the small coconut farmers, tillers, workers and owner cultivators, become direct participants and beneficiaries of the growth of the industry. It seeks to establish the Coconut Industry Rehabilitation and Development Fund from the proceeds of the coconut levy collections.

name: HBN-00392
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE, GIVING MUNICIPALITIES MORE EQUITABLE SHARE OF NATIONAL INTERNAL REVENUE TAXES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to amend the Local Government Code in order to remove the disparity in the share of local government units and grant municipalities a more equitable share in the collection of national taxes. The proposed increase in the internal revenue allotment to municipalities shall transfer to projects that would generate employment and livelihood, ensure food security, conserve the environment, increase agricultural productivity and improve health and sanitation.

name: HBN-00393
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE RE-ALLOCATION OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE ALLOTMENT OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 285 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to provide that starting 1999, the internal revenue allotment to be distributed shall be net of the cost of devolved functions and personnel, including personnel hired by local government units before December 1997 to perform the devolved functions, and the cost of city-funded hospitals and legislated benefits, excluding capital outlay. It provides that the cost deducted shall be distributed and remitted directly to the local government units concerned.

name: HBN-00394
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE SHARE OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS IN THE GROSS COLLECTION OF THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT FROM TAXES ON THE UTILIZATION AND DEVELOPMENT OF THE NATIONAL RESOURCES AND RAISING THE PERCENTAGE SHARE OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS IN THE PROCEEDS THEREOF TO LOWER THE COST OF ELECTRICITY IN THE PROVINCE WHERE THE RESOURCES ARE LOCATED, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to allot a larger portion of the royalty and income tax shares of the national government in the utilization and development of the national wealth particularly from hydro, geothermal and other sources of energy for power rate reduction to the provinces where the resources are located. It proposes to increase the share of the local government unit concerned from 40% to 50% of gross tax collection.

name: HBN-00395
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING PARAGRAPH (D), SECTION 324 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Natural disasters like typhoons, floods and earthquake, cause great loss to life and property. To augment the annual calamity fund, the bill seeks to amend Section 324 (d) as follows \"...PROVIDED, THAT THE TWENTY PERCENT (20%) OF THE UNEXPECTED BALANCE OF THE ANNUAL LUMP SUM APPROPRIATION FOR UNFORESEEN EXPENDITURES ARISING FROM THE OCCURRENCE OF CALAMITIES SHALL NOT REVERT TO THE UNAPPROPRIATED SURPLUS OF THE GENERAL FUN, BUT INSTEAD, SHALL BE USED TO AUGMENT THE ANNUAL CALAMITY FUND WHEN DEEMED NECESSARY\".

name: HBN-00396
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE FEDERATION NG MGA SANGGUNIANG KABATAAN THEREBY AMENDING SECTION 436 OF REPUBLIC ACT 7160 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to strengthen the Federation ng mga Sangguniang Kabataan by granting them autonomy in determining their goals and in implementing their programs. It also provides them with an annual budget and a national headquarters with a national secretariat. It further frees the national president of the SK from his/her responsibilities in the local councils so that he/she can attent to his/her studies and functions on a full-time basis.

name: HBN-00397
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING THE STATUTORY PROVISION AUTHORIZING THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES TO MAKE NECESSARY ADJUSTMENT IN THE INTERNAL REVENUE ALLOTMENT OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT INCURS AN UNMANAGEABLE PUBLIC SECTOR DEFICIT, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE SECTION 284 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to protect the internal revenue allotment share of the local government units from funding restrictions or holdback in the event that the national government incurs an unmanageable public sector deficit. Any funding restriction or holdback imposed by the President will mean restraining the freedom of the local government units to operate and respond to the needs of their constituents.

name: HBN-00398
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXPANDING THE TAXING AND OTHER REVENUE-RAISING POWERS OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The proposed amendment to the Local Government Code (LGC) expands the exclusive authority of municipalities to grant fishery privileges in the municipal waters by explicitly including lakes and gulfs within the coverage of the LGC. The bill further expands the authority of local government units to grant tax exemption privileges by including any and all taxes levied and imposed by LGUs.

name: HBN-00399
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 285 AND 286 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991, PROVIDING FOR AN EQUITABLE DISTRIBUTION OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE ALLOTMENT (IRA) TO LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to correct this inequity in the distribution of national tax collections by introducing poverty-sensitive factors in the IRA formula in lieu of population, land area and equal sharing. These factors are population density, poverty incidence and income class of the LGU. If these factors are used to calculate the IRA shares, poorer provinces, cities, municipalities and barangays would be favored over their richer counterparts.

name: HBN-00400
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTING A SYSTEM OF ALLOWING PARALLEL IMPORTATION OF MEDICINES INTO THE COUNTRY
congress_website_abstract: To enable the Department of Health to perform its role in the development of public health, the bill proposes to allow the parallel importation of medicines into the Philippines. The parallel importation of medicines in competition with exclusive distributors would make medicines affordable and compel local manufacturers to lower their prices to more realistic levels.

name: HBN-00401
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE IMPOSITION BY THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT OF AN IDLE LAND TAX, REPEALING FOR THE PURPOSE THE PERTINENT PROVISIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Studies on local finance show that the idle land tax is not being implemented by local government units due to certain operational difficulties. It is believed that if the national government were to impose the idle land tax, local officials will most likely implement the same. The bill seeks to provide for the imposition by the national government of a 10% idle land tax based on the zonal value or the fair market value of the land, whichever is higher.

name: HBN-00402
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE TAX ON IDLE LANDS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE, SECTION 236 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Of the country's total land area of 30 million hectares, around 14.12 hectares are classified as alienable and disposable. Had these lands been utilized, agricultural productivity and food security would not be a critical problem today. This measure seeks to increase the ceiling of the tax on idle lands from 5% to 10% to give local government units more flexibility in its imposition. The current maximum tax of 5% has not proven to be an effective deterrent against the non-utilization of agricultural lands for productive purposes.

name: HBN-00403
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS TO APPROPRIATE 10% OF THEIR DEVELOPMENT FUNDS TO SUPPORT THE SOCIAL REFORM AGENDA, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 324 OF THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The annual budgets of local government units are heavily focused on infrastructure development programs leaving social services and other development programs a second priority. This bill seeks to approximate an equal allotment of local government resources by requiring municipalities, cities and provinces to appropriate not less than 10% of annual development funds to support programs under the ambit of the Social Reform Agenda.

name: HBN-00404
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE AUTOMATIC RELEASE OF THE SHARE OF THE BARANGAY IN THE PROCEEDS OF NATIONAL TAXES AND TAXES ON THE UTILIZATION AND DEVELOPMENT OF THE NATIONAL WEALTH, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE SECTION 286, CHAPTER 1 AND SECTION 293, CHAPTER 2, TITLE 3, BOOK II, RESPECTIVELY, OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991 AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The government is in the process of redistributing power, resources and services from the central government to lower level political communities. However, in many cases, the local officials hold the barangays hostage by withholding the release of their share. The bill provides that the share of the barangays shall not be subject to any lien or holdback that may be imposed by the provincial, city or municipal government for whatever purpose.

name: HBN-00405
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING THAT THE SCHEDULE OF FAIR MARKET VALUES OF REAL PROPERTY SHALL BE PREPARED BY THE LOCAL ASSESSMENT COUNCIL AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: At present, the preparation of the schedule of fair market values of real property is the sole responsibility of the provincial, city or municipal assessor. Instead of one person performing such function, it is suggested that the function be assigned to a collegial body. This bill seeks to empower the local assessment council to prepare the schedule of fair market values of real property to eliminate the possibility of drawing up a schedule which favors certain groups of sectors or is erroneously done.

name: HBN-00406
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING LOCAL FISCAL AUTONOMY, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
