congress_website_abstract: Various opinions rendered by national government agencies have unduly restricted the utilization of local government funds by the local government units (LGUs). This bill seeks to strengthen the fiscal autonomy of LGU in terms of disposition, allocation and utilization of local government funds from whatever source. It also aims to increase the share of LGUs in internal revenue taxes and other national taxes and fees.

name: HBN-00407
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO AMEND SECTION TWO HUNDRED TWENTY OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE,' AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The Local Government Code granted the Sanggunians the authority to pass an ordinance increasing the assessment value of real property as basis in computing the real estate tax (residential, agricultural, commercial and industrial lands) once every three years. This provision has been viewed as unfair and prejudicial to taxpayers because it unduly burdens the owners of residential properties. This bill seeks to protect the owners of residential property from excessive real estate assessments by limiting the duration wherein a reassessment can be made to every ten years.

name: HBN-00408
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING THE TRANSFER OF ANCESTRAL LAND BELONGING TO THE INDIGENOUS PEOPLE TO NON-MEMBERS, AMENDING SECTION 8 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8371, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: RA 8371 (Indigenous Peoples' Act) does not clearly state whether the sale of ancestral lands by a member of the indigenous cultural communities (ICCs) and indigenous people to a non-member is allowed or not. In the absence of a clear prohibition, it is feared that our ancestral lands will be dissipated gradually. This bill seeks to remove the ambiguity in the law by amending RA 8371 to clearly prohibit the sale of ancestral lands by a member of the ICCs to a non-member.

name: HBN-00409
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED SEVEN THOUSAND NINE HUNDRED FORTY-ONE, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'THE PARTY-LIST SYSTEM ACT'
congress_website_abstract: RA 7941 failed to address the following concerns: 1) the procedure in allocating seats for party-list representatives; and 2) the liberal interpretation which allow mainstream political parties and organizations and non-marginalized sectors to join and participate in the party-list elections. This bill seeks to propose the following: a) a mechanism that will bridge the gap in the existing law; and b) disallow mainstream political parties and non-marginalized sectors from participating in the party-list elections.

name: HBN-00410
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A CEILING ON ALL PUBLIC DEBTS OF THE REPUBLIC OF THE PHILIPPINES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Public Debt Ceiling Act of 2004\". In view of the ballooning public debt of the national government, this bill seeks to impose a debt cap or debt ceiling so as not to hamper the delivery of basic social services and other priority concerns of the government to the detriment of public interest. It sets the total public debt ceiling at PhP1.2 trillion

name: HBN-00411
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING CERTAIN SECTIONS OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBERED SIX HUNDRED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE MARINE POLLUTION DECREE OF 1974
congress_website_abstract: This measure seeks to increase the penalty for the unlawful dumping of wastes and other matters that create hazards to human health, harm living resources and marine life, damages amenities or interfere with the legitimate uses of the sea. It holds the head of a corporation or any partnership culpable for violations of the provisions of the proposed measure. It defines the guidelines under which the Philippine Coast Guard may allow the dumping of waste materials. It likewise,authorizes the adoption of a dumping system.

name: HBN-00412
congress_website_title: AN ACT TOTALLY BANNING LOGGING OPERATIONS FOR THE NEXT 25 YEARS
congress_website_abstract: Our forests are being denuded at an alarming rate. In order to save our remaining forests and to ensure the successful reforestation of badly denuded areas, the measure proposes to impose a total log ban in forests, timberlands, forest reserves and watersheds for the next 25 years.

name: HBN-00413
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE PROMOTION OF BIO-ORGANIC FARMING IN THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: The adoption of bio-organic farming methods not only helps to reduce pollution and abate the destruction of useful organisms for the agricultural ecosystem, it also helps to solve the problem of waste and garbage disposal through the use of plant and animal waste and crop residues.

name: HBN-00414
congress_website_title: AN ACT BANNING FISH CULTURE PENS IN LAKES THROUGHOUT THE COUNTRY
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to prohibit the construction of fish culture pens in lakes and tributaries throughout the country. It also authorizes the summary abatement of existing ones as nuisance per set by the local government units that have jurisdiction over the fish culture pens concerned at the expense of the owners thereof without prejudice to their criminal liability.

name: HBN-00415
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A BARANGAY NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF MURCIA, PROVINCE OF NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE BARANGAY MINOYAN HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00416
congress_website_title: AN ACT URGING THE PRESIDENT AND THE OFFICIALS OF THE DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES TO DECLARE A TOTAL LOG BAN IN THE THIRD DISTRICT OF THE PROVINCE OF NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00417
congress_website_title: AN ACT ALLOWING A FARMER-BENEFICIARY TO MORTAGE HIS RIGHT TO FARM LAND AWARDED TO HIM, WITHOUT LOSING HIS SECURITY TO TENURE AND PROVIDING THAT THE CERTIFICATE OF LAND OWNERSHIP AWARD ISSUED TO HIM UNDER THE COMPREHENSIVE AGRARIAN REFORM PROGRAM SHALL BE ACCEPTED BY BANKS AND OTHER FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS AS COLLATERAL FOR LOANS AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTIONS TWENTY-SEVEN AND TWENTY-ONE OF REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED SIXTY-SIX HUNDRED AND FIFTY SEVEN, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'COMPREHENSIVE AGRARIAN REFORM LAW OF 1998,' AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill seeks to allow farmer-beneficiaries of the Comprehensive Agrarian Reform Law (CARL) to use awarded land as collateral in availing of both short-term and long-term production and other types of loans from commercial banks and other financial institutions for the acquisition of farm equipment and machineries, seeds, fertilizers and other similar items and to allow foreclosure of collateral in case of loan payment default by debtor and re-sale to the Land Bank in the amount equivalent to the unpaid portion of the loan plus interest. It provides that the farmer-beneficiary shall neither lose his security of tenure nor be ejected from the land within the mortgage period provided he regularly pays the amortization.

name: HBN-00418
congress_website_title: AN ACT REORGANIZING THE NATIONAL FOOD AUTHORITY INTO THE NATIONAL FOOD CORPORATION AND THE FOOD DEVELOPMENT AND REGULATORY ADMINISTRATION, REDEFINING THEIR POWERS, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Food Authority Reorganization Act of 2003\" The National Food Authority (NFA) is mandated to ensure food security in staple cereals in times and places of natural and man-made calamity. For the NFA to be truly effective, this bill seeks to strengthen the NFA financially and structurally. It aims to institute reforms in administration and reorganize the NFA into the National Food Corporation attached to the Office of the President.

name: HBN-00419
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE INCLUSION OF ALL ELECTRIC COOPERATIVES ORGANIZED AND EXISTING UNDER PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 269, AS AMENDED, AND REGISTERED WITH THE NATIONAL ELECTRIFICATION ADMINISTRATION (NEA) WITHIN THE COVERAGE OF TAX EXEMPTION PRIVILEGES AND INCENTIVES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: Sections 193 and 234 of the Local Government Code (LGC) effectively withdrew the tax exemption benefits enjoyed by electric cooperatives. The withdrawal of the incentive caused electric cooperatives to suffer heavy financial losses. This bill seeks to exempt electric cooperatives from the payment of real property taxes and other fees imposed by the local government units. It seeks to include all electric cooperatives organized under PD 269, as amended, and registered with the National Electrification Administration, within the coverage of tax exemption privileges and incentives granted under the LGC.

name: HBN-00420
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING FROM WITHHOLDING TAX THE INTEREST EARNINGS ON BONDS ISSUED BY THE LANDBANK OF THE PHILIPPINES TO LANDOWNERS ON THE SALE OF THEIR LANDS COVERED BY THE COMPREHENSIVE AGRARIAN REFORM LAW
congress_website_abstract: To hasten payment to landowners covered by the Comprehensive Agrarian Reform Law (CARL) and to maintain the financial viability of the Land Bank (LBP), one of the modes of compensation to landowners was in the form of LBP bonds bearing interest subject to withholding tax. This bill seeks to exempt from the withholding tax all interest earnings on bonds issued by the LBP to landowners for the sale of their lands covered by the CARL to give landowners some relief from their financial burdens.

name: HBN-00421
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVING FOR THE UPGRADING OF THE RATES OF COMPENSATION OF THE OFFICERS AND EMPLOYEES OF THE JUDICIARY AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Judiciary Compensation Restructure Act of 2003\". The Judiciary Compensation Act granted special monthly allowances to justices and judges only. This resulted in widespread demoralization among the ranks of court officers and employees who perform the bulk of tasks needed in the dispensation of justice. This bill seeks to provide for the upgrading of the rates of compensation of the officers and employees of the Philippine judiciary.

name: HBN-00422
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR SCHOOLS, COLLEGES AND UNIVERSITIES WHICH REFUSE ADMISSION OF QUALIFIED SCHOLARS OF THE PHILIPPINE VETERANS AFFAIRS OFFICE
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to penalize schools that refuse enrolment of qualified Philippine Veterans Office scholars due to non-payment of school bills, with a fine at P10,000.00 for the 1st offense, P20,000.00 for the 2nd offense and P50,000.00 for the 3rd offense.

name: HBN-00423
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING VICTORIAS NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL BARANGAY ESTADO EXTENSION HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY ESTADO, CITY OF VICTORIAS, FROM VICTORIAS NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, VICTORIAS CITY, AND CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00424
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE POSITION OF BARANGAY POPULATION WORKER, GRANTING BENEFITS THERETO, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE 'LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991', AS AMENDED, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The Barangay Population Worker shall be tasked to conduct studies on reproductive technologies currently in use; and collect, analyze and disseminate basic data on population principles and development.

name: HBN-00425
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A BARANGAY NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN THE CITY OF TALISAY, PROVINCE OF NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE BARANGAY EFEGENIO LIZARES NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00426
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO STABILIZE THE SUGAR INDUSTRY BY ESTABLISHING THE SUGAR INDUSTRY RESEARCH AND STABILIZATION FUND AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Since its creation, the programs of the Sugar Regulatory Administration (SRA) have suffered setbacks due to lack of funds. The bill seeks to provide the SRA with adequate funding to enable it to pursue and carry out its mandate by requiring that 20% of the value-added tax collections on sugar and its by-products be channeled back to the sugar industry.

name: HBN-00427
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE ADJUSTMENTS IN THE MONTHLY CONTRIBUTIONS OF PHILHEALTH MEMBERS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED SEVENTY EIGHT HUNDRED AND SEVENTY FIVE, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'NATIONAL HEALTH INSURANCE ACT OF 1995,' AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: At present, the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (Philhealth) covers only 5% to 10% of the total hospitalization expenses of its member. This bill seeks to adjust the monthly contributions of Philhealth members to increase the funds of Philhealth and consequently allow for a more reasonsble coverage in case a member is hospitalized.

name: HBN-00428
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE USE OF UNLEADED GASOLINE AS FUEL IN ALL LAND TRANSPORTS MOTOR VEHICLES
congress_website_abstract: \"Unleaded Gasoline Use Act of 2003\". Lead is a major environmental health hazard. The bill seeks the mandatory use of unleaded gasoline as fuel in all land transport motor vehicles. Unleaded gasoline is a clearer fuel than diesel or leaded gasoline and is estimated to be cheaper compared to the prices of other petroleum products prevailing in the market.

name: HBN-00429
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE NEGROS OCCIDENTAL SCIENCE HIGH SCHOOL TO NATIONAL SCIENCE HIGH SCHOOL IN THE CITY OF VICTORIAS, PROVINCE OF NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, TO BE KNOWN AS THE NEGROS OCCIDENTAL NATIONAL SCIENCE HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00430
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 717, ENTITLED 'PROVIDING AN AGRARIAN REFORM CREDIT AND FINANCING SYSTEM FOR AGRARIAN REFORM BENEFICIARIES THROUGH BANKING INSTITUTIONS'
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to correct the decline in agricultural production loans by redirecting the Agri-Agra Fund's use to favor small farmers. The enactment of the measure is expected to result in the infusion of more than PhP 100.0 billion into the countryside over the next two years. The bill will dramatically reduce rural poverty that has shackled the country for centuries.

name: HBN-00436
title: SYSTEM OF DISPOSITION OF SEIZED/CONFISCATED FOREST PRODUCTS
long_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A SYSTEM OF DISPOSITION OF SEIZED/CONFISCATED ILLEGALLY CUT, GATHERED, REMOVED AND POSSESSED LOGS, LUMBER, FLITCHES AND OTHER FOREST PRODUCTS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A SYSTEM OF DISPOSITION OF SEIZED/CONFISCATED ILLEGALY CUT, GATHERED, REMOVED, AND POSSESSED LOGS, LUMBER, FLITCHES, AND OTHER FOREST PRODUCTS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to prohibit the auctioning of seized/confiscated illegally cut, gathered, removed or processed logs, lumber, flitches and other forest products by the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) through the Natural Resources Development Corporation. It provides a system whereby the DENR shall dispose and apportion confiscated forest products.
subjects: ['Forest Products (Confiscation)', 'Seized/Confiscated Forest Products (System of Disposition)', 'Lumber, Logs, Flitches (Forest Products)']

congress_website_title: ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY ANTICALA, BUTUAN CITY TO BE KNOWN AS ANTICALA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

SANTOS, TUNGAO, SAN MATEO, DANKIAS, AND OTHER NEIGHBORING AREAS IN BUTUAN CITY, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00442
congress_website_title: AN ACT FIXING THE SPECIFIC LIMITS OF FORESTLANDS AND ALIENABLE AND DISPOSABLE LANDS WITHIN THE PROVINCE OF AGUSAN DEL NORTE AND BUTUAN CITY

name: HBN-00443
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE DESIGNATION OF A ONE METER WIDE LANE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF BICYCLE RIDERS IN ALL NATIONAL ROADS OF THE COUNTRY, PRESCRIBING THE PENALTY FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to create a bicycle lane in designated roads so as to provide biking enthusiasts a safe and well-deserved byway where they can pass without obstruction or danger from vehicular traffic. A successful promotion of biking as a means of mass transport will bring about a healthy populace free from respiratory ailments and will drastically cut fuel expenses. It will also declog the streets from too much traffic.

name: HBN-00444
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE DEPARTMENT OF TRADE AND INDUSTRY TO PROVIDE EVERY BARANGAY TEN (10) PIECES OF STANDARDIZED METAL WEIGHTS WEIGHING ONE (1) KILOGRAM EACH TO BE PUT UNDER THE CUSTODY OF THE BARANGAY CAPTAIN AND LOANED FOR FREE TO ANY BARANGAY RESIDENT WHO WISHES TO CHECK THE ACCURACY OF THE WEIGHING SCALE BEING USED IN THE MARKETING OF ANY PRODUCT WHICH PRICE IS DETERMINED BY WEIGHT AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Despite efforts by the Department of Trade and Industry, unscrupulous sellers and traders still manipulate their weighing scales to make a commodity appear to weigh more or weigh less. To ensure that the weighing instruments being used in the trade are \"honest\", this bill proposes to provide every barangay 10 units of standardized metal weighing instrument weighing one kilogram each to be kept under the custody of the barangay captain and loaned for free to any barangay resident.

name: HBN-00445
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING THE PUBLICATION OF LEWD PHOTOGRAPHS AND SEX STORIES AND ARTICLES IN TABLOID AND BROADSHEET NEWSPAPERS, PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to prohibit the publication of lewd photographs and articles in tabloids and broadsheet newspapers. Lewd materials should be kept away from the easy perusal and consumption of the yuoung to ensure a sane and sexually and morally-sound citizenry.

name: HBN-00446
congress_website_title: CARPARK REGULATION LAW OF 2004
congress_website_abstract: The non-availability of ample and safe parking spaces has become a perennial problem for motorists and car owners in practically all government agencies and commercial establishments. This bill seeks to regulate the operation of carparks to assure the public of free and easy access to parking spaces. It will also guarantee that vehicles will be well-protected and free from any attempt of theft and other premeditated damages.

name: HBN-00447
congress_website_title: AN ACT ENSURING SECURITY OF TENURE FOR AGRARIAN REFORM BENEFICIARIES OVER LANDS AWARDED TO THEM UNDER THE COMPREHENSIVE AGRARIAN REFORM PROGRAM, PROVIDING GUIDELINES FOR THE CANCELLATION OF LAND OWNERSHIP AWARDS, PENALTIES FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The rampant cancellation of Emancipation Patents (EPs) and Certificates of Land Ownership Awards (CLOAs) issued to farmer-beneficiaries under the land reform program has become alarming. The cancellations occur mainly because the subject pieces of land were erroneously covered by the program. This bill seeks to reaffirm that EPSs and CLOAs are land titles under PD 1527. The measure provides for compensation to landowners whose lands were erroneously covered by the land reform program. It ensures that the farmer-beneficiaries do not bear the consequences of the Department of Agrarian Reform's errors in distributing lands that should have been exempted from the coverage of the program.

name: HBN-00448
congress_website_title: AN ACT PRESCRIBING THE GEOGRAPHICAL REQUIREMENTS IN THE BIDDING AND AWARDING OF CONTRACTS FOR GOVERNMENT INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1594
congress_website_abstract: In addition to the legal, technical and financial prequalification requirements of prospective contractors spelled out in PD 1594, the bill prescribes certain geographical requirements such that project contractors based in Luzon may only be qualified to undertake government infrastructure projects within Luzon. The same requirement shall apply to contractors based in the Visayas and Mindanao.

name: HBN-00449
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE PENSION PAY OF FILIPINO AMATEUR ATHLETES WHO HAVE WON MEDALS IN THE OLYMPIC GAMES AND PROFESSIONAL FILIPINO ATHLETES WHO HAVE WON WORLD TITLES OR CHAMPIONSHIPS IN DULY RECOGNIZED AND SANCTIONED PROFESSIONAL INTERNATIONAL SPORTING COMPETITIONS
congress_website_abstract: Too often, we hear stories of Filipino former star athletes who brought honor and glory to the country only to succumb to poverty and obscurity in their old age. To duly recognize the sacrifices and efforts of old Filipino sports icons, this bill proposes the grant of a monthly pension to all Filipino athletes who, in the past, copped medals in the Olympic Games and won world titles or championships in duly recognized international professional sports competition.

name: HBN-00450
congress_website_title: AN ACT UPGRADING THE SALARY GRADE LEVEL OF THE SCHOOLS DISTRICT SUPERVISORS FROM SALARY GRADE NINETEEN (19) TO SALARY GRADE TWENTY-FOUR (24)
congress_website_abstract: It is highly anomalous for a subordinate to receive a much higher compensation than his superior. This is exactly the case between Schools District Supervisors (SDS) and Principals or School Heads whose respective salary grades (SG) are SG 19 and SG 21. This bill seeks to correct the anomaly by upgrading the salary grade level of SDS from SG 19 to SG 24. This will give SDS compensation commensurate to their duties and responsibilities.

name: HBN-00451
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8189 ENTITLED, 'AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A GENERAL REGISTRATION OF VOTERS, ADOPTING A SYSTEM OF CONTINUING REGISTRATION, PRESCRIBING THE PROCEDURES THEREOF AND AUTHORIZING THE APPROPRIATION OF FUNDS THEREFOR' BY DELETING SECTION 44 THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: Section 44 of RA 8189 mandates the reassignment of election officers every four years to a new station outside of the congressional district they presently serve. The provision is viewed to be onerous since it dislocates their families, affects the schooling of their children and entails additional expenses in establishing a new residence. This bill seeks to delete Section 44and revert back to Article 7, Section 56 of the Omnibus Election Code as the operative rule governing the reassignment of elections officers.

name: HBN-00452
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING REAL ESTATE DEVELOPERS TO PLANT AT LEAST FIFTY (50) SHADY TREES FOR EVERY HECTARE OF THE SUBDIVISION PROJECT THEY ARE DEVELOPING AND PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes to require real estate developers to plant at least 50 shady trees per hectare of the subdivision/residential areas they are developing. This will provide ecological balance and add up to the aesthetic appearance of the place. It will also greatly help in preventing natural disasters such as flooding and landslides.

name: HBN-00453
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATORILY CREATING THE POSITION OF THE MUNICIPAL GENERAL SERVICES OFFICER, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE BOOK III, TITLE TWO, CHAPTER 2, SECTION 443 (a) OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes the mandatory creation of the position of Municipal General Services Officer to guarantee uniformity of general services management in the provincial, city and municipal levels. The creation of the position would ensure fast delivery of services requiring general services expertise and technical support services. It will also serve to provide an improved system of procurement, care utilization, custody and disposal of supplies and properties of the local government unit.

name: HBN-00454
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING ALL OFFICIAL NON-WORKING DAYS TO FALL EITHER ON A MONDAY OR A FRIDAY (House passed its Senate version (SB02119) on Feb. 07, 2007)
congress_website_abstract: It has been observed that when a holiday falls on a Tuesday, employees tend to be absent on the Monday preceding the Tuesday holiday and on the Friday following the Thursday holiday. This bill proposes that all official non-working holidays be mandatorily scheduled to be observed on either a Monday of a Friday. This bill aims to solve the problem of \"intentional absenteeism\" among workers.

name: HBN-00471
congress_website_title: AN ACT RESTORING, REHABILITATING AND MODERNIZING THE OLD AND EXISTING LINES OF THE PHILIPPINE NATIONAL RAILWAYS (PNR), THE NORTH LINES FROM MANILA TO SAN FERNANDO, LA UNION, THE SOUTH LINES FROM MANILA TO LEGASPI CITY, THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE SORSOGON EXTENSION LINE FROM CUMON, DARAGA, ALBAY TO MATNOG, SORSOGON VIA BULAN, SORSOGON, THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE RAILWAY EXTENSION LINE FROM CALAMBA CITY TO BATANGAS CITY, AND THE ALLOCATION OF SEVENTY MILLION U.S. DOLLARS ($70,000,000.00) AS EQUITY OF THE REPUBLIC OF THE PHILIPPINES FROM THE GENERAL APPROPRIATIONS ACT (GAA) FOR THE FISCAL YEAR 2002 FOR THE USE OF THE OFFICIAL DEVELOPMENT ASSISTANCE (ODA)

name: HBN-00492
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE SECOND ENGINEERING DISTRICT IN THE SECOND CONGRESSIONAL DISTRICT OF THE PROVINCE OF SORSOGON AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00493
congress_website_title: AN ACT PENALIZING MALL AND STORE OWNERS ENGAGING IN THE SALE OF CONTRABAND PRODUCTS
congress_website_abstract: Smuggled and pirated goods not only distort normal pricing mechanisms but also affect other market forces that contribute to the decline of the local economy. This bill seeks to clamp down on these illegal activities by penalizing store/stall owners who engage in the selling of contraband products. It also provide penalties for mall owners who tolerate the selling of contraband products by their tenants.

name: HBN-00494
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE CAPABILITY OF THE BUREAU OF FOOD AND DRUGS (BFAD) BY ESTABLISHING ADEQUATE TESTING LABORATORIES AND FIELD OFFICES IN ALL PROVINCES AND CITIES, UPGRADING ITS EQUIPMENT, CREATING A DRUG ENFORCEMENT UNIT (DEU) UNDER ITS OPERATIONAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE CONTROL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"BFAD Empowerment Act of 2004\" The Bureau of Food and Drugs (BFAD) is tasked to ensure the quality of medicines and pharmaceuticals made available to the public. This bill seeks to introduce major reforms in the BFAD to strengthen the capability of the BFAD's regulatory functions of licensing, registration and monitoring. It provides for the augmentation of existing laboratories and the upgrading of their equipment. It also proposes the creation of a drug enforcement unit under its operation and administrative control.

name: HBN-00495
congress_website_title: AN ACT LIMITING THE BORROWING POWER OF THE PRESIDENT BY PRESCRIBING CERTAIN REQUIREMENTS FOR ALL FORMS OF FOREIGN AND DOMESTIC INDEBTEDNESS AND ALL MATTERS RELATING TO THE COUNTRY'S DEBT MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill provides for the prior approval of the Philippine Congress in negotiating and contracting any loan, whether foreign or domestic, in the name and liability of the national government, including those undertaken by government-owned and controlled corporations and local government units.

name: HBN-00496
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE JOINT LEGISLATIVE-EXECUTIVE NATIONAL DEBT COORDINATING COUNCIL OF THE PHILIPPINES (NDCCP) TO ASSIST THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT IN INSTITUTING REFORMS AND DIRECTIONS IN THE COUNTRY'S FOREIGN DEBT POLICY
congress_website_abstract: This bill calls for a more active participation of Congress in contracting loans in the name of the national government and in negotiations of all matters related to it. It proposes the creation of the Joint Legislative-Executive National Debt Coordinating Council of the Philippines (NDCCP) to assist the national government in the formulation, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of foreign debt management strategies.

name: HBN-00497
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE MANDATORY AUTOPSY OF ANY PERSON WHO DIED IN A VIOLENT, SUSPICIOUS, QUESTIONABLE, UNUSUAL AND/OR UNNATURAL MANNER, THE CONDITIONS FOR THE PROPER ISSUANCE OF THE DEATH CERTIFICATE OF SAID PERSON AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Autopsy Act of 2004\". The bill requires the conduct of a mandatory professional forensic autopsy in cases of violent, unnatural, questionable, mysterious or unusual death of a person. It also addresses the problem of deficient or inadequate autopsy and post-mortem reports that contribute to the non-prosecution or dismissal of complaints because of the absence or lack of guidelines in the conduct and preparation of the same.

name: HBN-00498
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8293, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES, TO LOWER THE COST OF DRUGS AND PHARMACEUTICAL PRODUCTS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: One of the impediments to the implementation of the Generics Law is that drug manufacturers have to wait for a long period for the patent of medicines to expire before they are allowed to manufacture low-priced drugs. To enable the government to control the prevailing price schemes of drug companies and to support the fledging local drug industry, the bill seeks to shorten the patent period of drugs and to institutionalize compulsory licensing.

name: HBN-00499
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE REGULATORY POWER OF THE STATE AND THE POSITION AND COMPETITIVENESS OF LOCAL DRUG FIRMS IN THE IMPORTATION, MANUFACTURE, PACKAGING, SALE AND DISTRIBUTION OF PHARMACEUTICAL DRUGS IN THE PHILIPPINES, AND INSTITUTING FURTHER MEASURES TO LOWER THE COST OF MEDICINES, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Drugs Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to introduce greater state intervention through a more active and efficient regulatory system in the drug industry complemented by a more vigorous and intrusive participation of the Department of Health in the purchase and distribution of drugs to effectively influence the prevailing price schemes in the market.

name: HBN-00500
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING AT LEAST ONE (1) SPECIAL EDUCATION (SPED) CENTER FOR EACH SCHOOL DIVISION AND AT LEAST THREE (3) SPED CENTERS IN BIG SCHOOL DIVISIONS FOR CHILDREN WITH SPECIAL NEEDS (CSNs), CREATING THE IMPLEMENTING MACHINERY THEREOF, PROVIDING GUIDELINES FOR GOVERNMENT FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE AND OTHER INCENTIVES AND SUPPORT, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Special Education Act of 2004\" This bill seeks to institutionalize special education program for children with special needs (CSN). It introduces administrative reforms particularly in the structural framework of the DECS and the formulation of appropriate programs to strengthen existing polices and concerns for CSNs. To give easy access to basic education among CSNs, the measure proposes the establishment of at least one special education (SPED) center in each division school and at least three SPED centers in big schools.

name: HBN-00501
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING COMPULSORY COVERAGE TO BARANGAY DAY CARE WORKERS IN THE NATIONAL HEALTH INSURANCE PROGRAM, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6972, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: In recognition of the invaluable services of the barangay day care center workers who continue to display serious commitment and who are at the forefront in the education and protection of children, the bill proposes to grant them compulsory enrollment in the National Health Insurance Program (PhilHealth) and their dependents to receive and avail of an adequate package of essential health services.

name: HBN-00502
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTIONS 61, 62, 63 ANd 65 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6975 PLACING FOR THE PURPOSE THE PROVINCIAL JAILS UNDER THE JURISDICTION OF THE BUREAU OF JAIL MANAGEMENT AND PENOLOGY (BJMP) APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to remove from the provincial governments the supervision and control over provincial and sub-provincial jails and place them with the Bureau of Jail Management and Penology (BJMP) to avoid confusion as to which government agency should be held responsible for the improvement of these jails and the discipline of its jail personnel.

name: HBN-00503
title: HOLIDAY: SOUTH COTABATO FOUNDATION DAY
long_title: AN ACT DECLARING JULY 18 AS SPECIAL NONWORKING HOLIDAY IN THE PROVINCE OF SOUTH COTABATO IN COMMEMORATION OF ITS FOUNDATION ANNIVERSARY AND THE CULMINATION OF THE T'NALAK FESTIVAL
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING JULY 18TH AS SPECIAL NONWORKING HOLIDAY IN THE PROVINCE OF SOUTH COTABATO IN COMMEMORATION OF ITS FOUNDATION ANNIVERSARY AND THE CULMINATION OF THE T'NALAK FESTIVAL

name: HBN-00487
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTING A PHILIPPINE MARITIME AND OCEANS POLICY AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Philippine Oceans Act of 2000\". In line with the State's policy to protect the nation's maritime wealth in its archipelagic waters and territorial seas, the bill seeks to institutionalize a maritime and oceans policy. It proposes the adoption of a five-year oceans development agenda covering these programs; a) Philippine oceans information system; b) coastal community capability enhancement of the oceans; c) ocean law and administration reforms; d) environmentally sustainable marine resource management; e) archipelagic development planning program; and f) oceans diplomacy

name: HBN-00488
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING STIFFER PENALTIES ON ILLEGAL GAMBLING, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBERED SIXTEEN HUNDRED AND TWO, AS AMENDED
congress_website_abstract: Illegal gambling, directly or indirectly, is perceived to be the reason for rise in social ills. The penalties imposed under the present laws have become inadequate to discourage gambling. The bill seeks to increase the penalties for illegal gambling to make it more effective in stopping this social menace which has fostred wrong values and false hopes among countrymen.

name: HBN-00489
congress_website_title: AN ACT IMPOSING STIFFER PENALTIES ON PHARMACIES AND DRUG STORES WHICH REFUSE TO HONOR SENIOR CITIZEN CARD ISSUED BY THE GOVERNMENT
congress_website_abstract: It has been noted that most pharmacies and drugstores refuse to grant the 20% discount for the purchase of medicines by the elderly as mandated under RA 7432. Some drugstores require a written authorization for purchases made by their representative. This bill seeks to impose stiffer penalties on pharmacies and drugstores which refuse to honor the senior citizen card issued by the government to senior citizens who wish to avail of the benefits and privileges granted under RA 7432.

name: HBN-00490
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONSOLIDATING THE MAPPING FUNCTION AND BUDGET UNDER THE OFFICE OF THE NATIONAL MAPPING AND RESOURCE INFORMATION AUTHORITY (NAMRIA)
congress_website_abstract: Despite the creation of the National Mapping and Resource Information Authority (NAMRIA) which serves as the central mapping agency of the government, it has been observed that some government agencies still do their own mapping services resulting in the duplication efforts and costs. Considering that the NAMRIA is well equipped to handle the job, this bill seeks to consolidate the mapping budget of various departments of the government and centralize such function under the office of the NAMRIA.

name: HBN-00491
congress_website_title: AN ACT PENALIZING DISCRIMINATION AGAINST WORK APPLICANTS
congress_website_abstract: When recruiting new employees, some private companies impose certain requirements that, for some, are almost impossible to comply. Examples of these discriminatory requirements are height, age, skin color and other physical attributes. To promote fairness, equality and justice in employment opportunities, the bill seeks to penalize employers who discriminate against a person for an opportunity to be employed by reason of non-related work requirements.

name: HBN-00512
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A STATE COLLEGE IN THE CITY OF KORONADAL, PROVINCE OF SOUTH COTABATO, TO BE KNOWN AS THE SOUTH COTABATO STATE COLLEGE (SCSC), AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00513
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING COMPULSORY COVERAGE TO ACCREDITED BARANGAY HEALTH WORKERS IN THE NATIONAL HEALTH INSURANCE PROGRAM, AMENDING FOR THIS PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7883, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: In gratitude to the important services of the barangay health workers who are committed, willing to sacrifice financial gains and even endure countless hardships because of the tasks they do, the bill seeks to grant compulsory enrollment in the National Health Insurance Program (PhilHealth) and their dependents to receive and avail of an adequate package of necessary health services.

name: HBN-00514
congress_website_title: AN ACT ENHANCING THE ADMINISTRATION OF JUVENILE JUSTICE, CREATING THE OFFICE OF JUVENILE JUSTICE AND DELINQUENCY PREVENTION UNDER THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to establish a comprehensive juvenile justice system based on the recommendations of the National Convention on the Rights of Children to reform youth offenders and for the government to adhere to the international standards concerning juvenile justice.

name: HBN-00515
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO STRENGTHEN AND PROPAGATE FOSTER CARE FOR ABANDONED AND NEGLECTED CHILDREN AND OTHER CHILDREN WITH SPECIAL NEEDS, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Foster Care Act of 2004\".The bill seeks to sytematize and enhance foster care in the country by providing incentives to qualified foster familites such as subsidy from the government, health insurance and tax exemptions. If this bill is enacted into law, it will also accord a legal framework under which every abused, neglected, surrendered, dependent and abandoned child can be provided with an alternative family.

name: HBN-00516
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING YOUTH CENTERS IN ALL FIFTEEN (15) REGIONS OF THE COUNTRY, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Youth Center Act of 2004\". The bill provides for a concrete program that will alleviate the plight of the youth by establishing an integrated youth center in every region in the country to be utilized as transient shelters, lodging facilities and skills training centers for those who are in need of immediate assistance and attention.

name: HBN-00517
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING A THREE(3)-DAY NON-CUMMULATIVE LEAVE WITH PAY ANNUALLY TO EMPLOYED PARENTS TO ATTEND TO THE SPECIAL NEEDS OF THEIR CHILDREN
congress_website_abstract: \"Parental Leave Act of 2004\". The bill institutionalizes the grant of an annual three-day parental leave of absence with pay to working parents or guardians to enable them to attend to special circumstances involving their children or ward in any school activity.

name: HBN-00518
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE ESTABLISHMENT OF MUNICIPAL LIBRARIES IN ALL MUNICIPALITY NATIONWIDE
congress_website_abstract: The purpose of the bill is to give as many children as possible the chance to develop their potentials by providing them access to informative reading materials through the establishment of municipal libraries nationwide.

name: HBN-00519
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING CENTERS FOR STREET CHILDREN IN EVERY REGION OF THE PHILIPPINES AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Declares it a State policy to prevent the exploitation of infants and children through mendicancy and provide rehabilitative service for those already exploited or in immediate danger of exploitation, and promote the rehabilitation of minors by providing an integrated development package of preventive, interceptive and/or remedial services.

name: HBN-00520
congress_website_title: AN ACT PENALIZING PROSTITUTION, DEFINING THE PERSONS LIABLE THEREFOR, PROVIDING MEASURES AND SUPPORT SERVICES FOR ITS VICTIMS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE REVISED PENAL CODE, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Anti-Prostitution Act\". The bill seeks to end the continuing sexual exploitation of women and children and all forms of gender-based violence; recognizing prostitution as a human rights violation which calls for a coordinated and sustained response from all agencies of the government. It imposes stiff penalties for prostitution and provides social services and programs to help victims of prostitution.

name: HBN-00521
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE CONTROL AND ERADICATION OF RABIES, PRESCRIBING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATION THEREOF, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Rabies Act of 2004\". The bill provides for a system for the control and prevention of the spread and eventual eradication of rabies.

name: HBN-00523
congress_website_title: AN ACT RENAMING THE PHILIPPINE NATIONAL POLICE (PNP) RANKS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED SIXTY-NINE HUNDRED AND SEVENTY-FIVE, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT ACT OF 1990'
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to clarify the common confusion brought about by the change in the classification of different ranks in the Philippine National Police as provided for under RA 6975 that has resulted to an alarming, if not ridiculous, improper identification of the officers.

name: HBN-00524
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE MONTHLY BENEFITS OF BARANGAY OFFICIALS AND PROVIDING ALLOWANCES TO BARANGAY TANODS, BARANGAY HEALTH WORKERS AND BARANGAY SOCIAL WORKERS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE, THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The bill provides for an increase in the monthly allowance granted to the punong barangay from PhP1,000.00 to PhP3,000.00 and to the sangguniang barangay members, barangay treasurer, and barangay secretary from PhP600.00 to PhP1,800.00. It also grants a PhP 1,500 monthly allowance to the lupong tagapamayapa, barangay tanods, barangay health workers and barangay social workers.

name: HBN-00525
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING RETIREMENT BENEFITS TO ALL ELECTIVE OR APPOINTIVE BARANGAY OFFICIALS WHO HAVE RENDERED A CONSECUTIVE PERIOD OF NINE (9)YEARS OR THREE (3) TERMS OF PUBLIC SERVICE IN THE BARANGAY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The bill entitles all elective and appointive barangay officials, who have rendered a consecutive period of nine (9) years or three (3) terms of honorable service, to receive a monthly pension for life amounting to not less than 65% of the monthly average honoraria, allowances and other emoluments authorized by law or barangay, municipal or city ordinance.

name: HBN-00526
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 66 OF BATAS PAMBANSA BLG. 881, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE OMNIBUS ELECTION CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: This bill requires that any person holding an appointive office or position, from the Department Secretary down to the Director level or their equivalent, who intends to run for an elective office, to resign from his position two (2) years prior to the next preceding election.

name: HBN-00527
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING THAT ALL CONTRACTUAL AND CASUAL EMPLOYEES IN THE GOVERNMENT SERVICE BE PAID THEIR CORRESPONDING WAGES DURING SPECIAL PUBLIC HOLIDAYS AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The bill entitles all contractual and casual government employees, who have rendered at least six (6) months of continuous service, to payment of their corresponding daily wages for special public holidays as may be proclaimed by the President of the Philippines.

name: HBN-00528
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING CIVIL SERVICE ELIGIBILITY UNDER CERTAIN CONDITIONS TO A GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEE WHOSE STATUS OF APPOINTMENT IS CASUAL OR CONTRACTUAL AND WHO HAS RENDERED A TOTAL OF TEN (10) YEARS OF EFFICIENT SERVICE
congress_website_abstract: The bill grants civil service eligibility to all government employees who are holding career service positions appointed under casual or contractual status and who have rendered at least ten (10) years of efficient service. Directs the Civil Service Commission to formulate performance evaluation standards in order to determine those qualified to avail of the privilege granted under this act.

name: HBN-00529
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR SURVIVORSHIP BENEFITS TO SURVIVING SPOUSE OF RETIRED JUSTICE-PENSIONER WHO DIES WHILE RECEIVING HIS MONTHLY PENSION, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED NINE HUNDRED TEN, AS AMENDED, ENTITLED 'AN ACT TO PROVIDE FOR THE RETIREMENT OF JUSTICES OF THE SUPREME COURT AND OF THE COURT OF APPEALS, FOR THE ENFORCEMENT OF THE PROVISIONS HEREOF BY THE GOVERNMENT SERVICE INSURANCE SYSTEM, AND TO REPEAL COMMONWEALTH ACT NUMBERED FIVE HUNDRED AND THIRTY-SIX'
congress_website_abstract: The bill provides that THE SURVIVING SPOUSE OF A RETIRED JUSTICE, WHO DIED AFTER RECEIVING RETIREMENT PENSION, SHALL BE PAID A MONTHLY PENSION EQUIVALENT TO EIGHTY PERCENT (80%) OF THE AMOUNT RECEIVED BY RETIRED JUSTICE-PENSIONER UNTIL THE SURVIVING SPOUSE REMARRIES OR DIES AND THE SURVIVING SPOUSE OF A JUSTICE WHO DIED WITHOUT HAVING RECEIVED RETIREMENT PENSION SHALL, IF SURVIVING SPOUSE DOES NOT REMARRY, ALSO BE PAID THE SAME PENSION MONTHLY WHEN THE SURVIVING SPOUSE REACHES THE AGE OF SIXTY-FIVE (65) AND UNTIL THE SURVIVING SPOUSE REMARRIES OR DIES, UNLESS THE SURVIVING SPOUSE IS ACTUALLY RECEIVING A SIMILAR PENSION FOR THE SAME CONSIDERATION FROM OTHER GOVERNMENT FUNDS.

subjects: ['Schools, Establishment (Capiz)', 'Mambusao East National High School (Menhs; Capiz)']

name: HBN-00552
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO MAXIMIZE THE COLLECTION OF REVENUES BY REQUIRING THE FILING OF STATEMENT OF ASSETS, LIABILITIES AND NETWORTH AS OF DECEMBER 31, 2003 AND BY GRANTING TAX AMNESTY ON ALL UNPAID INTERNAL REVENUE TAXES IMPOSED BY THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT FOR TAXABLE YEAR 2003 AND PRIOR YEARS
congress_website_abstract: This bill proposes a tax amnesty program which aims to bring in a large number of corporate and individual taxpayers into the folds of national taxation. It envisions an honest-to-goodness tax amnesty whereby all persons concerned shall be afforded an opportunity to come up with a clean slate by making a true and faithful statement of their true and correct networth as of December 31, 2003.

name: HBN-00553
congress_website_title: AN ACT IMPOSING A SEVEN PERCENT (7%)COMPUTER EDUCATION TAX ON RECEIPTS (EXCLUDING VALUE-ADDED TAX) FROM CELLULAR PHONE CALLS TO BE BORNE BY CELLULAR PHONE COMPANIES, CREATING A SPECIAL COMPUTER LITERACY EDUCATION FUND FOR PUBLIC SCHOOLS, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill creates a Special Computer Literacy Education Fund for public schools, including state colleges and universities, to be constituted from the proceeds of the tax imposed in this Act. The Fund shall be used to promote a comprehensive computer literacy education in public schools.

name: HBN-00554
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR OPTIMUM PERFORMANCE IN REVENUE COLLECTION THROUGH THE GRANT OF SPECIAL INCENTIVES AND REWARDS FOR EXEMPLARY SERVICE AND THROUGH LATERAL ATTRITION IN REVENUE-GENERATING AGENCIES OF GOVERNMENT AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill establishes a syatem of special incentives and rewards in revenue collection to motivate collection officials, officers and employees to render exemplary service while upholding the highest ethical standards in the performance of their tasks.

name: HBN-00555
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE OMNIBUS HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT
congress_website_abstract: \"Omnibus Housing and Urban Development Act of 2004\". The bill seeks to rationalize the housing program of the country to be able to reach out the broadest number of people, especially the needy and marginalized ones. Creates the Department of Housing and Urban Development which is tasked to formulate and implement a comprehensive national urban development strategy and a national shelter strategy and to coordinate in delivering urban infrastructure, services and housing.

name: HBN-00556
congress_website_title: AN ACT FURTHER STRENGTHENING THE HOME DEVELOPMENT MUTUAL FUND AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2004\". The bill strengthens the powers of the Home Development Mutual Fund of Pag-IBIG Fund so that it can meet the challenge of financing the government's national shelter program.

name: HBN-00557
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE DEPARTMENT OF INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Department of Information and Communications Technology Act of 2004\". Merges the communications aspect of the Department of Transportation and Communications (DOTC) with the National Computer Center (NCC), which is presently under the Office of the President (OP), with the purpose of streamlining its inherent functions to address the new phenomenon of converging and emerging information technologies.

name: HBN-00558
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING GOVERNMENT SERVICE AND INSURANCE SYSTEM (GSIS) AND NATIONAL HEALTH INSURANCE CORPORATION (NHIC) MEMBERSHIP TO ACCREDITED BARANGAY HEALTH WORKERS AND BARANGAY TANODS AND OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill mandates the membership of barangay health workers and members of the barangay tanod to the GSIS and NHIC with the national and provincial governments sharing in their monthly contributions on a 50-50 basis.

name: HBN-00559
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE ESTABLISHMENT OF AN ORGANIC FARMING TRAINING AND PRODUCTION FACILITY IN EVERY AGRICULTURAL BARANGAY IN THE COUNTRY AND ESTABLISHING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Organic Farming Act of 2004\". The bill aims to institutionalize the gradual use of organic farm fertilizers and pesticides and eventually ease out the use of petrochemical farm inputs which render agricultural lands acidic and less productive. It encourages the farmers to revert to organic farming system in order to cut on agricultural costs as well as to save the degenerating natural environment in the countryside.

name: HBN-00560
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A FACILITY FOR NON-SCHOOL AGE CHILDREN IN PRIVATE COMPANIES EMPLOYING AT LEAST ONE HUNDRED (100) WOMEN WORKERS AND OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Mother-Child Friendly Facility Act of 2004\". Mandates every private company employing at least one hundred (100) women to establish a drop-in facility for non-school age children in the workplace to ensure their proper care while the mothers are disposing of their professional duties in the company.

name: HBN-00561
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE LEGITIMATION OF CHILDREN BORN TO PARENTS BELOW MARRYING AGE, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE FAMILY CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES, AS AMENDED
congress_website_abstract: The bill aims to legitimize the status of children born to unmarried couples below eighteen (18) years old who remain illegitimate even after their parents eventually enter into a contract of marriage, by amending certain provisions in the Family Code of the Philippines in order for said parents to avoid tedious, expensive and time consuming way of legitimizing these children.

name: HBN-00562
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE RESEARCH OF LOCAL HISTORIES AND INTEGRATING SUCH FINDINGS INTO THE ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY CURRICULUM OF EVERY MUNICIPALITY AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Local Histories Research and Curriculum Integration Act of 2004\". This bill seeks to remedy our youth's obvious lack of information in the context of political consciousness because of so much emphasis in the educational curriculum on national events yet we hardly know the history of our own village or municipality.

name: HBN-00563
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A MORE RESPONSIVE SYSTEM OF CIVIL REGISTRATION
congress_website_abstract: \"New Civil Registration Act of 2004\". Documents concerning the civil status of persons such as birth certificate, death, marriage, and court decrees and orders have become essential instruments of every citizen. However, the basic law that governs this sytem is already outdated. The bill aims to create a new system of civil registration to make it more responsive.

name: HBN-00564
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONSTITUTING A CONSTITUTIONAL CONVENTION TO AMEND OR REVISE THE PRESENT CONSTITUTION, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Constitutional Convention Act of 2004\". The bill calls for a Constitutional Convention that will amend or revise the present Constitution in order to make it more responsive to the demands of the times and meet the requirements of a good Charter possessing the elements of clarity, comprehensiveness and brevity.

name: HBN-00565
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE BOHOL SECOND DISTRICT ENGINEERING OFFICE IN THE PROVINCE OF BOHOL TO BE LOCATED IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF UBAY, PROVINCE OF BOHOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00566
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED 5173, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS AN ACT CREATING A PHILIPPINE COAST GUARD, PRESCRIBING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, APPROPRIATING THE NECESSARY FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES BY ESTABLISHING A SEPARATE AND DISTINCT PHILIPPINE COAST GUARD SERVICE ATTACHED TO THE DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION AND COMMUNICATIONS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Philippine Coast Guard Act of 2004\". The bill intends to transfer the Philippine Coast Guard from the Philippine Navy to the Department of Transportation and Communications to enable it to concentrate on its functions of marine safety administration, marine environmental protection, marine search and rescue and maritime law enforcement. It is expected that said transfer would result in a unified and integrated approach to maritime safety and water transport development.

name: HBN-00567
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL CELLULAR AND WIRELESS TELEPHONE PROGRAM
congress_website_abstract: \"National Cellular and Wireless Act of 2004\". The bill directs the Department of Transportation and Communications (DOTC) to implement a five-year (5 year) National Cellular and Wireless Telephone Program using a combination of government and private investments in an environment of free competition and mandatory interconnection. It further mandates that all landline telephone programs of the government be accordingly revised and contracts under negotiation be programmed and redirected towards the enforcement of the program.

name: HBN-00568
congress_website_title: AN ACT FIXING THE DATE OF REGULAR ELECTION FOR ELECTIVE OFFICIALS OF THE AUTONOMOUS REGION IN MUSLIM MINDANAO PURSUANT TO REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9054, ENTITLED 'AN ACT TO STRENGTHEN AND EXPAND THE ORGANIC ACT FOR THE AUTONOMOUS REGION IN MUSLIM MINDANAO, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6734, ENTITLED 'AN ACT PROVIDING FOR AN ORGANIC ACT FOR THE AUTONOMOUS REGION IN MUSLIM MINDANAO', AS AMENDED

name: HBN-00569
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE GENERAL GUIDELINES FOR THE PHILIPPINE HALAL SYSTEM, CREATING FOR THE PURPOSE THE PHILIPPINE HALAL EXECUTIVE COUNCIL AND CONVERTING THE SAME INTO A BOARD AFTER THE TRANSITION PERIOD, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Halal Act of 2004\". The bill defines the term \"Halal\" as \"lawful,\" as pertaining to food or products permitted for human consumption under the Shariah Law. It establishes the general guidelines on the preparation, handling and verification of Halal products such as food, beverage, cosmetics, garments and textile which serve as a basic requirement for certification. It also includes the accreditation of abattoirs and outlets and other service establishments.

congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE GOA-NAPAWON-SAN ROQUE-DAHAT-LAGONOY ROAD IN THE 3RD CONGRESSIONAL DISTRICT, PROVINCE OF CAMARINES SUR FROM A PROVINCIAL ROAD TO A NATIONAL SECONDARY ROAD AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00593
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING TAX INCENTIVES TO BROADCAST AND PRINT MEDIA DEVOTING AIR TIME AND SPACE TO EDUCATION, AS WELL AS, TO SPONSORS SUPPORTING SUCH PROGRAMS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to grant tax incentives to educational programs and publications, as well as to their respective advertisers and sponsors, with educational thrust. It recognizes that media is an effective alternative vehicle in educating the populace, thus every Filipino should be extended such services beyond the classroom to their homes. It is also hoped that this bill will alleviate the economic plight of the media industry as a whole and become an effective tool in the success of the educational program.

name: HBN-00594
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING THE MUNICIPALITY OF DONSOL, PROVINCE OF SORSOGON AS THE WHALE SHARK CAPITAL OF THE PHILIPPINES

name: HBN-00595
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXEMPTING FROM TAXATION ALL DONATIONS, GRANTS OR GIFTS TO NON-STOCK AND NON-PROFIT EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS
congress_website_abstract: It grants FULL TAX EXEMPTION and TAX CREDITS to non-stock and non-profit educational institutions that actively support the educational institutions and programs of the government.

name: HBN-00596
congress_website_title: AN ACT REGULATING THE PRACTICE OF PLUMBING ENGINEERING IN THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: \"Philippine Plumbing Engineering Act.\" The bill updates the Plumbing Law of 1955 which regulates the trade of master plumber and confers licenses to high school graduates with five (5) years plumbing experience. With the advent of high-rise buildings, the demand has risen for master plumbers with engineering expertise. The bill seeks to elevate the plumbing trade to the level of professional engineering and to regulate the practice of the same.

name: HBN-00597
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE UNIVERSITY OF THE PHILIPPINES AS THE NATIONAL UNIVERSITY
congress_website_abstract: \"The University of the Philippines Charter\". The measure strengthens the University of the Philippines by declaring it a policy of the State to promote, foster, nurture and protect the right of all citizens to quality education.

name: HBN-00598
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING AUTOMATIC PROMOTION OF GOVERNMENT OFFICIALS AND EMPLOYEES UPON RETIREMENT FROM GOVERNMENT SERVICE AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill aims to give due recognition to government officials and employees who have been in the government service for more than twenty (20) years despite their meager salaries, by promoting them one grade higher at the time of their retirement. The adjusted salary grade level shall be used as the basis in computing his retirement pay.

name: HBN-00599
congress_website_title: AN ACT PENALIZING INFLUENCE PEDDLING AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Influence Peddling Act.\" The bill defines influence peddling as an act of representing oneself, either orally or in writing, as being able, whether real or imagined, to influence, facilitate or assist another person having some business, transaction, application, request or contract with the government in which a public official or employee has to intervene. It seeks to penalize any person who engages in influence peddling.

name: HBN-00600
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOPMENT AND EDUCATION CENTERS FOR CHILDREN AGED ZERO (0)TO FIVE (5)YEARS, CREATING THE IMPLEMENTING MACHINERY THEREOF, PROVIDING GUIDELINES, INCENTIVES, GOVERNMENT FINANCIAL SUPPORT AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Early Childhood Development and Education Act.\" The bill seeks to establish Early Childhood Development Centers which will develop a program of activities that will focus primarily on the total development of children with their first language as their medium of instruction; provide the parents with training programs which focuses endeavor to inculcate responsble parenthood and provide guidance in child rearing, health, nutrition and education.

name: HBN-00601
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING THE SCIENTIFIC CAREER SYSTEM
congress_website_abstract: The measure reintroduces the Scientific Career System (SCS), a special career system that applies to permanent scientific personnel in the government with masteral and doctoral degrees in natural sciences, engineering and technology, medical sciences and agricultural sciences directly involved in research and development, laboratory works and field research. The SCS adheres to the rank concept in public service.

name: HBN-00602
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO GRANT MONTHLY PENSION TO GOVERNMENT RETIREES UNDER REPUBLIC ACT NO. 1616 WHO HAVE REACHED THE AGE OF SEVENTY-FOUR (74), APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill directs the Government Service Insurance Service to determine the monthly pension of a 74-year old retiree and to draw the guidelines in the disbursement of funds for the beneficiaries.

name: HBN-00603
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING INCENTIVES TO PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS WHO ARE ASSIGNED OUTSIDE OF THEIR TOWNS AND PROVINCES, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Public School Teachers Incentive Act\". Grants PhP1,000.00 monthly incentive allowance to teachers assigned to another town other than his own within the same province. It also grants teachers assigned to a town in another province other than his own a PhP2,000.00 monthly incentive allowance.

name: HBN-00604
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL STRATEGIC MISSING CHILDREN RECOVERY PROGRAM, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Strategic Missing Children Recovery Program Act\". Creates a sixth sectoral panel (Sectoral Panel on Missing Children) under the Council for the Welfare of Children. The Council shall operate a missing child recovery program which shall develop effective models in locating missing children, effective methods of promoting community awareness on the problem, and programs for counseling and treating recovered children and their families.

name: HBN-00605
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE FILIPINO ACADEMY OF ARTS, LETTERS AND PHILOSOPHY, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Filipino Academy of Arts, Letters and Philosophy Act\". Organizes the country's artists, writers and philosophers into a community receiving State support but free from government interference. The Academy shall plan, formulate and implement programs for the conservation, development and propagation of Filipino arts, letters and philosophy.

name: HBN-00606
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE PAYMENT OF RETIREMENT BENEFITS TO GOVERNMENT RETIREES ON THE DATE OF THEIR RETIREMENT
congress_website_abstract: The bill requires all government agencies to process the papers of a retiree three months before retirement to ensure payment of benefits on the day he retires.

name: HBN-00607
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING ANY PERSON, ASSOCIATION, CORPORATION, OR ENTITY TO SECURE THE NECESSARY CLEARANCE OR PERMIT FROM THE NATIONAL HISTORICAL INSTITUTE (NHI) BEFORE COMMENCING ANY CONSTRUCTION OF REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT PROJECTS IN AREAS IDENTIFIED, DESIGNATED AND APPROPRIATELY MARKED AS HISTORICAL PLACES OR IN AREAS PROXIMATE THERETO, PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATION THEREOF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill prohibits persons or firms from constructing projects or real estate development in historical places without a permit from the National Historical Institute. It provides stiff penalties for non-compliance with the permit requirement.

name: HBN-00608
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING PUBLIC OFFICIALS AND EMPLOYEES TO SUBMIT A WRITTEN PERMISSION OR WAIVER IN FAVOR OF THE OMBUDSMAN TO LOOK INTO ALL DEPOSITS OF WHATEVER NATURE WITH BANKS OR BANKING INSTITUTIONS BOTH WITHIN AND OUTSIDE THE PHILIPPINES INCLUDING INVESTMENT BONDS ISSUED BY THE GOVERNMENT OF THE PHILIPPINES, ITS POLITICAL SUBDIVISIONS AND INSTRUMENTALITIES AND PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The measure aims to avoid graft and corruption by requiring all public officials and employees to allow the Ombudsman to look into their bank deposits and bonds issued by the government. A written permission or waiver shall be required from the employee upon assumption of office.

name: HBN-00609
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING FIVE HUNDRED PESOS (P500.00) MONTHLY ALLOWANCE TO ALL BARANGAY TANODS AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Provides incentives to barangay tanods by giving them a PhP500.00 monthly allowance. A maximum of 10 barangay tanods in every barangay shall be covered by the incentive.

name: HBN-00610
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING DIFFICULTY DIFFERENTIAL TO GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The measure grants incentives to government workers in remote areas by giving them difficulty differential equivalent to 10% of their basic pay.

name: HBN-00611
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 13-A OF THE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8291 (GSIS ACT OF 1997) BY PRESCRIBING OTHER CONDITIONS FOR ENTITLEMENT TO THE RETIREMENT BENEFITS, UNDER SECTION 13 OF THE SAME ACT, FOR GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES OR OFFICIALS WHO RETIRES OR HAVE PREVIOUSLY RETIRED AS A RESULT OF BEING PHASED OUT AND/OR NOT RETAINED BY HIS OFFICE/BUREAU/CORPORATION/PRIVATIZING/ABOLISHING HIS OFFICE
congress_website_abstract: The bill provides monthly old-age pension to retirees with 20 years of service to commence after five years from the date of retirement.

name: HBN-00612
congress_website_title: AN ACT REVERTING TO THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT THE DISCHARGE OF BASIC AGRICULTURAL FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES DEVOLED TO LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS AND AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The bill reverts to the national government the following devolved functions: dispersal of livestock and poultry, fingerlings and seedlings aquaculture, agricultural extension and on-site research services. Accordingly, it reverts to the national government all personnel previously devolved to the local government units.

name: HBN-00613
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING AND VESTING THE BUREAU OF FOOD AND DRUGS WITH QUASI-JUDICIAL POWERS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES AMENDING CERTAIN SECTIONS OF REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED THIRTY-SEVEN HUNDRED AND TWENTY, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 'FOODS, DRUGS AND DEVICES, AND COSMETIC ACT'
congress_website_abstract: The bill imposes a P50,000 fine for violation resulting in death; suspends, closes or revokes license to operate when public interest requires. Allows the Bureau of Food and Drugs to inspect, investigate or seize adulterated food, drugs, or cosmetics or counterfeited, misbranded or registered.

name: HBN-00614
congress_website_title: AN ACT FURTHER AMENDING CERTAIN SECTIONS OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1177, AS AMENDED, IN ORDER TO INSTITUTE LINE-ITEM BUDGETING WITH RESPECT TO THE APPROPRIATION FOR PERSONAL SERVICES
congress_website_abstract: The bill requires every agency of the government to itemize personal services according to position title, number of employee and basic salary.

name: HBN-00615
congress_website_title: AN ACT LOWERING THE RETIREMENT AGE OF GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED EIGHTY-TWO HUNDRED AND NINETY-ONE, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED GSIS ACT OF 1997
congress_website_abstract: The measure lowers the compulsory retirement age from 65 to 60 years old and the optional retirement age from 60 to 55 years old.

name: HBN-00616
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING THE NATIONAL ARCHIVES OF THE PHILIPPINES, DEFINING ITS POWERS, FUNCTIONS AND DUTIES, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Archives Act\". Creates the National Archives of the Philippines to store, conserve and make available to the public historical documents.

name: HBN-00617
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING AN EDUCATION TRUST FUND FOR GRANTEES OF GSIS AND SSS MEMBERS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Education Trust Fund Act\". The bill defines the fund as the accumulated money deducted from GSIS and the SSS members and paid to the system for the education of their respective grantees, then paid to colleges or universities for a vocational, technical or baccalaureate program as provided in the policy contract.

name: HBN-00618
congress_website_title: AN ACT REVERTING TO THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT THE DISCHARGE OF BASIC HEALTH SERVICES DEVOLVED TO LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS (LGUs) AND AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: \"Re-Nationalization of Devolved Health Services and Facilities Act\". Aims to correct the flaws of decentralization such as non-implementation of the Magna Carta for Health Workers and delays or non-payment of their salaries, by reverting to the national government the devolved functions.

name: HBN-00619
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING TO RESIDENTS OF A REGION WHERE A GEOTHERMAL ENERGY PLANT IS LOCATED A THIRTY PERCENT (30%) DISCOUNT ON THEIR POWER RATE CONSUMPTION
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to compensate residents for pollution caused by geothermal plants located in their region by giving them a 30% discount on their electric bill.

name: HBN-00620
congress_website_title: AN ACT UPGRADING THE MINIMUM SALARY GRADE OF PUBLIC SCHOOLS DISTRICT SUPERVISORS (PSDS) AND EDUCATION SUPERVISORS-1 (ES1), AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE 'COMPENSATION AND POSITION CLASSIFICATION ACT OF 1989'
congress_website_abstract: The measure upgrades the salary grade of public school district supervisors and education supervisors-1 from grade 19 to 21.

name: HBN-00621
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTING REFORMS IN LAND ADMINISTRATION
congress_website_abstract: \"Land Administration Reform Act of 2004\". Creates the Land Administration Authority under the Office of the President to conduct, integrate, regulate geodetic and geophysical surveys, land classification, mapping, aerial photography, etc. and/or dispose alienable and disposable lands under Commonwealth Act No. 141 or the Public Land Act.

name: HBN-00622
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTING A MARITIME CODE, THEREBY REVISING AND CONSOLIDATING ADMIRALTY AND MARITIME LAWS TO MODERNIZE THE MARITIME INDUSTRY, PROMOTE AND REGULATE MERCHANT MARINE ACTIVITIES, RATIONALIZE THE ORGANIZATION AND FUNCTIONS OF GOVERNMENT AGENCIES RELATED TO ADMIRALTY AND MARITIME MATTERS, AND INSURE THE PROTECTION OF THE MARINE WEALTH IN THE PHILIPPINE TERRITORY
congress_website_abstract: \"Maritime Code of the Philippines\". Seeks to solve the following: obsolete laws unresponsive to the industry; failure of the Coast Guard to inspect vessels and their compliance with communications regulations; absence of admiralty courts; acute need for search and rescue vessels; and frequent sea disasters and tragedies

name: HBN-00623
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A BARANGAY HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY BUENAVISTA, MUNICIPALITY OF CASTILLA, PROVINCE OF SORSOGON, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00634
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING DISCRIMINATION ON THE BASIS OF SEXUAL ORIENTATION AND GENDER IDENTITY AND PROVIDING PENALTIES THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Discrimination Act\". The bill cites the need to correct discrimination against lesbian and gays. It defines discriminating practices and provides penalties for violations.

name: HBN-00635
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING MEDICAL ETHICS BY REPEALING PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 169, AS AMENDED BY EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 212, WHICH REQUIRES ANY ATTENDING PHYSICIAN OF ANY HOSPITAL, MEDICAL CLINIC, SANITARIUM OR OTHER MEDICAL ESTABLISHMENTS OR ANY MEDICAL PRACTITIONER WHO HAS TREATED ANY PERSON FOR SERIOUS OR LESS SERIOUS PHYSICAL INJURIES TO REPORT THE FACT OF TREATMENT
congress_website_abstract: The bill repeals PD 169 claiming it is a martial law edict that violates the confidentiality of patient-doctor relationship.

name: HBN-00636
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR REASONABLE COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS FOR THE LOSS OF LIVES, INJURIES AND DAMAGES TO PROPERTY SUFFERED BY NON-COMBATANT INDIVIDUALS IN THE COURSE OF OPERATIONS BY THE MILITARY, POLICE AND/OR OTHER LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill compensates non-combatant victims of military and police operations, as follows:a) if the victim dies - PhP50,000.00 to his legal heirs; b) for physical, emotional and psychological injury - cost of hospitalization; and c) for loss or damage to property - assessed value of the lost or destroyed property.

name: HBN-00637
congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING RESPECT FOR AND FOSTERING HUMAN RIGHTS IN THE SYSTEM OF FORMAL AND INFORMAL EDUCATION IN THE PHILIPPINES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill directs the Department of Education and the Commission on Higher Education to include the study of human rights in the curricula of all levels of education in all schools in the country.

name: HBN-00638
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED 7309, ENTITLED \"AN ACT CREATING A BOARD OF CLAIMS UNDER THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE FOR VICTIMS OF UNJUST IMPRISONMENT OR DETENTION AND VICTIMS OF VIOLENT CRIMES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES\"
congress_website_abstract: The bill increases the payment to victims of unjust imprisonment from P1,000 to P7,500 monthly while the maximum claim for expenses incurred is raised from P10,000 to P75,000.

name: HBN-00639
congress_website_title: AN ACT GOVERNING THE ESTABLISHMENT, OPERATION AND REGULATION OF LENDING COMPANIES
congress_website_abstract: \"Lending Company Regulation Act of 2004\". The bill requires lending companies to have a paid-up capital of one million pesos, be registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission, and to obtain a quasi-banking license from the Central Bank.

name: HBN-00640
congress_website_title: AN ACT CRIMINALIZING VIDEO AND PHOTO VOYEURISM AND IMPOSING PENALTIES THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Voyeurism Act of 2004\". Imposes imprisonment from 18 months to 4 years and/or a fine of P200,000 to those who invade the privacy of others using a camera or a video camera.

name: HBN-00641
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 223 OF ACT NO. 3815, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED PENAL CODE
congress_website_abstract: The bill increases the penalty imposed on jail guards and public officers for allowing the escape of prisoners in their custody from \"prision correctional\" to \"reclusion perpetua\" and permanent absolute disqualification.

MARAVILLA, SR. NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00646
congress_website_title: AN ACT LOWERING THE RETIREMENT AGE FOR PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS FROM SIXTY (60)TO FIFTY-FIVE (55) YEARS OLD AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: Retires a 55-year old public school teacher who has served for 20 years with all the retirement benefits granted under existing laws.

name: HBN-00647
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE WELFARE OF BARANGAY TANODS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 393 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: \"Barangay Tanod Welfare Act\". The bill extends to barangay tanods the following benefits granted under RA 7160: a) Christmas bonus of at least P1,000; b) insurance coverage in accordance with RA 6942; c) free medical care; d) free tuition fees for legitimate children in state colleges and universities; and e) appropriate civil service on the basis of the number of years of sevice in the barangay. The above benefits shall be extended to a maximum of 20 barangay tanods in every barangay.

name: HBN-00648
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING GRATUITY PAY TO ALL DULY ELECTED BARANGAY CAPTAINS AND BARANGAY COUNCIL MEMBERS, WHO HAVE COMPLETELY SERVED THREE CONSECUTIVE TERMS, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREOF AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Barangay Council Gratuity Act of 2004\". Grants gratuity pay of P200,000 to barangay captains and P150,000 to council members who have served three consecutive terms.

name: HBN-00649
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING A SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE AND FREE PORT IN THE CITY OF BACOLOD CREATING FOR THIS PURPOSE THE BACOLOD CITY SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE AUTHORITY, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00650
congress_website_title: AN ACT FURTHER STRENGHTENING THE HOME DEVELOPMENT MUTUAL FUND AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2004\". Proposes the following amendments to PD 1752: 1. Restate the Fund's tax exemptions, 2. Expand the Board of Trustees, 3. Exempt the Fund from the Salary Standardization Law, and 4. Strengthen the enforcement power of the Fund to sanction delinquent borrowers.

name: HBN-00651
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING SECTION 106 (A) (1) (a) AND SECTION 108 (A) OF PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 1158, AS AMENDED BY REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8424, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE NATIONAL INTERNAL REVENUE CODE
congress_website_abstract: The bill reduces the value-added tax rate from 10% to 2% on the sale or lease of real properties.

name: HBN-00652
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE FIELD OF LAND TITLE INSURANCE AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NO. 612 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE INSURANCE CODE OF 1978 AS AMENDED BY PRESIDENTIAL DECREES NO. 1455 AND 1460, AND BATAS PAMBANSA BLG. 874
congress_website_abstract: The bill creates the field of \"title insurance\" to protect land buyers from fake or forged titles, reimburse them on fraudulent transactions and save the integrity of the Torrens Titling System from attack by crime syndicates.

name: HBN-00653
congress_website_title: AN ACT DEFINING AS A CRIME THE ACT OF DRIVING ANY MOTOR VEHICLE WHILE UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGES AND/OR PROHIBITED DRUGS AND PROVIDING GRADUATED PENALTIES THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Drunk Driving Act of 2004.\" Declares it unlawful for any driver to operate a motor vehicle if he has a blood alcohol concentration level of 6% and/or is under the influence of a prohibited drug.
