congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE TARLAC CITY-NUEVA ECIJA VIA VICTORIA ROAD, PROVINCE OF TARLAC INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00830
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE PANIQUI-RAMOS ROAD IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF PANIQUI AND RAMOS, PROVINCE OF TARLAC INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00831
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE SAN JOSE-MAYANTOC ROAD, PROVINCE OF TARLAC INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00832
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY MALACAMPA, MUNICIPALITY OF CAMILING, PROVINCE OF TARLAC, TO BE KNOWN AS THE MALACAMPA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00833
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING THE PUBLIC DISPLAY OF PERSONS ARRESTED, ACCUSED, OR UNDER CUSTODIAL INVESTIGATION IN A DEGRADING MANNER, AND AMENDING FOR SUCH PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7438, ENTITLED \"AN ACT DEFINING CERTAIN RIGHTS OF PERSONS ARRESTED, DETAINED OR UNDER CUSTODIAL INVESTIGATION AS WELL AS THE DUTIES OF THE ARRESTING, DETAINING, AND INVESTIGATING OFFICERS, AND PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: The bill declares that NO PERSON ARRESTED, DETAINED OR UNDER CUSTODIAL INVESTIGATION SHALL BE PUT ON PUBLIC DISPLAY IN HAND CUFFS, WITH NAME TAGS OR OTHER TAGS, OR IN ANY MANNER CAUSING OR TENDING TO CAUSE DEGRADATION, HUMILIATION, DISHONOR, DISCREDIT, RIDICULE OR CONTEMPT, NOR SHALL SUCH PERSON BE PRESENTED TO THE PRESS IN SUCH MANNER. SUBJECT TO THE PRECEDING RESTRICTIONS, THE PUBLIC DISPLAY OR PRESENTATION TO THE PRESS OF ANY PERSON ARRESTED, ACCUSED, DETAINED, OR UNDER CUSTODIAL INVESTIGATION SHALL ONLY BE ALLOWED IF SUCH PERSON IS ASSISTED BY COUNSEL OF HIS CHOICE DURING THE PUBLIC DISPLAY OR PRESENTATION.

name: HBN-00834
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY CALIPAYAN, MUNICIPALITY OF SANTA IGNACIA, PROVINCE OF TARLAC, TO BE KNOWN AS THE CALIPAYAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-00835
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING A TWENTY-FIVE PERCENT (25%) DUSCOUNT ON TRANSPORTATION FARE FOR THE OFFICIALS OF THE SANGGUNIANG KABATAAN
congress_website_abstract: The bill explains that in the local level, the youth rely on their elected Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) officials to represent their interests, particularly in the local sanggunians. Young as they are, the SKs perform their tasks with unwavering idealism, vigor and dedication. The other reality, however, is that most SKs are also students and unemployed. The honoraria and other allowances that they receive from the local governments are barely adequate to support the various programs and activities they they undertake.

name: HBN-00836
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF TECHNICAL-INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SCHOOLS IN ALL THE REGIONS OF THE PHILIPPINES, AND APPRORPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The bill provides high school graduates with a better and affordable option in the matter of technical education. With the establishment of technical and information technology schools in all regions in the country that offer free and affordable technological education, the country will be ensured of a continuous generation of highly-trained graduates who are at par with the best in the world.

name: HBN-00837
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE NUMBER OF PROVINCIAL PROSECUTORS IN THE PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA AND CITY PROSECUTORS IN THE CITY OF CABANATUAN, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE PRESIDENTIAL DECREE NUMBERED TWELVE HUNDRED AND SEVENTI-FIVE, AS AMENDED, ENTITLED \"REORGANIZING THE PROSECUTION STAFF OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE AND THE OFFICES OF THE PROVINCIAL AND CITY FISCALS, REGIONALIZING THE PROSECUTION SERVICE AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00838
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY VEGA, MUNICIPALITY OF BONGABON, PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA, TO BE KNOWN AS VEGA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPRORIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00839
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY POLILIO, CABANATUAN CITY, NUEVA ECIJA, TO BE KNOWN AS THE POLILIO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00840
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY ANTIPOLO, MUNICIPALITY OF BONGABON, PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA, TO BE KNOWN AS ANTIPOLO NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00841
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE TEACHING OF ECOLOGY IN ALL ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY LEVELS, WHETHER PUBLIC OR PRIVATE OF THE PHILIPPINES, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The inclusion of ecology in the curriculum of elementary and secondary levels aims to inculcate in the youth ecological awareness and vigilance and make them understand more the dynamic relationships which support and maintain the bio-system.

name: HBN-00842
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A NATIONAL BAMBOO AND RATTAN DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: \"Integrated National Bamboo and Rattan Development Program.\" The Program shall cover the following: (1) Propagation or production of high quality, genetically superior planting stocks through sexual and macro and micro-sexual means; (2) Dispersal or distribution of planting materials at a nominal amount; (3) Production or bamboo farming and/or plantation development and management; (4) Utilization, processing and marketing including high value product development; (5) Training and extension, including technology packaging and transfer; and (6) Research and technology.

name: HBN-00843
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE THE AUTHORITY TO SETTLE MINOR CRIMINAL OFFENSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill authorizes to the Department of Justice (DOJ) through the Office of the Chief State Prosecutor, to bring together to an extra-judicial settlement the parties to a criminal action involving minor offenses. Minor offenses are offenses punishable by imprisonment not exceeding one (1) year and/or a fine not exceeding Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00).

name: HBN-00844
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO HELP INSURE THE SAFETY OF BANK DEPOSITS AND FUNDS THEREBY STRENGTHENING THE OPERATIONS OF BANKING INSTITUTIONS AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill helps in ensuring the safety of bank deposits and funds thereby strengthening the operations of banking institutions. It authorizes all banking institutions recognized and accredited by the Banko Sentral ng Pilipinas to provide special security services of their banking activities and operations and to procure and possess firearms for security purposes.

name: HBN-00845
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH TO PROVIDE AT LEAST ONE (1) DENTIST IN EVERY MUNICIPALITY OF EVERY PROVINCE, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The bill aims to provide one (1) dentist in every municipality of every province under the control and supervision of the Department of Health. Directs the Department of Health to issue rules and regulations for the effective implementation of this Act.

name: HBN-00846
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING SUBSIDY TO PRIVATE HOSPITALS AND CLINICS WHICH ADMIT FOR CONFINEMENT AND TREATMENT INDIGENT PATIENTS UNDER EMERGENCY CASES AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: This bill grants subsidy from the government for the hospital expenses of indigent patients admitted and confined in private hospitals and clinics under emergency cases. It further requires all persons who wish to avail of the financial assistance granted under this Act to apply for an indigent identification card with the Department of Social Welfare and Development. Directs hospitals concerned to transmit monthly billings to the Department of Health of the total emergency admission fees extended to emergency patients.

name: HBN-00847
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ARTICLE 63, TITLE II OF EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 209 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE FAMILY CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES
congress_website_abstract: This bill amends the present laws on marriage to allow the innocent spouse, who has been granted a decree of legal separation, to initiate the necessary petition before the proper courts to finally terminate their marriage and to re-marry, after a lapse of five (5) years from the date of issuance of the decree. Requires the petitioner during the hearing to properly establish the following elements: (1) That the petitioner is the innocent spouse; (2) That five years have elapsed from the time the decree of legal separation became final and executory; and (3) Reconciliation between the spouses is no longer possible.

name: HBN-00848
congress_website_title: AN ACT MANDATING THE DESIGNATION OF AT LEAST ONE TOURISM OFFICER IN EVERY MUNICIPALITY AND CITY AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: This bill aims to provide one (1) Tourism Officer in every municipality and city who would promote the economic, social and cultural development of their respective areas through income generating tourism promotions and development programs and projects. Prioritizes municipalities or cities where tourism is considered a major local industry.

name: HBN-00849
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE EXISTING MILITARY AIRPORT IN FORT MAGSAYSAY, CITY OF PALAYAN, PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA, INTO A DOMESTIC AIRPORT, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00850
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE MAPALAD, STA. ROSA-FORT MAGSAYSAY, PALAYAN CITY ROAD IN THE PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA, INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00851
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE GENERAL NATIVIDAD-CABANATUAN CITY ROAD IN THE PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00852
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A FIFTY-BED EXTENSION PROVINCIAL HOSPITAL IN PALAYAN CITY, PROVINCE OF NUEVA ECIJA, TO BE KNOWN AS THE PAULINO J. GARCIA MEMORIAL RESEARCH AND MEDICAL CENTER (PJGMRMC, PALAYAN CITY EXTENSION, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-00853
congress_website_title: AN ACT MAKING THE POSITION OF THE MUNICIPAL AGRICULTURIST MANDATORY, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 482 OF REPUBLIC ACT 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE \"LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991\"
congress_website_abstract: The bill mandates the appointment of a MUNICIPAL AGRICULTURIST so that the agricultural municipal government is assured that it can rely on the expertise of an agriculturist at all times.

name: HBN-00854

name: HBN-00855
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A CONGRESSIONAL DISTRICT OFFICE IN EVERY POLITICAL DISTRICT IN THE PHILIPPINES, AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Rationale: The Congressional Representative, as distinguished from those from the party0list, represents a definite political subdivision, a District. He is a local leader, inasmuch as he represents the interests of his constituents in the halls of Congress and before departments and agencies the government. However, he is also a national stakeholder and in performing his duties, he is not limited to promoting only parochial concerns but also issues and causes that affect other localities or the entire country as well. With all these concerns on his burden, it is not suprising that the Congressman is, perhaps, the most visited public official, especially when he is in his district, thus this bill.

congress_website_abstract: This bill amends some provisions in P.D. No. 442, as follows: Composition of the NLRC from fourteen (14) to TWENTY-THREE (23) members; (2) The Commission may sit en banc or in EIGHT (8) divisions; (3) THE COMMISSION AND ITS EIGHT (8) DIVISIONS SHALL BE ASSISTED BY THE COMMISSION ATTORNEY'S IN ITS APPELLATE AND ADJUDICATORY FUNCTIONS WHOSE TERM SHALL BE CO-TERMINUS WITH THE COMMISSIONERS WITH WHOM THEY ARE ASSIGNED. congress_website_abstract: \"The University of the Philippines Charter of 2004.\" The bill aims to strengthen the University of the Philippines by declaring it the national university of the State. As the national universityof the State, it shall: (a) set standards and initiate innovations in teaching, research and faculty development; (b) serve as a graduate university; (c) serve as a research university; (d) provide various forms of community, public and volunteer service, and scholarly and technical assistance; (e) provide opportunities for training and learning in progressive leadership; and (f) serve as a regional and global university.

It creates the Bio-Organic Farming Authority to direct the program.

congress_website_abstract: \"Barangay Official Retirement Benefits Act.\" Grants one month salary for every year of service on P25,000, whichever is higher, to barangay officials who have served for 15 years.

name: HBN-00999
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHT TO SECURITY OF TENURE
congress_website_abstract: \"Security of Tenure Act of 2004.\" Considers it as unfair labor practice to classify as casual, contractual, subcontractor, or labor-only contracting, employees who are regular by virtue of their activities.

name: HBN-01000
congress_website_title: AN ACT UPGRADING THE MINIMUM SALARY GRADE LEVEL OF PUBLIC SCHOOL TEACHERS IN ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY LEVELS FROM GRADE 10 TO GRADE 18 AND AUTHORIZING THE APPROPRIATION OF FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Claims that teachers are overworked and underpaid and half of their time they sell goodies to survive. The bill seeks to help them by upgrading their salary from Grade 10 to 18.

name: HBN-01001
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO REGULATE THE SALE, PURCHASE AND POSSESSION OF TOXIC AND HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCES, INCLUDING SODIUM CYANIDE PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATION THEREOF, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Toxic and Hazardous Substances Control of 2004.\" To preserve our aquamarine resources and ensure our food supply, the bill seeks to regulate the sale/purchase, possession, use, transport, storage, transfer and disposal of toxic substances by requiring a permit from the Philippine National Police and the Department of the Interior and Local Government.

name: HBN-01002
congress_website_title: AN ACT UPGRADING THE SALARY GRADE LEVEL OF THE SCHOOLS DISTRICT SUPERVISORS FROM SALARY GRADE NINETEEN TO SALARY GRADE TWENTY-FOUR
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to correct the inequity in the present compensation scheme whereby Schools District Supervisors receive lower salaries than their underlings, the School Heads or Principals. It proposes to upgrade the salaries of District Supervisors from salary grade 19 to grade 24.

name: HBN-01003
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING A NATIONAL PROGRAM FOR FILIPINO GIFTED CHILDREN AND SCIENCE-ORIENTED YOUTH, PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

It proposes the creation of the Center for the Gifted Children and Science-Oriented Youth which shall identify and place them in appropriate educational institutions and assist them in securing jobs.

name: HBN-01004
congress_website_title: AN ACT STREAMLINING AND STRENGTHENING THE PHILIPPINE DISASTER PREPAREDNESS AND PREVENTION CAPABILITY, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

It provides a mechanism for the allocation, release and monitoring of the national calamity fund.

name: HBN-01005
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO PROMOTE AND ENHANCE PUBLIC SAFETY BY PROHIBITING CRANK CALLS TO ALL EMERGENCY HOTLINES AND PROVIDING PENALTY THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Anti-Crank Calls Act.\" Intended to protect existing emergency hotlines from illegitimate calls, the bill imposes a fine of PhP5,000.00; imprisonment of 6 months and severance of of the caller's telephone line and/or blockage of his mobile line for all crank, sex, and unnecessary calls and other forms of illegitimate calls. In case a crank caller uses a pay phone, the punishment shall be one year imprisonment.

name: HBN-01006
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE TEACHING OF ECOLOGY IN ALL ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY LEVELS, WHETHER PUBLIC OR PRIVATE, ALL THROUGHOUT THE COUNTRY AND PROVIDING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: To equip out citizenry on the ways to deal with environmental problems, the bill requires all elementary and secondary schools, private and public, to teach ecology.

name: HBN-01007
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT 9161 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS AN ACT ESTABLISHING REFORMS IN THE REGULATION OF RENTALS OF CERTAIN RESIDENTIAL UNITS, PROVIDING THE MECHANISMS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The measure raises the ceiling of the rental rates of residential units in the National Capital Region and other highly urbanized cities to PhP9,000.00 per month and in other areas to PhP6,000.00 per month beginning January 1, 2005 for a period of three years ending on December 31, 2007. It also mandates lessors not to increase rentals by more than 10% to be computed on a cumulative basis.

name: HBN-01008
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING EMPLOYERS TO INFORM THEIR EMPLOYEES OF THEIR RIGHTS TO SOCIAL WELFARE BENEFITS, COLLECTIVE BARGAINING AGREEMENTS OR COMPANY POLICIES UNDER EXISTING LAWS IN WRITING ON THE FIRST DAY OF THEIR EMPLOYMENT AND EVERY TIME A PARTICULAR CONTINGENCY OCCURS
congress_website_abstract: More often than not, employees fail to avail themselves of the benefits due them under the laws because of ignorance. The bill directs every employer to inform his employee, in writing on the first day of his employment and every time a compensable contingency occurs, of the benefits the latter may avail of. The employee should also be informed of the collective bargaining agreements, company policies and practices and employee contracts arising from the employer-employee relationship.

name: HBN-01009
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE SOUTHERN BUKIDNON ARTS AND TRADE SCHOOL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF DON CARLOS, PROVINCE OF BUKIDNON

congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE BUKIDNON NATIONAL SCHOOL OF HOME INDUSTRIES FROM SECONDARY SCHOOL TO A COLLEGE TO BE KNOWN AS BUKIDNON POLYTECHNIC COLLEGE

name: HBN-01032
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX IN BARANGAY BALIDE, MUNICIPALITY OF AURORA, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA DEL SUR FROM THE COMMONWEALTH NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY COMMONWEALTH, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE AURORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-01033
congress_website_title: AN ACT INCREASING THE INTERNAL REVENUE ALLOTMENT FOR LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNITS AND LIKEWISE INCREASING THE ALLOCATION FOR BARANGAYS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7160, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE LOCAL GOVERNMENT CODE OF 1991
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes to increase the share of local government units in the internal revenue allotment (IRA) from 40% to 45% starting in FY 2007. The proposed 5% increase shall be allocated to barangays in addition to the 20% allocation provided under Section 285 of the Local Government Code.

name: HBN-01034
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING THE ESTABLISHMENT OF PROVINCIAL, CITY, MUNICIPAL AND BARANGAY LIBRARIES AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Libraries constitute the cheapest source of knowledge and information. The bill seeks the establishment of libraries in every local government unit (LGU). The funding for the establishment, operation and maintenance of libraries shall be taken from the 20% economic development fund of the LGU.

name: HBN-01035
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A DRUG REHABILITATION CENTER IN THE AURORA DISTRICT HOSPITAL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF AURORA, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA DEL SUR, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-01036
congress_website_title: AN ACT EXTENDING THE PERIOD OF VALIDITY ON THE PRIVILEGES FOR MULTIPLE ENTRY VISA AND REENTRY PERMIT OF FILIPINO VETERANS AND THEIR IMMEDIATE FAMILY MEMBERS, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE THE ACT GRANTING PERMANENT RESIDENT STATUS, OTHER RIGHTS AND PRIVILEGES TO FILIPINO VETERANS OF WORLD WAR II WHO ACQUIRED AMERICAN CITIZENSHIP UNDER THE UNITED STATES IMMIGRANT ACT OF 1990 AND ANY OTHER PRIOR ACTS FOR THESE PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes that the multiple entry visa and reentry permit granted to Filipino veterans who have acquired American citizenship and their immediate family members be extended until their application for permanent resident status in the Philippines has been approved. This will save them time, money and effort in having their visa or permit renewed.

name: HBN-01037
congress_website_title: AN ACT CREATING THE IFUGAO RICE TERRACES AUTHORITY, DEFINING ITS POWERS AND FUNCTIONS, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

name: HBN-01038
congress_website_title: AN ACT CHANGING THE NAME OF THE IFUGAO STATE COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURE AND FORESTRY TO SIMPLE THE IFUGAO STATE COLLEGE

congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE POTIA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL-ANNEX IN BARANGAY CARAGASAN, MUNICIPALITY OF ALFONSO LISTA, PROVINCE OF IFUGAO FROM POTIA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO A DISTINCT AND INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE CARAGASAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-01051
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONSTITUTING THE SITIOS OF LANGLANG, ABAGONG, BILOG, ITUJO, MALANGO, PANNEP, TOLPOC, TAPOYOC, BADANG AND MAGUITAWA, ALL WITHIN THE MUNICIPALITY OF AGUINALDO, PROVINCE OF IFUGAO INTO A DISTINCT AND INDEPENDENT BARANGAY TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY MAGUITAWA

name: HBN-01012
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING AN ELECTIVE OFFICIAL IPSO FACTO RESIGNED FROM HIS ELECTIVE OFFICE UPON THE FILING OF HIS CERTIFICATE OF CANDIDACY
congress_website_abstract: Section 67 of the Omnibus Election Code is deemed unfair in its treatment of candidates as it exempts candidates for the positions of President and Vice President from resigning from the public office they are currently holding. The bill seeks to do declare all elective officials running for any office other than one he is holding in a permanent capacity as ipso facto resigned from his office upon filing of the certificate of candidacy.

name: HBN-01013
congress_website_title: AN ACT REGULATING THE ESTABLISHMENT AND OPERATION OF HEALTH MAINTENANCE ORGANIZATIONS (HMOs), PROVIDING THEM INCENTIVES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Health Maintenance Organization Act of 2004.\" To make quality health care available through affordable health insurance policies, the bill grants HMOs incentives to encourage the private sector to participate in the government's program. It defines the regulatory framework for the operations of health maintenance organizations (HMOs). It delineates the fundamental rights of members and the health care providers.

name: HBN-01014
congress_website_title: AN ACT SEPARATING AND CONVERTING THE IFUGAO COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURE AND FORESTRY-IFUGAO COLLEGE OF ARTS AND TRADE [ISCAF-ICAT] LOCATED IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF LAGAWE, IFUGAO PROVINCE INTO A TESDA SUPERVISED SCHOOL IN COORDINATION WITH THE DEPARTMENT OF LABOR AND EMPLOYMENT (DOLE)

name: HBN-01015
congress_website_title: AN ACT IMPOSING STIFFER PENALTIES TO PERSONS CAUGHT DRIVING A MOTOR VEHICLE WHILE UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF ALCOHOL/LIQUOR AND/OR PROHIBITED DRUGS
congress_website_abstract: \"Deadly Driver Reduction Act of 2004.\" The bill imposes stiffer penalties for driving under the influence of liquor or prohibited drugs ranging from prision correccional in its minimum term and a fine of PhP20,000 to prision correccional in its maximum term and a fine of PhP80,000.

name: HBN-01016
congress_website_title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE LAGAWE-HINGYON PROVINCIAL ROAD TRAVERSING BARANGAYS POBLACION AND BURNAY, MUNICIPALITY OF LAGAWE, AND BARANGAYS UMALBONG, MOMPOLIA, BITU AND PIWONG, MUNICIPALITY OF HINGYON, PROVINCE OF IFUGAO INTO A NATIONAL ROAD AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-01017
congress_website_title: AN ACT ACCORDING EQUAL RIGHTS TO WIVES AND HUSBANDS IN THE PHILIPPINES BY AMENDING ARTICLES 333, 334 AND 344 OF ACT NUMBERED THREE THOUSAND EIGHT-HUNDRED AND FIFTEEN, AS AMENDED, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE REVISED PENAL CODE
congress_website_abstract: Articles 333 and 334 of the Revised Penal Code define the felonies of adultery and concubinage, respectively, Under the law, a wife in an adultery case incurs a heavier penalty than a husband in a concubinage case. This bill seeks to amend Articles 333 and 334 of the Revised Penal Code to attune it to the Constitutional mandate of fundamental equality of women and men before the law.

name: HBN-01018
congress_website_title: AN ACT GRANTING MATERNITY LEAVE BENEFITS TO UNMARRIED PREGNANT WOMEN WHO ARE IN GOVERNMENT SERVICE
congress_website_abstract: Unmarried pregnant women in the private sector enjoy maternity benefits under RA1161. This bill seeks to extend the same benefits to unmarried pregnant women in the government service by granting them maternity leave benefits equivalent to 60 days for normal delivery and 78 days in case of caesarian delivery.

name: HBN-01019
congress_website_title: AN ACT TO CREATE A NATIONAL WOMEN'S MUSEUM, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"National Filipino Women's Museum Act of 2003.\" The National Filipino Women's Museum shall be established to enshrine and commemorate the heritage, heroism and martyrdom of Filipino women who played significant roles in the country's struggle for liberation, justice, peace and prosperity.

name: HBN-01020
congress_website_title: AN ACT DECLARING NOVEMBER TWENTY-FIVE OF EVERY YEAR AS NATIONAL CONSCIOUSNESS DAY FOR THE ELIMINATION OF VIOLENCE AGAINST WOMEN
congress_website_abstract: The proposed nationwide observance of the National Consciousness Day for the Elimination of Violence Against Women in November 25 of every year aims to raise the public's awareness on the problem of violence and the elimination of all violence against women.

name: HBN-01021
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING TITLE FOUR OF BOOK FOUR OF THE LABOR CODE OF THE PHILIPPINES PROVIDING FOR WOMEN AND GENDER EDUCATION IN THE WORKPLACE
congress_website_abstract: The bill requires every employer to give their employees and workers free annual seminars on women's rights, welfare, development and gender equality, RA 7877 (Anti-Sexual Harassment Act of 1995) and RA 9208 (Anti-Trafficking in Persons Act of 2003) in the workplace. The seminars shall use participatory and non-education modules prescribed by the National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women and other women's organizations.

name: HBN-01022
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING ALL RETIRED AND ACTIVE MILITARY AND POLICE PERSONNEL, INCLUDING OFFICERS, FROM BEING APPOINTED IN A CONCURRENT OR ANY OTHER CAPACITY TO THE POSITIONS OF SECRETARY, UNDERSECRETARY, HEAD OR MEMBER OF GOVERNING BODIES OF GOVERNMENT-OWNED OR -CONTROLLED CORPORATIONS AND AGENCIES AND OTHER EQUIVALENT POSITIONS
congress_website_abstract: To uphold civilian supremacy as mandated in the Constitution, the bill seeks to disqualify all military and police personnel, including officers, from appointment to the positions of secretary, undersecretary and heads or members of government-owned or controlled corporations. The prohibition shall apply even after the military or police officer has been separated from service including retirement.

name: HBN-01023
congress_website_title: AN ACT INTEGRATING EDUCATION ON WOMEN, GIRLS AND GENDER RIGHTS, EQUALITY AND WELFARE IN ALL LEVELS OF PUBLIC AND PRIVATE SCHOOLS
congress_website_abstract: \"Women, Girls and Gender Education Act of 2003.\" The bill requires all public and private educational institutions, including computer, technical and vocational schools, to integrate in their curriculum women's rights, welfare, development and gender equality, RA 7877 (Anti-Sexual Harassment Act of 1995) and RA 9208 (Anti-Trafficking in Persons Act of 2003).

name: HBN-01024
congress_website_title: AN ACT STRENGTHENING THE RIGHT TO SECURITY OF TENURE, PROVIDING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATIONS THEREOF, AMENDING PERTINENT PORTIONS OF THE LABOR CODE, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Strengthening the Right to Security of Tenure Act of 2001.\" To address the current practice of contractualization, the bill stipulates that an employee is deemed regular from the time he is engaged to perform activities by the employer whether or not such activities are necessary or desirable in the usual business or trade of the employer except where the employment is fixed for a specific project the completion of which has been determined at the time of hiring. The bill also provides for the repeal of Article 81 and declares probationary and casual employment as illegal.

name: HBN-01025
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ARTICLE 26 OF EXECUTIVE ACT NO. 209, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE FAMILY CODE, AS AMENDED BY EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 227
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks to grant the right to remarry to a Filipino citizen whose spouse was a Filipino citizen at the time of marriage and who, thereafter, acquired a foreign citizenship at the time a divorce was obtained. The Filipino citizen who remarries under these circumstances shall not be prosecuted for bigamy.

name: HBN-01026
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING ARTICLE 177 OF EXECUTIVE ACT NO. 209, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE FAMILY CODE, AS AMENDED BY EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 227
congress_website_abstract: Article 177 of the Family Code provides that only children conceived and born out of wedlock of parents who, at the time of the conception of the former, were not disqualified by any impediment to marry each other may be legitimated. The bill seeks to amend the provision, as follows \"CHILDREN CONCEIVED AND BORN OUTSIDE OF WEDLOCK OF PARENTS MAY BE LEGITIMIZED.\"

name: HBN-01027
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THE INCLUSION IN THE HISTORY BOOKS OF ELEMENTARY, SECONDARY, AND COLLEGIATE CURRICULA THE LIVES AND HEROISM OF FILIPINO COMFORT WOMEN DURING THE JAPANESE OCCUPATION AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: The measure proposes to inform and educate our children on the lives, sacrifices and heroism of Filipino comfort women during the Japanese occupation. Their inclusion in history books would give due recognition to the courageous struggles of these women.

name: HBN-01028
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A SECURED AND SEPARATE PRISON CELL FOR FEMALE PRISONERS IN EVERY DISTRICT, CITY AND MUNICIPAL JAIL, AMENDING FOR THE PURPOSE SECTION 63 OF REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6975
congress_website_abstract: There have been a number of reports of rape and sexual harassment of female prisoners by male prisoners. The bill provides for a secured, clean, safe, sanitary and separate jail for female prisoners in every district, city and municipal.

name: HBN-01029
congress_website_title: AN ACT REQUIRING WOMEN'S AND GIRLS' HUMAN RIGHTS AWARENESS PROGRAMS IN MEDIA, PROVIDING INCENTIVES THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"Women's and Girl's Human Rights Awareness Program in Media Act of 2003.\" The bill requires that a minimum of 15% of the daily air time of radio and television broadcasting networks be allotted for women's and girls' human rights programs and shows. The programs shall include, among others, the lives of Filipino heroines, RA 7877 (Anti-Sexual Harassment Act of 1995) and RA 9208 (Anti-Trafficking in Persons Act of 2003 and the UN Declaration on the Rights of Women.

name: HBN-01030
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROHIBITING DRAG RACING AND PRESCRIBING PENALTIES FOR VIOLATION THEREOF
congress_website_abstract: The bill bans drag racing and provides a penalty of prision correccional in its maximum period if held for betting purposes. It allows drag racing only in duly authorized race tracts where safeguards are taken for the protection of racers and racing aficionados.

name: HBN-01061

name: HBN-01062
congress_website_title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6234 OTHERWISE KNOWN AS 'AN ACT CREATING THE METROPOLITAN WATERWORKS AND SEWERAGE SYSTEM AND DISSOLVING THE NATIONAL WATERWORKS AND SEWERAGE AUTHORITY, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES'

name: HBN-01063
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR A P125.00 DAILY ACROSS-THE-BOARD INCREASE IN THE SALARY RATES OF EMPLOYEES AND WORKERS IN THE PRIVATE SECTOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The P125.00 Daily Across-the-Board Wage Increase Act\". To alleviate the living condition of the working class, the bill mandates all employers in the private sector, whether agriculture or non-agricultural, regardless of capitalization and number of employees, to pay their workers an across-the-board increase of P125.00 daily.

name: HBN-01064
congress_website_title: AN ACT PROVIDING FOR THREE THOUSAND PESOS (P3,000.00) ACROSS-THE-BOARD MONTHLY SALARY INCREASE IN THE WAGE AND SALARY RATES OF EMPLOYEES AND WORKERS IN THE PUBLIC SECTOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: The bill proposes to grant an across-the-board-increase of PhP3,000 to all employees, whether permanent, temporary, emergency, contractual or casual, of the national government, its agencies, and instrumentalities including local government units.

name: HBN-01065
congress_website_title: AN ACT REPEALING REPUBLIC ACT NUMBERED 8479, OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE DOWNSTREAM OIL DEREGULATION ACT OF 1998
congress_website_abstract: The bill seeks the scrapping of the downstream oil industry deregulation law and to facilitate the nationalization of the industry. The oil deregulation law only worsened the problems in the oil industry and further victimized the Filipino consumers.

name: HBN-01066
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL IN BARANGAY SALACOT, MUNICIPALITY OF SAN MIGUEL, PROVINCE OF BULACAN TO BE KNOWN AS THE VEDASTO R. SANTIAGO MEMORIAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

name: HBN-01067
congress_website_title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A COMPREHENSIVE JUVENILE JUSTICE SYSTEM AND DELINQUENCY PREVENTION PROGRAM, CREATING THE OFFICE OF JUVENILE JUSTICE AND DELINQUENCY PREVENTION UNDER THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES
congress_website_abstract: \"The Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention Act of 2004.\" Youth offenders should be treated differently from adult offenders. The bill intends to provide a just and humane system of treating juvenile offenders through a comprehensive approach toward rehabilitating and reintegrating them into society. It increases the age of criminal responsibility from nine to twelve years old.
